process.exit(1);\n66\t  }\n67\t  return new Database(path);\n68\t}\n69\t\n70\t/**\n71\t * Format milliseconds as human-readable duration.\n72\t */\n73\tfunction formatDuration(ms: number): string }\n109\t\n110\t        const targetVersion = options.to ? parseInt(options.to, 10) : undefined;\n111\t        const results = await runMigrations(\n112\t          db,\n113\t          {\n114\t     );\n120\t\n121\t        if (options.dryRun) {\n122\t          console.log(\"\\nDry-run results:\");\n123\t        } else {\n124\t          console.log(\"\\nMigration results:\");\n125\t        }\n126\t\n127\t        let appliedCount = 0;\n128\t        let skippedCount = 0;\n129\t\n130\t        for (const result of results) {\n131\t          if (result.applied) {\n132\t })\n157\t  );\n158\t\n159\t/**\n160\t * Rollback migrations.\n161\t */\n162\tprogram\n163\t  .command(\"down\")\n164\t  .description(\"Rollback migrations\")\n165\t  .option(\"-d, --db <path>\", {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_c8400334b684432faee38ffc","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t/**\n2\t * Data migration system with version tracking, rollback, and safety features.\n3\t *\n4\t * Migrations are numbered files in packages/server/src/migration/migrations/\n5\t * Each migration exports:\n6\t * - up(): Function to apply the migration\n7\t * - * import { runMigrations, rollbackToVersion } from './migration/index.js';\n20\t * await runMigrations(db);\n21\t * await rollbackToVersion(db, 5); // Rollback all migrations after version 5\n22\t * ```\n23\t */\n24\t\n25\timport { copyFile, mkdir, readdir, unlink } from \"node:fs/promises\";\n26\timport { constants } from \"node:os\";\n27\timport type { constants as Constants } from \"node:os\";\n28\timport { dirname, join } from \"node:path\";\n29\timport { fileURLToPath } from \"node:url\";\n30\timport type Database from \"better-sqlite3\";\n31\timport { logger } from \"../observability/logger.js\";\n32\t\n33\tconst __dirname = dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url));\n34\tconst MIGRATIONS_DIR = join(__dirname, \"migrations\");\n35\tconst BACKUP_DIR = join(__dirname, \"../../data/backups\");\n36\t\n37\t/** Migration interface */\n38\texport interface Migration {\n39\t  version: number;\n40\t  name: string;\n41\t  description: string;\n42\t  up: (db: Database.Database) => void;\n43\t  down: (db: Database.Database) => void;\n44\t  validate?: (db: Database.Database) => { valid: boolean; errors?: string[] };\n45\t}\n46\t\n47\t/** Migration record in database */\n48\texport interface MigrationRecord {\n49\t  version: number;\n50\t  name: string;\n51\t  description: string | null;\n52\t  appliedAt: string;\n53\t  executionTimeMs: number;\n54\t}\n55\t\n56\t/** Migration options */\n57\texport interface MigrationOptions {\n58\t  dryRun?: boolean;\n59\t  backup?: boolean;\n60\t  force?: boolean;\n61\t}\n62\t\n63\t/** Migration number;\n71\t  error?: string;\n72\t}\n73\t\n74\t/** Migration lock */\n75\tlet migrationLock = false;\n76\t\n77\t/**\n78\t * Acquire migration lock to prevent concurrent migrations.\n79\t */\n80\tfunction acquireLock(): boolean {\n81\t  if (migrationLock) {\n82\t    return false;\n83\t  }\n84\t  migrationLock = true;\n85\t  return true;\n86\t}\n87\t\n88\t/**\n89\t * Release migration lock.\n90\t */\n91\tfunction releaseLock(): void {\n92\t  migrationLock = false;\n93\t}\n94\t\n95\t/**\n96\t * Initialize migrations table in the database.\n97\t */\n98\tfunction initMigrationsTable(db: Database.Database): void {\n99\t  db.exec(`\n100\t    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS _migrations (\n101\t      version INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,\n102\t      name TEXT NOT NULL,\n103\t      description TEXT,\n104\t      appliedAt TEXT NOT NULL,\n105\t      executionTimeMs INTEGER NOT NULL\n106\t    )\n107\t  `);\n108\t\n109\t  // Add checksum column for data integrity if not exists\n110\t  try {\n111\t    db.exec(`ALTER TABLE _migrations ADD COLUMN checksum TEXT`);\n112\t  } catch {\n113\t    // Column already exists\n114\t  }\n115\t}\n116\t\n117\t/**\n118\t * Get all applied migrations from the database.\n119\t */\n120\tfunction getAppliedMigrations(db: Database.Database): Set<number> {\n121\t  const rows = db.prepare(\"SELECT version FROM _migrations ORDER BY version\").all() as Array<{\n122\t    version: number;\n123\t  }>;\n124\t  return new Set(rows.map((r) => r.version));\n125\t}\n126\t\n127\t/**\n128\t * Create a backup of the database file.\n129\t *\n130\t * @param dbPath - Path to the database file\n131\t * @returns Path to the backup file\n132\t */\n133\texport async function createBackup(dbPath: string): Promise<string> {\n134\t  await mkdir(BACKUP_DIR, { recursive: true });\n135\t\n136\t  const timestamp = new Date().toISOString().replace(/[:.]/g, \"-\");\n137\t  const basename = dbPath.split(\"/\").pop() ?? \"database.db\";\n138\t  const backupPath = join(BACKUP_DIR, `${basename}-${timestamp}.bak`);\n139\t\n140\t  await copyFile(dbPath, backupPath, (constants as typeof Constants).COPYFILE_EXCL);\n141\t\n142\t  logger.info(\"Backup created\", { backupPath });\n143\t\n144\t  return backupPath;\n145\t}\n146\t\n147\t/**\n148\t * List all available backups.\n149\t *\n150\t * @returns Array of backup file information\n151\t */\n152\texport async function listBackups(): Promise<Array<{ path: string; size: number; created: Date }>> {\n153\t  try {\n154\t    const { readdir } = await import(\"node:fs/promises\");\n155\t    const { stat } = await import(\"node:fs/promises\");\n156\t    const files = await readdir(BACKUP_DIR);\n157\t    const backups: Array<{ path: string; size: number; created: Date }> = [];\n158\t\n159\t    for (const file of files) {\n160\t      if (file.endsWith(\".bak\")) {\n161\t        const fullPath = join(BACKUP_DIR, file);\n162\t        const stats = await stat(fullPath);\n163\t        backups.push({\n164\t          path: fullPath,\n165\t          size: stats.size,\n166\t          created: stats.mtime,\n167\t        });\n168\t      }\n169\t    }\n170\t\n171\t    return backups.sort((a, b) => b.created.getTime() - a.created.getTime());\n172\t  } catch {\n173\t    return [];\n174\t  }\n175\t}\n176\t\n177\t/**\n178\t * Restore a database from a backup.\n179\t *\n180\t * @param backupPath - Path to the backup file\n181\t * @param targetPath - Path where to restore the database\n182\t */\n183\texport async function restoreBackup(backupPath: string, targetPath: string): Promise<void> {\n184\t  const { copyFile } = await import(\"node:fs/promises\");\n185\t  await copyFile(backupPath, targetPath);\n186\t\n187\t  logger.info(\"Backup restored\", { backupPath, targetPath });\n188\t}\n189\t\n190\t/**\n191\t * Delete old backups, keeping only the most recent ones.\n192\t *\n193\t * @param keepCount - Number of backups to keep (default: 10)\n194\t */\n195\texport async function cleanupOldBackups(keepCount: number = 10): Promise<number> {\n196\t  const backups = await listBackups();\n197\t\n198\t  if (backups.length <= keepCount) {\n199\t    return 0;\n200\t  }\n201\t\n202\t  const toDelete = backups.slice(keepCount);\n203\t  let deletedCount = 0;\n204\t\n205\t  for (const backup of toDelete) {\n206\t    try {\n207\t      await unlink(backup.path);\n208\t      deletedCount++;\n209\t    } catch (err) {\n210\t      logger.warn(\"Failed to delete backup\", err instanceof Error ? err : undefined, {\n211\t        backupPath: backup.path,\n212\t      });\n213\t    }\n214\t  }\n215\t\n216\t  if (deletedCount > 0) {\n217\t    logger.info(\"Backups cleaned\", {\n218\t      deletedCount,\n219\t      remainingCount: backups.length - deletedCount,\n220\t    });\n221\t  }\n222\t\n223\t  return deletedCount;\n224\t}\n225\t\n226\t/**\n227\t * Load all migration files from the migrations directory.\n228\t *\n229\t * @param customDir - Optional custom directory to load migrations from\n230\t */\n231\tasync function loadMigrations(customDir?: string): Promise<Migration[]> {\n232\t  const migrations: Migration[] = [];\n233\t  const dir = customDir ?? MIGRATIONS_DIR;\n234\t\n235\t  try {\n236\t    const files = await readdir(dir);\n237\t    const migrationFiles = files.filter((f) => f.endsWith(\".ts\") && !f.endsWith(\".test.ts\"));\n238\t\n239\t    for (const file of migrationFiles) {\n240\t      // Extract version number from filename (e.g., 001-add-users.ts)\n241\t      const match = file.match(/^(\\d+)-(.+)\\.ts$/);\n242\t      if (!match) continue;\n243\t\n244\t      const [, versionStr, name] = match as [string, string, string];\n245\t      const version = parseInt(versionStr, 10);\n246\t\n247\t      try {\n248\t        const module = await import(join(dir, file));\n249\t        if (module.up && module.down && module.description) {\n250\t          migrations.push({\n251\t            version,\n252\t            name,\n253\t            description: module.description,\n254\t            up: module.up,\n255\t            down: module.down,\n256\t            validate: module.validate,\n257\t          });\n258\t        }\n259\t      } catch (err) {\n260\t        logger.error(\"Failed to load migration\", err instanceof Error ? err : undefined, {\n261\t          file,\n262\t        });\n263\t      }\n264\t    }\n265\t  } catch {\n266\t    // Migrations directory doesn't exist yet\n267\t    logger.debug(\"No migrations directory found\");\n268\t  }\n269\t\n270\t  return migrations.sort((a, b) => a.version - b.version);\n271\t}\n272\t\n273\t/**\n274\t * Run all pending migrations.\n275\t *\n276\t * @param db - Better-sqlite3 database instance\n277\t * @param options - Migration options (dryRun, backup, force)\n278\t * @param targetVersion - Optional target version to migrate to\n279\t * @returns Array of migration results\n280\t */\n281\texport async function runMigrations(\n282\t  db: Database.Database,\n283\t  options: MigrationOptions = {},\n284\t  targetVersion?: number\n285\t): Promise<MigrationResult[]> {\n286\t  // Acquire lock\n287\t  if (!acquireLock()) {\n288\t    throw new Error(\"Migration already in progress\");\n289\t  }\n290\t\n291\t  try {\n292\t    const { dryRun = false, force = false } = options;\n293\t\n294\t    initMigrationsTable(db);\n295\t    const applied = getAppliedMigrations(db);\n296\t    const migrations = await loadMigrations();\n297\t    const results: MigrationResult[] = [];\n298\t\n299\t    for (const migration of migrations) {\n300\t      // Skip if already applied\n301\t      if (applied.has(migration.version)) {\n302\t        results.push({\n303\t          version: migration.version,\n304\t          name: migration.name,\n305\t          description: migration.description,\n306\t          applied: false,\n307\t          skipped: true,\n308\t          executionTimeMs: 0,\n309\t        });\n310\t        continue;\n311\t      }\n312\t\n313\t      // Stop if target version is specified and we've reached it\n314\t      if (targetVersion !== undefined && migration.version > targetVersion) {\n315\t        break;\n316\t      }\n317\t\n318\t      const startTime = Date.now();\n319\t      let validationErrors: string[] | undefined;\n320\t\n321\t      // Run pre-migration validation if available\n322\t      if (migration.validate && !dryRun) {\n323\t        const validation = migration.validate(db);\n324\t        if (!validation.valid) {\n325\t          validationErrors = validation.errors;\n326\t          if (!force) {\n327\t            throw new Error(\n328\t              `Migration ${migration.version} validation failed: ${validationErrors?.join(\", \")}`\n329\t            );\n330\t          }\n331\t        }\n332\t      }\n333\t\n334\t      try {\n335\t        if (dryRun) {\n336\t          // In dry-run mode, just log what would be done\n337\t          logger.info(\"Migration dry run\", {\n338\t            version: migration.version,\n339\t            name: migration.name,\n340\t            description: migration.description,\n341\t            validationErrors,\n342\t          });\n343\t\n344\t          results.push({\n345\t            version: migration.version,\n346\t            name: migration.name,\n347\t            description: migration.description,\n348\t            applied: false,\n349\t            skipped: false,\n350\t            executionTimeMs: 0,\n351\t          });\n352\t        } else {\n353\t          // Run migration within a transaction\n354\t          db.transaction(() => {\n355\t            migration.up(db);\n356\t\n357\t            // Record migration\n358\t            db.prepare(\n359\t              \"INSERT INTO _migrations (version, name, description, appliedAt, executionTimeMs) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\"\n360\t            ).run(\n361\t              migration.version,\n362\t              migration.name,\n363\t              migration.description,\n364\t              new Date().toISOString(),\n365\t              Date.now() - startTime\n366\t            );\n367\t          })();\n368\t\n369\t          results.push({\n370\t            version: migration.version,\n371\t            name: migration.name,\n372\t            description: migration.description,\n373\t            applied: true,\n374\t            skipped: false,\n375\t            executionTimeMs: Date.now() - startTime,\n376\t          });\n377\t\n378\t          logger.info(\"Migration applied\", {\n379\t            version: migration.version,\n380\t            name: migration.name,\n381\t            description: migration.description,\n382\t            executionTimeMs: Date.now() - startTime,\n383\t          });\n384\t        }\n385\t      } catch (err) {\n386\t        const error = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);\n387\t        logger.error(\"Migration failed\", err instanceof Error ? err : undefined, {\n388\t          version: migration.version,\n389\t          name: migration.name,\n390\t          error,\n391\t        });\n392\t\n393\t        results.push({\n394\t          version: migration.version,\n395\t          name: migration.name,\n396\t          description: migration.description,\n397\t          applied: false,\n398\t          skipped: false,\n399\t          executionTimeMs: Date.now() - startTime,\n400\t          error,\n401\t        });\n402\t\n403\t        if (!dryRun) {\n404\t          throw err;\n405\t        }\n406\t      }\n407\t    }\n408\t\n409\t    return results;\n410\t  } finally {\n411\t    releaseLock();\n412\t  }\n413\t}\n414\t\n415\t/**\n416\t * Rollback a specific migration.\n417\t *\n418\t * @param db - Better-sqlite3 database instance\n419\t * @param version - Migration version to rollback\n420\t * @param options - Migration options (dryRun, backup)\n421\t * @returns True if rolled back, false if not found\n422\t */\n423\texport async function rollbackMigration(\n424\t  db: Database.Database,\n425\t  version: number,\n426\t  options: MigrationOptions = {}\n427\t): Promise<boolean> {\n428\t  // Acquire lock\n429\t  if (!acquireLock()) {\n430\t    throw new Error(\"Migration already in progress\");\n431\t  }\n432\t\n433\t  try {\n434\t    const { dryRun = false } = options;\n435\t\n436\t    initMigrationsTable(db);\n437\t    const applied = getAppliedMigrations(db);\n438\t\n439\t    if (!applied.has(version)) {\n440\t      logger.warn(\"Rollback: migration not applied\", { version });\n441\t      return false;\n442\t    }\n443\t\n444\t    const migrations = await loadMigrations();\n445\t    const migration = migrations.find((m) => m.version === version);\n446\t\n447\t    if (!migration) {\n448\t      logger.error(\"Rollback: migration file not found\", undefined, { version });\n449\t      return false;\n450\t    }\n451\t\n452\t    const startTime = Date.now();\n453\t\n454\t    if (dryRun) {\n455\t      logger.info(\"Rollback dry run\", {\n456\t        version: migration.version,\n457\t        name: migration.name,\n458\t      });\n459\t      return true;\n460\t    }\n461\t\n462\t    try {\n463\t      db.transaction(() => {\n464\t        // Run rollback\n465\t        migration.down(db);\n466\t\n467\t        // Remove from migrations table\n468\t        db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM _migrations WHERE version = ?\").run(version);\n469\t      })();\n470\t\n471\t      logger.info(\"Migration rolled back\", {\n472\t        version: migration.version,\n473\t        name: migration.name,\n474\t        executionTimeMs: Date.now() - startTime,\n475\t      });\n476\t\n477\t      return true;\n478\t    } catch (err) {\n479\t      logger.error(\"Rollback failed\", err instanceof Error ? err : undefined, {\n480\t        version: migration.version,\n481\t        name: migration.name,\n482\t      });\n483\t      throw err;\n484\t    }\n485\t  } finally {\n486\t    releaseLock();\n487\t  }\n488\t}\n489\t\n490\t/**\n491\t * Rollback all migrations after a specific version.\n492\t *\n493\t * @param db - Better-sqlite3 database instance\n494\t * @param targetVersion - Target version to rollback to (all migrations after this will be rolled back)\n495\t * @param options - Migration options (dryRun, backup)\n496\t * @returns Array of rolled back migration versions\n497\t */\n498\texport async function rollbackToVersion(\n499\t  db: Database.Database,\n500\t  targetVersion: number,\n501\t  options: MigrationOptions = {}\n502\t): Promise<number[]> {\n503\t  // Acquire lock\n504\t  if (!acquireLock()) {\n505\t    throw new Error(\"Migration already in progress\");\n506\t  }\n507\t\n508\t  try {\n509\t    const { dryRun = false } = options;\n510\t\n511\t    initMigrationsTable(db);\n512\t    const applied = db\n513\t      .prepare(\"SELECT version FROM _migrations ORDER BY version DESC\")\n514\t      .all() as Array<{   if (toRollback.length === 0) {\n520\t      logger.info(\"Rollback to version: no migrations to rollback\", {\n521\t        targetVersion,\n522\t      });\n523\t      return [];\n524\t    }\n525\t\n526\t    if (dryRun) {\n527\t      logger.info(\"Rollback to version dry run\", {\n528\t        targetVersion,\n529\t        migrationsToRollback: toRollback.map((m) => m.version),\n530\t      });\n531\t      return toRollback.map((m) => m.version);\n532\t    }\n533\t\n534\t    const rolledBack: number[] = [];\n535\t\n536\t    // Rollback migrations in reverse order (highest version first)\n537\t    for (const { version } of toRollback.sort((a, b) => b.version - a.version)) {\n538\t      const success = await rollbackMigration(db, version, { dryRun: false });\n539\t      if (success) {\n540\t        rolledBack.push(version);\n541\t      } else {\n542\t        throw new Error(`Failed to rollback migration ${version}`);\n543\t      }\n544\t    }\n545\t\n546\t    logger.info(\"Rollback to version complete\", {\n547\t      targetVersion,\n548\t      rolledBackVersions: rolledBack,\n549\t    });\n550\t\n551\t    return rolledBack;\n552\t  } finally {\n553\t    releaseLock();\n554\t  }\n555\t}\n556\t\n557\t/**\n558\t * Get migration status.\n559\t *\n560\t * @param db - Better-sqlite3 database instance\n561\t * @returns Applied and pending migrations\n562\t */\n563\texport async function getMigrationStatus(db: Database.Database): Promise<{\n564\t  applied: MigrationRecord[];\n565\t  pending: Migration[];\n566\t  currentVersion: number;\n567\t  latestVersion: number;\n568\t}> {\n569\t  initMigrationsTable(db);\n570\t  const applied = db\n571\t    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM _migrations ORDER BY version\")\n572\t    .all() as MigrationRecord[];\n573\t\n574\t  const migrations = await loadMigrations();\n575\t  const appliedVersions = new Set(applied.map((a) => a.version));\n576\t  const pending = migrations.filter((m) => Math.max(...applied.map((a) => a.version)) : 0;\n579\t  const latestVersion = migrations.length > 0 ? \"better-sqlite3\";\n10\timport {\n11\t  buildColumnList,\n12\t  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars\n13\t  buildPlaceholderList,\n14\t  validateColumnName,\n15\t inserted: number;\n34\t  skipped: number;\n35\t  errors: string[];\n36\t}\n37\t\n38\t/**\n39\t * Seed data into a table with conflict handling.\n40\t *\n41\t * @param db - Database instance\n42\t * @param table - Table name\n43\t * @param data - Array of data objects to insert\n44\t * @param options - Seeding options\n45\t * @returns Seed result with counts\n46\t */\n47\texport function seedData<T extends Record<string, unknown>>(\n48\t  db: Database.Database,\n49\t  table: string,\n50\t  data: T[],\n51\t  options: {\n52\t    skipDuplicates?: boolean;\n53\t    uniqueKeys?: string[];\n54\t    onConflict?: \"ignore\" | \"replace\" | \"update\";\n55\t  } = {}\n56\t): SeedResult {\n57\t  const { skipDuplicates = true, onConflict = \"ignore\" } = options;\n58\t  const errors: string[] = [];\n59\t  let inserted = 0;\n60\t  let skipped = 0;\n61\t\n62\t  if (data.length === 0) {\n63\t    return { table, inserted: 0, skipped: 0, errors: [] };\n64\t  }\n65\t\n66\t  // Validate table name to prevent SQL injection\n67\t  const validatedTable = validateTableName(table);\n68\t\n69\t  // Build column names from first data object\n70\t  const columns = Object.keys(data[0]);\n71\t\n72\t  // Validate all column names to prevent SQL injection\n73\t  const columnValidation = validateSqlIdentifiers(columns);\n74\t  if (!columnValidation.valid) {\n75\t    return {\n76\t      table,\n77\t      inserted: 0,\n78\t      skipped: 0,\n79\t      errors: [`Invalid column names: ${columnValidation.invalid.join(\", \")}`],\n80\t    };\n81\t  }\n82\t\n83\t  const placeholders = columns.map(() => \"?\").join(\", \");\n84\t  const columnList = buildColumnList(columns);\n85\t\n86\t  // Prepare appropriate statement based on conflict handling\n87\t  let sql: string;\n88\t  switch (onConflict) {\n89\t    case \"replace\":\n90\t      sql = `INSERT OR REPLACE INTO ${validatedTable} (${columnList}) VALUES (${placeholders})`;\n91\t      break;\n92\t    case \"update\":\n93\t      // For UPDATE, we need to use INSERT OR REPLACE with a different approach\n94\t      // SQLite doesn't have ON CONFLICT UPDATE in all versions\n95\t      sql = `INSERT OR REPLACE INTO ${validatedTable} (${columnList}) VALUES (${placeholders})`;\n96\t      break;\n97\t    case \"ignore\":\n98\t    default:\n99\t      sql = `INSERT OR IGNORE INTO ${validatedTable} (${columnList}) VALUES (${placeholders})`;\n100\t      break;\n101\t  }\n102\t\n103\t  const stmt = db.prepare(sql);\n104\t\n105\t  for (const row of data) {\n106\t    try {\n107\t      const values = columns.map((col) => row[col as keyof T] as ParameterType);\n108\t      const result = stmt.run(...values);\n109\t\n110\t      if (result.changes > 0) {\n111\t        inserted++;\n112\t      } else if (skipDuplicates) {\n113\t        skipped++;\n114\t      }\n115\t    } catch (err) {\n116\t      const error = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);\n117\t      errors.push(`Row validateTableName(table);\n159\t    const result = db.prepare(`SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM ${validatedTable}`).get() as\n160\t      | { count: number }\n161\t      | undefined;\n162\t    return !result || result.count < minCount;\n163\t  } catch {\n164\t    return true;\n165\t  }\n166\t}\n167\t\n168\t/**\n169\t * Upsert single row (insert or update if exists).\n170\t *\n171\t * @param db - was inserted or updated\n176\t */\n177\texport function upsertRow(\n178\t  db: Database.Database,\n179\t  table: string,\n180\t  data: Record<string, unknown>,\n181\t  uniqueColumns: string[]\n182\t): }\n192\t\n193\t  // Validate unique column names\n194\t  const uniqueColumnValidation = validateSqlIdentifiers(uniqueColumns);\n195\t  if (!uniqueColumnValidation.valid) {\n196\t    throw new Error(`Invalid unique column names: ${uniqueColumnValidation.invalid.join(\", \")}`);\n197\t  }\n198\t\n199\t  const values = columns.map((col) => data[col]);\n200\t  const placeholders = columns.map(() => \"?\").join(\", \");\n201\t  const columnList = buildColumnList(columns);\n202\t\n203\t  // Build WHERE clause for unique columns\n204\t  const whereClause = uniqueColumns.map((col) => `${validateColumnName(col)} = ?`).join(\" AND \");\n205\t  const whereValues = uniqueColumns.map((col) => data[col]);\n206\t\n207\t  // Check if row exists\n208\t  const existsStmt = updateClause = columns\n214\t      .filter((col) => !uniqueColumns.includes(col))\n215\t      .map((col) => `${validateColumnName(col)} = ?`)\n216\t      .join(\", \");\n217\t\n218\t    if (updateClause) {\n219\t      const updateValues = columns\n220\t        .filter((col) => !uniqueColumns.includes(col))\n221\t        .map((col) => data[col]);\n222\t\n223\t      const updateStmt = db.prepare(\n224\t        `UPDATE ${validatedTable} SET ${updateClause} WHERE ${whereClause}`\n225\t      );\n226\t      updateStmt.run(...updateValues, ...whereValues);\n227\t    }\n228\t    return true;\n229\t  } else {\n230\t    // Insert new row\n231\t    const insertStmt = db.prepare(\n232\t      `INSERT INTO ${validatedTable} (${columnList}) VALUES (${placeholders})`\n233\t    );\n234\t    const result = insertStmt.run(...values);\n235\t    return result.changes > 0;\n236\t  }\n237\t}\n238\t\n239\t/**\n240\t * Seed configuration data (key-value pairs).\n241\t *\n242\t * Creates a simple key-value configuration table if it doesn't exist\n243\t * and seeds it with initial configuration.\n244\t *\n245\t * @param db - Database instance\n246\t * @param tableName - Name of the configuration table\n247\t * @param config - Configuration object to seed\n248\t * @param options - Table creation options\n249\t * @returns Seed result\n250\t */\n251\texport function valueColumn?: string;\n258\t    createIfNotExists?: boolean;\n259\t  } = {}\n260\t): SeedResult {\n261\t  const { keyColumn = \"name\", valueColumn = \"value\", createIfNotExists = true } = });\n286\t}\n287\t\n288\t/**\n289\t * Bulk seed from JSON file content.\n290\t *\n291\t * @param db - Database instance\n292\t * @param table - Table name\n293\t * @param jsonData - JSON string or parsed object\n294\t * @returns Seed result\n295\t */\n296\texport function seedFromJSON(\n297\t  db: Database.Database,\n298\t  table: string,\n299\t  jsonData: string | Record<string, unknown> | Skip automatic backup before migrations\n104\t  --help           Show this help message\n105\t  --validate       Include validation in new migration\n106\t\n107\tExamples:\n108\t  node */\n121\tasync function initDatabase(dbPath) {\n122\t  const Database = await import(\"better-sqlite3\");\n123\t  const db = new Database.default(dbPath);\n124\t  // Enable WAL mode for better concurrency\n125\t  db.pragma(\"journal_mode = WAL\");\n126\t  return db;\n127\t}\n128\t\n129\t/**\n130\t * Command: Run all pending migrations.\n131\t */\n132\tasync function cmdMigrate(db, options) {\n133\t  const { runMigrations } = await loadMigrationModule();\n134\t\n135\t  console.log(JSON.stringify({ event: \"migrate_start\", dryRun: options.dryRun }));\n136\t\n137\t  const results = await JSON.stringify({\n237\t      event: \"migrate_down\",\n238\t      version: targetVersion,\n239\t      dryRun: options.dryRun,\n240\t    })\n241\t  );\n242\t\n243\t  const success = await }\n262\t}\n263\t\n264\t/**\n265\t * Command: Rollback to a specific version.\n266\t */\n267\tasync function cmdRollback(db, targetVersion, options) {\n268\t  const { rollbackToVersion } = await JSON.stringify({\n312\t      event: \"create_migration\",\n313\t      name: migrationName,\n314\t      description,\n315\t    })\n316\t  );\n317\t\n318\t  const filepath = await createMigration(migrationName, description, options.validate);\n319\t\n320\t  console.log(\n321\t    JSON.stringify({\n322\t      event: \"migration_created\",\n323\t      filepath,\n324\t    })\n325\t  );\n326\t}\n327\t\n328\t/**\n329\t * Command: Validate database integrity.\n330\t */\n331\tasync function cmdValidate(db) {\n332\t  const { validateDatabase } = await loadMigrationModule();\n333\t\n334\t  console.log(JSON.stringify({ event: \"validate_start\" }));\n335\t\n336\t  const result = validateDatabase(db);\n337\t\n338\t  console.log(\n339\t    JSON.stringify({\n340\t      event: \"validate_complete\",\n341\t      valid: result.valid,\n342\t      errorCount: result.errors.length,\n343\t      warningCount: result.warnings.length,\n344\t      errors: result.errors,\n345\t      warnings: result.warnings,\n346\t    })\n347\t  );\n348\t\n349\t  if (!result.valid) {\n350\t    process.exit(1);\n351\t  }\n352\t}\n353\t\n354\t/**\n355\t * */\n380\tasync function cmdRestore(backupFile, dbPath) {\n381\t  const { restoreBackup } = await loadMigrationModule();\n382\t\n383\t  if (!backupFile) {\n384\t    console.error(\"Error: Backup file );\n406\t}\n407\t\n408\t/**\n409\t * Command: List available backups.\n410\t */\n411\tasync function cmdBackups() {\n412\t  const { listBackups } = await loadMigrationModule();\n413\t\n414\t  const old backups.\n431\t */\n432\tasync function cmdCleanup(keepCount) {\n433\t  const { cleanupOldBackups } = await loadMigrationModule();\n434\t  const keep = keepCount ? parseInt(keepCount, 10) : 10;\n435\t\n436\t  console.log(\n437\t    JSON.stringify({\n438\t      event: \"cleanup_start\",\n439\t      keepCount: keep,\n440\t    })\n441\t  );\n442\t\n443\t  const deletedCount = await cleanupOldBackups(keep);\n444\t\n445\t  console.log(\n446\t    JSON.stringify({\n447\t      event: \"cleanup_complete\",\n448\t      deletedCount,\n449\t      remainingCount: Math.max(0, keep - deletedCount),\n450\t    })\n451\t  );\n452\t}\n453\t\n454\t/**\n455\t * Main entry point.\n456\t */\n457\tasync function main() {\n458\t  const args = process.argv.slice(2);\n459\t  const { values, positionals } = parseArguments(args);\n460\t\n461\t  if (values.help) {\n462\t    showHelp();\n463\t    return;\n464\t  }\n465\t\n466\t  const [command, ...commandArgs] = positionals;\n467\t\n468\t  try {\n469\t    let db;\n470\t\n471\t    // Commands that don't require database connection\n472\t    if (command === \"create\") {\n473\t      await cmdCreate(commandArgs[0], values);\n474\t      return;\n475\t    }\n476\t\n477\t    if (command === \"backups\") {\n478\t      await cmdBackups();\n479\t      return;\n480\t    }\n481\t\n482\t    if (command === \"restore\") {\n483\t      await cmdRestore(commandArgs[0], values.db);\n484\t      return;\n485\t    }\n486\t\n487\t    if (command === \"cleanup\") {\n488\t      await cmdCleanup(commandArgs[0]);\n489\t      return;\n490\t    }\n491\t\n492\t    // Commands that require database connection\n493\t    try {\n494\t      db = await initDatabase(values.db);\n495\t    } catch (err) {\n496\t      console.error(\n497\t        JSON.stringify({\n498\t          event: \"database_init_error\",\n499\t          error: err.message,\n500\t          dbPath: values.db,\n501\t        })\n502\t      );\n503\t      process.exit(1);\n504\t    }\n505\t\n506\t    try {\n507\t      switch (command) {\n508\t        case \"status\":\n509\t          await cmdStatus(db);\n510\t          break;\n511\t\n512\t        case \"up\":\n513\t          await cmdUp(db, commandArgs[0], values);\n514\t          break;\n515\t\n516\t        case \"down\":\n517\t          if (!commandArgs[0]) {\n518\t            console.error(\"Error: Version number is required for 'down' command\");\n519\t            process.exit(1);\n520\t          }\n521\t          await cmdDown(db, commandArgs[0], values);\n522\t          break;\n523\t\n524\t        case \"rollback\":\n525\t          if (!commandArgs[0]) {\n526\t            console.error(\"Error: Target version is required for 'rollback' command\");\n527\t            process.exit(1);\n528\t          }\n529\t          await cmdRollback(db, commandArgs[0], values);\n530\t          break;\n531\t\n532\t        case \"validate\":\n533\t          await cmdValidate(db);\n534\t          break;\n535\t\n536\t        case \"backup\":\n537\t          await cmdBackup(db, values.db);\n538\t          break;\n539\t\n540\t        default:\n541\t          // No command specified - run pending migrations\n542\t          if (!command || command === \"\") {\n543\t            // Create backup before migrations unless disabled\n544\t            if (!values[\"no-backup\"] && !values.dryRun) {\n545\t              try {\n546\t                await cmdBackup(db, values.db);\n547\t              } catch (err) {\n548\t                console.warn(\"Warning: Backup failed, proceeding with migrations:\", err.message);\n549\t              }\n550\t            }\n551\t            await cmdMigrate(db, values);\n552\t          } else {\n553\t            console.error(`Unknown command: ${command}`);\n554\t            console.error('Run \"node scripts/migrate.mjs --help\" for usage');\n555\t            process.exit(1);\n556\t          }\n557\t      }\n558\t    } finally {\n559\t      if (db) {\n560\t        db.close();\n561\t      }\n562\t    }\n563\t  } catch (err) {\n564\t    console.error(\n565\t      JSON.stringify({\n566\t        event: \"error\",\n567\t        command,\n568\t        error: err.message,\n569\t        stack: err.stack,\n570\t      })\n571\t    );\n572\t    process.exit(1);\n573\t  }\n574\t}\n575\t\n576\tmain();\n577\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"call_d04d4ce3b8384cea88617e01","session_id":"b6d66451-43d3-43fc-8fc1-2f6e30be96e3","uuid":"2709db10-a6dc-4388-9413-5506f2ba78ed","timestamp":"2026-07-03T20:11:19.933Z","subagent_type":"Explore","task_description":"Explore Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: \\\"chart\\\", \\\"graph\\\", \\\"plot\\\", \\\"data viz\\\", \\\"visualization\\\", \\\"dashboard\\\", \\\"analytics\\\", \\\"visualize data\\\", \\\"categorical colors\\\", \\\"sequential / diverging palette\\\", \\\"stat tile\\\", \\\"sparkline\\\", \\\"heatmap\\\", \\\"legend\\\", \\\"axis\\\", \\\"tooltip\\\", \\\"chart colors\\\", \\\"color by series\\\".\\n- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors (\\\"from now on when X\\\", \\\"each time X\\\", \\\"whenever X\\\", \\\"before/after X\\\") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions (\\\"allow X\\\", \\\"add permission\\\", \\\"move permission to\\\"), env vars (\\\"set X=Y\\\"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: \\\"allow npm commands\\\", \\\"add bq permission to global settings\\\", \\\"move permission to user settings\\\", \\\"set DEBUG=true\\\", \\\"when claude stops show X\\\". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.\\n- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: \\\"rebind ctrl+s\\\", \\\"add a chord shortcut\\\", \\\"change the submit key\\\", \\\"customize keybindings\\\".\\n- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.\\n- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.\\n- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.\\n- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.\\n- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. \\\"check the deploy every 5 minutes\\\", \\\"keep running /babysit-prs\\\"). migration.description,\\n364\\t              new Date().toISOString(),\\n365\\t              Date.now() - startTime\\n366\\t            );\\n367\\t          })();\\n368\\t\\n369\\t }\\n413\\t}\\n414\\t\\n415\\t/**\\n416\\t * Rollback a specific migration.\\n417\\t *\\n418\\t * @param db - Better-sqlite3 database instance\\n419\\t * @param version - Migration version to }\\n451\\t\\n452\\t    const startTime = Date.now();\\n453\\t\\n454\\t    if (dryRun) {\\n455\\t      logger.info(\\\"Rollback dry run\\\", {\\n456\\t        version: migration.version,\\n457\\t migration.down(db);\\n466\\t\\n467\\t        // Remove from migrations table\\n468\\t        db.prepare(\\\"DELETE FROM _migrations WHERE version = ?\\\").run(version);\\n469\\t     version: migration.version,\\n481\\t        name: migration.name,\\n482\\t      });\\n483\\t      throw err;\\n484\\t    }\\n485\\t  } finally {\\n486\\t    releaseLock();\\n487\\t  }\\n488\\t}\\n489\\t\\n490\\t/**\\n491\\t * Rollback all migrations after a specific version.\\n492\\t *\\n493\\t * @param db - Better-sqlite3 database instance\\n494\\t * @param targetVersion - Target version to rollback to (all migrations after this will be rolled back)\\n495\\t * @param options - Migration options (dryRun, backup)\\n496\\t * @returns Array of rolled back migration versions\\n497\\t */\\n498\\texport async function rollbackToVersion(\\n499\\t  db: Database.Database,\\n500\\t  targetVersion: number,\\n501\\t  options: MigrationOptions = {}\\n502\\t): Promise<number[]> {\\n503\\t  // Acquire lock\\n504\\t  if (!acquireLock()) {\\n505\\t    throw new Error(\\\"Migration already in progress\\\");\\n506\\t  }\\n507\\t\\n508\\t  try {\\n509\\t    const rolledBack: number[] = [];\\n535\\t\\n536\\t    // Rollback migrations in reverse order (highest version first)\\n537\\t    for (const { version } of toRollback.sort((a, b) => b.version - a.version)) (${placeholders})`;\\n96\\t      break;\\n97\\t    case \\\"ignore\\\":\\n98\\t    default:\\n99\\t      sql = `INSERT OR IGNORE INTO ${validatedTable} (${columnList}) VALUES \\\");\\n315\\t}\\n316\\t\\n317\\t/**\\n318\\t * Build a placeholder list for parameterized queries.\\n319\\t * Returns a string like \\\"(?, ?, ?)\\\" for the given count.\\n320\\t *\\n321\\t *       case \\\"status\\\":\\n509\\t          await cmdStatus(db);\\n510\\t          break;\\n511\\t\\n512\\t        case \\\"up\\\":\\n513\\t          await cmdUp(db, commandArgs[0], values);\\n514\\t => Promise<void>) =>\n  (...args: T): void => {\n    void fn(...args);\n  };\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Configuration\n// ============================================================================\n\nconst DEFAULT_DB_PATH = \"./packages/server/data/database.db\";\nconst _MIGRATIONS_DIR = \"./packages/server/src/migration/migrations\";\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Utility Functions\n// ============================================================================\n\n/**\n * Get database path from arguments or default.\n */\nfunction getDbPath(path?: string): string {\n  if (path) return resolve(path);\n  return resolve(DEFAULT_DB_PATH);\n}\n\n/**\n * Open database connection.\n */\nfunction openDatabase(path: string): Database.Database {\n  if (!existsSync(path)) {\n    console.error(`Database not found: ${path}`);\n    console.error(\"Run with --init to create a new database\");\n    process.exit(1);\n  }\n  return new Database(path);\n}\n\n/**\n * Format milliseconds as human-readable duration.\n */\nfunction formatDuration(ms: number): string {\n  if (ms < 1000) return `${ms}ms`;\n  if (ms < 60000) return `${(ms / 1000).toFixed(1)}s`;\n  return `${(ms / 60000).toFixed(1)}m`;\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Commands\n// ============================================================================\n\nprogram.name(\"mta-migrate\").description(\"MTA My Way database migration CLI\").version(\"1.0.0\");\n\n/**\n * Apply pending migrations.\n */\nprogram\n  .command(\"up\")\n  .description(\"Apply pending migrations\")\n  .option(\"-d, --db <path>\", \"Database path\", DEFAULT_DB_PATH)\n  .option(\"--dry-run\", \"Preview migrations without executing\")\n  .option(\"--backup\", \"Create backup before migration\")\n  .option(\"--force\", \"Skip validation errors\")\n  .option(\"--to <version>\", \"Migrate to specific version\")\n  .action(\n    asyncAction(async (options) => {\n      const dbPath = getDbPath(options.db);\n      const db = openDatabase(dbPath);\n\n      try {\n        console.log(`Connecting to database: ${dbPath}`);\n\n        if (options.backup) {\n          console.log(\"Creating backup...\");\n          const backupPath = await createBackup(dbPath);\n          console.log(`✓ Backup created: ${backupPath}`);\n        }\n\n        const targetVersion = options.to ? parseInt(options.to, 10) : undefined;\n        const results = await runMigrations(\n          db,\n          {\n            dryRun: options.dryRun ?? false,\n            backup: false, // Already handled above\n            force: options.force ?? false,\n          },\n          targetVersion\n        );\n\n        if (options.dryRun) {\n          console.log(\"\\nDry-run results:\");\n        } else {\n          console.log(\"\\nMigration results:\");\n        }\n\n        let appliedCount = 0;\n        let skippedCount = 0;\n\n        for (const result of results) {\n          if (result.applied) {\n            console.log(\n              `  ✓ Applied v${result.version}: ${result.name} (${formatDuration(result.executionTimeMs)})`\n            );\n            appliedCount++;\n          } else if (result.skipped) {\n            console.log(`  - Skipped v${result.version}: ${result.name}`);\n            skippedCount++;\n          } else if (result.error) {\n            console.log(`  ✗ Failed v${result.version}: ${result.name}`);\n            console.log(`    Error: ${result.error}`);\n          }\n        }\n\n        console.log(`\\nTotal: ${appliedCount} applied, ${skippedCount} skipped`);\n\n        if (options.dryRun) {\n          console.log(\"(dry-run mode - no changes were made)\");\n        }\n      } catch (error) {\n        console.error(\"Migration failed:\", error);\n        process.exit(1);\n      } finally {\n        db.close();\n      }\n .option(\"--to <version>\", \"Rollback to specific version\")\n  .option(\"--version <version>\", \"Rollback specific migration version\")\n  .option(\"--dry-run\", \"Preview rollback without executing\")\n  .action(\n    asyncAction(async (options) => {\n      const dbPath = getDbPath(options.db);\n      const db = openDatabase(dbPath);\n\n      try {\n        console.log(`Connecting to rolledBack = await rollbackToVersion(db, targetVersion, {\n            dryRun: options.dryRun ?? false,\n          });\n\n          if (rolledBack.length === 0) {\n            console.log(\"No migrations to rollback\");\n          } else {\n            console.log(`Rolled back ${rolledBack.length} migration(s):`);\n            for (const version of rolledBack) {\n              console.log(`  ✓ Rolled back v${version}`);\n            }\n          }\n\n          if (options.dryRun) {\n            console.log(\"(dry-run mode - no changes were made)\");\n          }\n        } else if (options.version) {\n          const version = parseInt(options.version, 10);\n          console.log(`Rolling back migration v${version}...`);\n\n          const success = await rollbackMigration(db, version, {\n            dryRun: options.dryRun ?? false,\n          });\n\n          if (success) {\n            console.log(`✓ Rolled back v${version}`);\n          } else {\n            console.log(`Migration v${version} not found or already rolled back`);\n          }\n\n          if (options.dryRun) {\n            console.log(\"(dry-run mode - no changes were made)\");\n          }\n        } else {\n          console.error(\"Either --to or --version must be specified\");\n          process.exit(1);\n        }\n      } catch (error) {\n        console.error(\"Rollback failed:\", error);\n        process.exit(1);\n      } finally {\n        db.close();\n      }\n    })\n  );\n\n/**\n * Show migration status.\n */\nprogram\n  .command(\"status\")\n  .description(\"Show migration status\")\n  .option(\"-d, --db <path>\", \"Database path\", DEFAULT_DB_PATH)\n  .action(\n    asyncAction(async (options) => {\n      const dbPath = console.error(\"Failed to get status:\", error);\n        process.exit(1);\n      } finally {\n        db.close();\n      }\n    })\n  );\n\n/**\n * Create a new migration.\n */\nprogram\n mkdir(BACKUP_DIR, { recursive: true });\n135\t\n136\t  const timestamp = new Date().toISOString().replace(/[:.]/g, \"-\");\n137\t  const basename = dbPath.split(\"/\").pop() ?? \"database.db\";\n138\t logger.info(\"Backup created\", { backupPath });\n143\t\n144\t  return backupPath;\n145\t}\n146\t\n147\t/**\n148\t * List all available backups.\n149\t *\n150\t * @returns Array of backup file information\n151\t */\n152\texport async function listBackups(): Promise<Array<{ path: string; size: number; created: Date }>> {\n153\t  try {\n154\t    const { readdir } = await import(\"node:fs/promises\");\n155\t    const { stat } = await import(\"node:fs/promises\");\n156\t    const files = await readdir(BACKUP_DIR);\n157\t    const backups: Array<{ path: string; size: number; created: Date }> = [];\n158\t\n159\t    for (const file of files) {\n160\t      if (file.endsWith(\".bak\")) {\n161\t        const fullPath = join(BACKUP_DIR, file);\n162\t        const stats = await stat(fullPath);\n163\t        backups.push({\n164\t          path: fullPath,\n165\t          size: stats.size,\n166\t          created: stats.mtime,\n167\t        });\n168\t      }\n169\t    }\n170\t\n171\t    return backups.sort((a, b) => b.created.getTime() - a.created.getTime());\n172\t  } catch {\n173\t    return [];\n174\t  }\n175\t}\n176\t\n177\t/**\n178\t * Restore a database from a backup.\n179\t *\n180\t * @param backupPath - Path to the backup file\n181\t * @param targetPath - Path where to restore the database\n182\t */\n183\texport async function restoreBackup(backupPath: string, targetPath: string): Promise<void> {\n184\t  const { copyFile } = await import(\"node:fs/promises\");\n185\t  await copyFile(backupPath, targetPath);\n186\t\n187\t  logger.info(\"Backup restored\", { backupPath, targetPath });\n188\t}\n189\t\n190\t/**\n191\t * Delete old backups, keeping only the most recent ones.\n192\t *\n193\t * @param keepCount - Number of backups to keep (default: 10)\n194\t */\n195\texport async function cleanupOldBackups(keepCount: number = 10): Promise<number> {\n196\t  const backups = await listBackups();\n197\t\n198\t  if (backups.length <= keepCount) {\n199\t    return 0;\n200\t  }\n201\t\n202\t  const toDelete = backups.slice(keepCount);\n203\t  let deletedCount = 0;\n204\t\n205\t  for (const backup of toDelete) {\n206\t    try {\n207\t      await unlink(backup.path);\n208\t      deletedCount++;\n209\t    } catch (err) {\n210\t      logger.warn(\"Failed to delete backup\", err instanceof Error ? err : undefined, {\n211\t        backupPath: backup.path,\n212\t      });\n213\t    }\n214\t  }\n215\t\n216\t  if (deletedCount > 0) {\n217\t    logger.info(\"Backups cleaned\", {\n218\t      deletedCount,\n219\t      remainingCount: backups.length - deletedCount,\n220\t    });\n221\t  }\n222\t\n223\t  return deletedCount;\n224\t}\n225\t\n226\t/**\n227\t * Load all migration files from the migrations directory.\n228\t *\n229\t * @param customDir - Optional custom directory to load migrations from\n230\t */\n231\tasync function loadMigrations(customDir?: string): Promise<Migration[]> {\n232\t  const migrations: Migration[] = [];\n233\t  const dir = customDir ?? MIGRATIONS_DIR;\n234\t\n235\t  try {\n236\t    const files = await readdir(dir);\n237\t    const migrationFiles = files.filter((f) => f.endsWith(\".ts\") && !f.endsWith(\".test.ts\"));\n238\t\n239\t    for (const file of migrationFiles) {\n240\t      // Extract version number from filename (e.g., 001-add-users.ts)\n241\t      const match = file.match(/^(\\d+)-(.+)\\.ts$/);\n242\t      if (!match) continue;\n243\t\n244\t      const [, versionStr, name] = match as [string, string, string];\n245\t      const version = parseInt(versionStr, 10);\n246\t\n247\t      try {\n248\t        const module = await import(join(dir, file));\n249\t        if (module.up && module.down && module.description) {\n250\t          migrations.push({\n251\t            version,\n252\t            name,\n253\t            description: module.description,\n254\t            up: module.up,\n255\t            down: module.down,\n256\t            validate: module.validate,\n257\t          });\n258\t        }\n259\t      } catch (err) {\n260\t        logger.error(\"Failed to load migration\", err instanceof Error ? err : undefined, {\n261\t          file,\n262\t        });\n263\t      }\n264\t    }\n265\t  } catch {\n266\t    // Migrations directory doesn't exist yet\n267\t    logger.debug(\"No migrations directory found\");\n268\t  }\n269\t\n270\t  return migrations.sort((a, b) => a.version - b.version);\n271\t}\n272\t\n273\t/**\n274\t * Run all pending migrations.\n275\t *\n276\t * @param db - Better-sqlite3 database instance\n277\t * @param options - Migration options (dryRun, backup, force)\n278\t * @param targetVersion - Optional target version to migrate to\n279\t * @returns Array of migration results\n280\t */\n281\texport async function runMigrations(\n282\t  db: Database.Database,\n283\t  options: MigrationOptions = {},\n284\t  targetVersion?: number\n285\t): Promise<MigrationResult[]> {\n286\t  // Acquire lock\n287\t  if (!acquireLock()) {\n288\t    throw new Error(\"Migration already in progress\");\n289\t  }\n290\t\n291\t  try {\n292\t    const { dryRun = false, force = false } = options;\n293\t\n294\t    initMigrationsTable(db);\n295\t    const applied = getAppliedMigrations(db);\n296\t    const migrations = await loadMigrations();\n297\t    const results: MigrationResult[] = [];\n298\t\n299\t    for (const migration of migrations) {\n300\t      // Skip if already applied\n301\t      if (applied.has(migration.version)) {\n302\t        results.push({\n303\t          version: migration.version,\n304\t          name: migration.name,\n305\t          description: migration.description,\n306\t          applied: false,\n307\t          skipped: true,\n308\t          executionTimeMs: 0,\n309\t        });\n310\t        continue;\n311\t      }\n312\t\n313\t      // Stop if target version is specified and we've reached it\n314\t      if (targetVersion !== undefined && migration.version > targetVersion) {\n315\t        break;\n316\t      }\n317\t\n318\t      const startTime = Date.now();\n319\t      let validationErrors: string[] | undefined;\n320\t\n321\t      // Run pre-migration validation if available\n322\t      if (migration.validate && !dryRun) {\n323\t        const validation = migration.validate(db);\n324\t        if (!validation.valid) {\n325\t          validationErrors = validation.errors;\n326\t          if (!force) {\n327\t            throw new Error(\n328\t              `Migration ${migration.version} validation failed: ${validationErrors?.join(\", \")}`\n329\t            );\n330\t          }\n331\t        }\n332\t      }\n333\t\n334\t      try {\n335\t        if (dryRun) {\n336\t          // In dry-run mode, just log what would be done\n337\t          logger.info(\"Migration dry run\", {\n338\t            version: migration.version,\n339\t            name: migration.name,\n340\t            description: migration.description,\n341\t            validationErrors,\n342\t          });\n343\t\n344\t          results.push({\n345\t            version: migration.version,\n346\t            name: migration.name,\n347\t            description: migration.description,\n348\t            applied: false,\n349\t            skipped: false,\n350\t            executionTimeMs: 0,\n351\t          });\n352\t        } else {\n353\t          // Run migration within a transaction\n354\t          db.transaction(() => {\n355\t            migration.up(db);\n356\t\n357\t            // Record migration\n358\t            db.prepare(\n359\t              \"INSERT INTO _migrations (version, name, description, appliedAt, executionTimeMs) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\"\n360\t            ).run(\n361\t              migration.version,\n362\t              migration.name,\n363\t              migration.description,\n364\t              new Date().toISOString(),\n365\t              Date.now() - startTime\n366\t            );\n367\t          })();\n368\t\n369\t          results.push({\n370\t            version: migration.version,\n371\t            name: migration.name,\n372\t            description: migration.description,\n373\t            applied: true,\n374\t            skipped: false,\n375\t            executionTimeMs: Date.now() - startTime,\n376\t          });\n377\t\n378\t          logger.info(\"Migration applied\", {\n379\t            version: migration.version,\n380\t            name: migration.name,\n381\t            description: migration.description,\n382\t            executionTimeMs: Date.now() - startTime,\n383\t          });\n384\t        }\n385\t      } catch (err) {\n386\t        const error = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);\n387\t        logger.error(\"Migration failed\", err instanceof Error ? err : undefined, {\n388\t          version: migration.version,\n389\t          name: migration.name,\n390\t          error,\n391\t        });\n392\t\n393\t        results.push({\n394\t          version: migration.version,\n395\t          name: migration.name,\n396\t          description: migration.description,\n397\t          applied: false,\n398\t          skipped: false,\n399\t          executionTimeMs: Date.now() - startTime,\n400\t          error,\n401\t        });\n402\t\n403\t        if (!dryRun) {\n404\t          throw err;\n405\t        }\n406\t      }\n407\t    }\n408\t\n409\t    return results;\n410\t  } Migration version to rollback\n420\t * @param options - Migration options (dryRun, backup)\n421\t * @returns True if rolled back, false if not found\n422\t */\n423\texport async function rollbackMigration(\n424\t  db: Database.Database,\n425\t  version: number,\n426\t  options: MigrationOptions = {}\n427\t): Promise<boolean> {\n428\t  // Acquire lock\n429\t  if (!acquireLock()) {\n430\t    throw new Error(\"Migration already in progress\");\n431\t  }\n432\t\n433\t  try {\n434\t    const { dryRun = false } = options;\n435\t\n436\t    initMigrationsTable(db);\n437\t    const logger.error(\"Rollback: migration file not found\", undefined, { version });\n449\t      return false;\n450\t    }\n451\t\n452\t    const startTime = Date.now();\n453\t\n454\t    if (dryRun) {\n455\t      logger.info(\"Rollback dry run\", {\n456\t        version: migration.version,\n457\t        name: migration.name,\n458\t      });\n459\t      return true;\n460\t    }\n461\t\n462\t    try {\n463\t      db.transaction(() => {\n464\t        // Run rollback\n465\t        migration.down(db);\n466\t\n467\t        // Remove from migrations table\n468\t        db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM _migrations WHERE version = ?\").run(version);\n469\t      })();\n470\t\n471\t      logger.info(\"Migration rolled back\", {\n472\t        version: migration.version,\n473\t        name: migration.name,\n474\t        executionTimeMs: Date.now() - startTime,\n475\t      });\n476\t\n477\t      return true;\n478\t    } catch (err) {\n479\t      logger.error(\"Rollback failed\", err instanceof Error ? err : undefined, {\n480\t        version: migration.version,\n481\t        name: migration.name,\n482\t      });\n483\t      throw err;\n484\t    }\n485\t  } finally {\n486\t    releaseLock();\n487\t  }\n488\t}\n489\t\n490\t/**\n491\t * Rollback all migrations after a specific version.\n492\t *\n493\t * @param db - Better-sqlite3 database instance\n494\t * @param targetVersion - Target version to rollback to (all migrations after this will be rolled back)\n495\t * @param options - Migration options (dryRun, backup)\n496\t * @returns Array of rolled back migration versions\n497\t */\n498\texport async function rollbackToVersion(\n499\t  db: Database.Database,\n500\t  targetVersion: number,\n501\t  options: MigrationOptions = {}\n502\t): Promise<number[]> {\n503\t  // Acquire lock\n504\t  if (!acquireLock()) {\n505\t    throw new Error(\"Migration already in progress\");\n506\t  }\n507\t\n508\t  try {\n509\t    const { dryRun = false } = options;\n510\t\n511\t    initMigrationsTable(db);\n512\t    const applied = db\n513\t      .prepare(\"SELECT version FROM _migrations ORDER BY version DESC\")\n514\t      .all() as Array<{ version: number }>;\n515\t\n516\t    // Find all migrations to rollback (those greater than targetVersion)\n517\t    const toRollback = applied.filter((m) => m.version > targetVersion);\n518\t\n519\t    if (toRollback.length === 0) {\n520\t      logger.info(\"Rollback to version: no migrations to rollback\", {\n521\t        targetVersion,\n522\t      });\n523\t      return [];\n524\t    }\n525\t\n526\t    if (dryRun) {\n527\t      logger.info(\"Rollback to version dry run\", {\n528\t        targetVersion,\n529\t        migrationsToRollback: toRollback.map((m) => m.version),\n530\t      });\n531\t      return toRollback.map((m) => m.version);\n532\t    }\n533\t\n534\t    const rolledBack: number[] = [];\n535\t\n536\t    // Rollback migrations in reverse order (highest version first)\n537\t    for (const { version } of toRollback.sort((a, b) => b.version - a.version)) {\n538\t      const success = await rollbackMigration(db, version, { dryRun: false });\n539\t      if (success) {\n540\t        rolledBack.push(version);\n541\t      } else {\n542\t        throw new Error(`Failed to rollback migration ${version}`);\n543\t      }\n544\t    }\n545\t\n546\t    logger.info(\"Rollback to version complete\", {\n547\t      targetVersion,\n548\t      rolledBackVersions: rolledBack,\n549\t    });\n550\t\n551\t    return rolledBack;\n552\t  } finally {\n553\t    releaseLock();\n554\t  }\n555\t}\n556\t\n557\t/**\n558\t * Get migration status.\n559\t *\n560\t * @param db - Better-sqlite3 database instance\n561\t * @returns Applied and pending migrations\n562\t */\n563\texport async function getMigrationStatus(db: Database.Database): Promise<{\n564\t  applied: MigrationRecord[];\n565\t  pending: Migration[];\n566\t  currentVersion: number;\n567\t  latestVersion: number;\n568\t}> {\n569\t  initMigrationsTable(db);\n570\t  const applied = db\n571\t    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM _migrations ORDER BY version\")\n572\t    .all() as Math.max(...applied.map((a) => a.version)) : 0;\n579\t  const latestVersion = migrations.length > 0 ? Array<Record<string, unknown>> | undefined;\n692\t    if (fkResult && fkResult.length > 0) {\n693\t      errors.push(`Foreign key violations found: ${JSON.stringify(fkResult)}`);\n694\t .all() as Array<{ name: string }>;\n700\t\n701\t    for (const { name } of tables) {\n702\t      const pkInfo = db.pragma(`table_info(${name})`) as Array<{ pk: number }>;\n703\t      const hasPK = version: number;\n716\t    }>;\n717\t\n718\t    // Check for gaps in migration versions\n719\t    for (let i = 1; i < migrations.length; i++) {\n720\t      const current = migrations[i];\n721\t and ${current.version}`);\n724\t      }\n725\t    }\n726\t  } catch (err) {\n727\t    errors.push(`Database validation error: ${err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)}`);\n728\t };\n735\t}\n736\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"b6d66451-43d3-43fc-8fc1-2f6e30be96e3","uuid":"6ec4a3b4-0c0d-41b6-8104-a6b5db9725d8","timestamp":"2026-07-03T20:13:42.317Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/mta-my-way/packages/server/src/migration/migration.ts","content":"/**\n * Data migration system with version tracking, rollback, and safety features.\n *\n * Migrations are numbered files in packages/server/src/migration/migrations/\n * Each migration exports:\n * - up(): Function to apply the migration\n * - down(): Function to rollback the migration\n * - description: Human-readable description\n *\n * Features:\n * - Dry-run mode to preview migrations without executing\n * - Pre-migration database backup\n * - Migration locking to prevent concurrent runs\n * - Rollback to specific version\n * - Data validation helpers\n *\n * Usage:\n * ```ts\n * import { runMigrations, rollbackToVersion } from './migration/index.js';\n * await runMigrations(db);\n * await rollbackToVersion(db, 5); // Rollback all migrations after version 5\n * ```\n */\n\nimport { copyFile, mkdir, readdir, unlink } from \"node:fs/promises\";\nimport { constants } from \"node:os\";\nimport type { constants as Constants } from \"node:os\";\nimport { dirname, join } from \"node:path\";\nimport { fileURLToPath } from \"node:url\";\nimport type Database from \"better-sqlite3\";\nimport { logger } from \"../observability/logger.js\";\n\nconst __dirname = dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url));\nconst MIGRATIONS_DIR = join(__dirname, \"migrations\");\nconst BACKUP_DIR = join(__dirname, \"../../data/backups\");\n\n/** Migration interface */\nexport interface Migration {\n  version: number;\n  name: string;\n  description: string;\n  up: (db: Database.Database) => void;\n  down: (db: Database.Database) => void;\n  validate?: (db: Database.Database) => { valid: boolean; errors?: string[] };\n}\n\n/** Migration record in database */\nexport interface MigrationRecord {\n  version: number;\n  name: string;\n  description: string | null;\n  appliedAt: string;\n  executionTimeMs: number;\n}\n\n/** Migration options */\nexport interface MigrationOptions {\n  dryRun?: boolean;\n  backup?: boolean;\n  force?: boolean;\n}\n\n/** Migration result */\nexport interface MigrationResult {\n  version: number;\n  name: string;\n  description: string;\n  applied: boolean;\n  skipped: boolean;\n  executionTimeMs: number;\n  error?: string;\n}\n\n/** Migration lock */\nlet migrationLock = false;\n\n/**\n * Acquire migration lock to prevent concurrent migrations.\n */\nfunction acquireLock(): boolean {\n  if (migrationLock) {\n    return false;\n  }\n  migrationLock = true;\n  return true;\n}\n\n/**\n * Release migration lock.\n */\nfunction releaseLock(): void {\n  migrationLock = false;\n}\n\n/**\n * Initialize migrations table in the database.\n */\nfunction initMigrationsTable(db: Database.Database): void {\n  db.exec(`\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS _migrations (\n      version INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,\n      name TEXT NOT NULL,\n      description TEXT,\n      appliedAt TEXT NOT NULL,\n      executionTimeMs INTEGER NOT NULL\n    )\n  `);\n\n  // Add checksum column for data integrity if not exists\n  try {\n    db.exec(`ALTER TABLE _migrations ADD COLUMN checksum TEXT`);\n  } catch {\n    // Column already exists\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Get all applied migrations from the database.\n */\nfunction getAppliedMigrations(db: Database.Database): Set<number> {\n  const rows = db.prepare(\"SELECT version FROM _migrations ORDER BY version\").all() as Array<{\n    version: number;\n  }>;\n  return new Set(rows.map((r) => r.version));\n}\n\n/**\n * Create a backup of the database file.\n *\n * @param dbPath - Path to the database file\n * @returns Path to the backup file\n */\nexport async function createBackup(dbPath: string): Promise<string> {\n  await mkdir(BACKUP_DIR, { recursive: true });\n\n  const timestamp = new Date().toISOString().replace(/[:.]/g, \"-\");\n  const basename = dbPath.split(\"/\").pop() ?? \"database.db\";\n  const backupPath = join(BACKUP_DIR, `${basename}-${timestamp}.bak`);\n\n  await copyFile(dbPath, backupPath, (constants as typeof Constants).COPYFILE_EXCL);\n\n  logger.info(\"Backup created\", { backupPath });\n\n  return backupPath;\n}\n\n/**\n * List all available backups.\n *\n * @returns Array of backup file information\n */\nexport async function listBackups(): Promise<Array<{ path: string; size: number; created: Date }>> {\n  try {\n    const { readdir } = await import(\"node:fs/promises\");\n    const { stat } = await import(\"node:fs/promises\");\n    const files = await readdir(BACKUP_DIR);\n    const backups: Array<{ path: string; size: number; created: Date }> = [];\n\n    for (const file of files) {\n      if (file.endsWith(\".bak\")) {\n        const fullPath = join(BACKUP_DIR, file);\n        const stats = await stat(fullPath);\n        backups.push({\n          path: fullPath,\n          size: stats.size,\n          created: stats.mtime,\n        });\n      }\n    }\n\n    return backups.sort((a, b) => b.created.getTime() - a.created.getTime());\n  } catch {\n    return [];\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Restore a database from a backup.\n *\n * @param backupPath - Path to the backup file\n * @param targetPath - Path where to restore the database\n */\nexport async function restoreBackup(backupPath: string, targetPath: string): Promise<void> {\n  const { copyFile } = await import(\"node:fs/promises\");\n  await copyFile(backupPath, targetPath);\n\n  logger.info(\"Backup restored\", { backupPath, targetPath });\n}\n\n/**\n * Delete old backups, keeping only the most recent ones.\n *\n * @param keepCount - Number of backups to keep (default: 10)\n */\nexport async function cleanupOldBackups(keepCount: number = 10): Promise<number> {\n  const backups = await listBackups();\n\n  if (backups.length <= keepCount) {\n    return 0;\n  }\n\n  const toDelete = backups.slice(keepCount);\n  let deletedCount = 0;\n\n  for (const backup of toDelete) {\n    try {\n      await unlink(backup.path);\n      deletedCount++;\n    } catch (err) {\n      logger.warn(\"Failed to delete backup\", err instanceof Error ? err : undefined, {\n        backupPath: backup.path,\n      });\n    }\n  }\n\n  if (deletedCount > 0) {\n    logger.info(\"Backups cleaned\", {\n      deletedCount,\n      remainingCount: backups.length - deletedCount,\n    });\n  }\n\n  return deletedCount;\n}\n\n/**\n * Load all migration files from the migrations directory.\n *\n * @param customDir - Optional custom directory to load migrations from\n */\nasync function loadMigrations(customDir?: string): Promise<Migration[]> {\n  const migrations: Migration[] = [];\n  const dir = customDir ?? MIGRATIONS_DIR;\n\n  try {\n    const files = await readdir(dir);\n    const migrationFiles = files.filter((f) => f.endsWith(\".ts\") && !f.endsWith(\".test.ts\"));\n\n    for (const file of migrationFiles) {\n      // Extract version number from filename (e.g., 001-add-users.ts)\n      const match = file.match(/^(\\d+)-(.+)\\.ts$/);\n      if (!match) continue;\n\n      const [, versionStr, name] = match as [string, string, string];\n      const version = parseInt(versionStr, 10);\n\n      try {\n        const module = await import(join(dir, file));\n        if (module.up && module.down && module.description) {\n          migrations.push({\n            version,\n            name,\n            description: module.description,\n            up: module.up,\n            down: module.down,\n            validate: module.validate,\n          });\n        }\n      } catch (err) {\n        logger.error(\"Failed to load migration\", err instanceof Error ? err : undefined, {\n          file,\n        });\n      }\n    }\n  } catch {\n    // Migrations directory doesn't exist yet\n    logger.debug(\"No migrations directory found\");\n  }\n\n  return migrations.sort((a, b) => a.version - b.version);\n}\n\n/**\n * Run all pending migrations.\n *\n * @param db - Better-sqlite3 database instance\n * @param options - Migration options (dryRun, backup, force)\n * @param targetVersion - Optional target version to migrate to\n * @returns Array of migration results\n */\nexport async function runMigrations(\n  db: Database.Database,\n  options: MigrationOptions = {},\n  targetVersion?: number\n): Promise<MigrationResult[]> {\n  // Acquire lock\n  if (!acquireLock()) {\n    throw new Error(\"Migration already in progress\");\n  }\n\n  try {\n    const { dryRun = false, force = false } = options;\n\n    initMigrationsTable(db);\n    const applied = getAppliedMigrations(db);\n    const migrations = await loadMigrations();\n    const results: MigrationResult[] = [];\n\n    for (const migration of migrations) {\n      // Skip if already applied\n      if (applied.has(migration.version)) {\n        results.push({\n          version: migration.version,\n          name: migration.name,\n          description: migration.description,\n          applied: false,\n          skipped: true,\n          executionTimeMs: 0,\n        });\n        continue;\n      }\n\n      // Stop if target version is specified and we've reached it\n      if (targetVersion !== undefined && migration.version > targetVersion) {\n        break;\n      }\n\n      const startTime = Date.now();\n      let validationErrors: string[] | undefined;\n\n      // Run pre-migration validation if available\n      if (migration.validate && !dryRun) {\n        const validation = migration.validate(db);\n        if (!validation.valid) {\n          validationErrors = validation.errors;\n          if (!force) {\n            throw new Error(\n              `Migration ${migration.version} validation failed: ${validationErrors?.join(\", \")}`\n            );\n          }\n        }\n      }\n\n      try {\n        if (dryRun) {\n          // In dry-run mode, just log what would be done\n          logger.info(\"Migration dry run\", {\n            version: migration.version,\n            name: migration.name,\n            description: migration.description,\n            validationErrors,\n          });\n\n          results.push({\n            version: migration.version,\n            name: migration.name,\n            description: migration.description,\n            applied: false,\n            skipped: false,\n            executionTimeMs: 0,\n          });\n        } else {\n          // Run migration within a transaction\n          db.transaction(() => {\n            migration.up(db);\n\n            // Record migration\n            db.prepare(\n              \"INSERT INTO _migrations (version, name, description, appliedAt, executionTimeMs) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\"\n            ).run(\n              migration.version,\n              migration.name,\n              migration.description,\n              new Date().toISOString(),\n              Date.now() - startTime\n            );\n          })();\n\n          results.push({\n            version: migration.version,\n            name: migration.name,\n            description: migration.description,\n            applied: true,\n            skipped: false,\n            executionTimeMs: Date.now() - startTime,\n          });\n\n          logger.info(\"Migration applied\", {\n            version: migration.version,\n            name: migration.name,\n            description: migration.description,\n            executionTimeMs: Date.now() - startTime,\n          });\n        }\n      } catch (err) {\n        const error = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);\n        logger.error(\"Migration failed\", err instanceof Error ? err : undefined, {\n          version: migration.version,\n          name: migration.name,\n          error,\n        });\n\n        results.push({\n          version: migration.version,\n          name: migration.name,\n          description: migration.description,\n          applied: false,\n          skipped: false,\n          executionTimeMs: Date.now() - startTime,\n          error,\n        });\n\n        if (!dryRun) {\n          throw err;\n        }\n      }\n    }\n\n    return results;\n  } finally {\n    releaseLock();\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Rollback a specific migration.\n *\n * @param db - Better-sqlite3 database instance\n * @param version - Migration version to rollback\n * @param options - Migration options (dryRun, backup)\n * @returns True if rolled back, false if not found\n */\nexport async function rollbackMigration(\n  db: Database.Database,\n  version: number,\n  options: MigrationOptions = {}\n): Promise<boolean> {\n  // Acquire lock\n  if (!acquireLock()) {\n    throw new Error(\"Migration already in progress\");\n  }\n\n  try {\n    const { dryRun = false } = options;\n\n    initMigrationsTable(db);\n    const applied = getAppliedMigrations(db);\n\n    if (!applied.has(version)) {\n      logger.warn(\"Rollback: migration not applied\", { version });\n      return false;\n    }\n\n    const migrations = await loadMigrations();\n    const migration = migrations.find((m) => m.version === version);\n\n    if (!migration) {\n      logger.error(\"Rollback: migration file not found\", undefined, { version });\n      return false;\n    }\n\n    const startTime = Date.now();\n\n    if (dryRun) {\n      logger.info(\"Rollback dry run\", {\n        version: migration.version,\n        name: migration.name,\n      });\n      return true;\n    }\n\n    try {\n      db.transaction(() => {\n        // Run rollback\n        migration.down(db);\n\n        // Remove from migrations table\n        db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM _migrations WHERE version = ?\").run(version);\n      })();\n\n      logger.info(\"Migration rolled back\", {\n        version: migration.version,\n        name: migration.name,\n        executionTimeMs: Date.now() - startTime,\n      });\n\n      return true;\n    } catch (err) {\n      logger.error(\"Rollback failed\", err instanceof Error ? err : undefined, {\n        version: migration.version,\n        name: migration.name,\n      });\n      throw err;\n    }\n  } finally {\n    releaseLock();\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Rollback all migrations after a specific version.\n *\n * @param db - Better-sqlite3 database instance\n * @param targetVersion - Target version to rollback to (all migrations after this will be rolled back)\n * @param options - Migration options (dryRun, backup)\n * @returns Array of rolled back migration versions\n */\nexport async function rollbackToVersion(\n  db: Database.Database,\n  targetVersion: number,\n  options: MigrationOptions = {}\n): Promise<number[]> {\n  // Acquire lock\n  if (!acquireLock()) {\n    throw new Error(\"Migration already in progress\");\n  }\n\n  try {\n    const { dryRun = false } = options;\n\n    initMigrationsTable(db);\n    const applied = db\n      .prepare(\"SELECT version FROM _migrations ORDER BY version DESC\")\n      .all() as Array<{ version: number }>;\n\n    // Find all migrations to rollback (those greater than targetVersion)\n    const toRollback = applied.filter((m) => m.version > targetVersion);\n\n    if (toRollback.length === 0) {\n      logger.info(\"Rollback to version: no migrations to rollback\", {\n        targetVersion,\n      });\n      return [];\n    }\n\n    if (dryRun) {\n      logger.info(\"Rollback to version dry run\", {\n        targetVersion,\n        migrationsToRollback: toRollback.map((m) => m.version),\n      });\n      return toRollback.map((m) => m.version);\n    }\n\n    const rolledBack: number[] = [];\n\n    // Rollback migrations in reverse order (highest version first)\n    for (const { version } of toRollback.sort((a, b) => b.version - a.version)) {\n      const success = await rollbackMigration(db, version, { dryRun: false });\n      if (success) {\n        rolledBack.push(version);\n      } else {\n        throw new Error(`Failed to rollback migration ${version}`);\n      }\n    }\n\n    logger.info(\"Rollback to version complete\", {\n      targetVersion,\n      rolledBackVersions: rolledBack,\n    });\n\n    return rolledBack;\n  } finally {\n    releaseLock();\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Get migration status.\n *\n * @param db - Better-sqlite3 database instance\n * @returns Applied and pending migrations\n */\nexport async function getMigrationStatus(db: Database.Database): Promise<{\n  applied: MigrationRecord[];\n  pending: Migration[];\n  currentVersion: number;\n  latestVersion: number;\n}> {\n  initMigrationsTable(db);\n  const applied = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM _migrations ORDER BY version\")\n    .all() as MigrationRecord[];\n\n  const migrations = await loadMigrations();\n  const appliedVersions = new Set(applied.map((a) => a.version));\n  const pending = migrations.filter((m) => !appliedVersions.has(m.version));\n\n  const currentVersion = applied.length > 0 ? Math.max(...applied.map((a) => a.version)) : 0;\n  const latestVersion = migrations.length > 0 ? Math.max(...migrations.map((m) => m.version)) : 0;\n\n  return { applied, pending, currentVersion, latestVersion };\n}\n\n/**\n * Create a new migration file.\n *\n * @param name - Migration name (kebab-case)\n * @param description - Human-readable description\n * @param includeValidation - Whether to include validation template\n * @param migrationsDir - Optional custom migrations directory (for testing)\n * @returns Path to the created migration file\n */\nexport async function createMigration(\n  name: string,\n  description: string,\n  includeValidation = false,\n  migrationsDir = MIGRATIONS_DIR\n): Promise<string> {\n  const migrations = await loadMigrations(migrationsDir);\n  const nextVersion = migrations.length > 0 ? Math.max(...migrations.map((m) => m.version)) + 1 : 1;\n  const filename = `${String(nextVersion).padStart(3, \"0\")}-${name}.ts`;\n  const filepath = join(migrationsDir, filename);\n\n  const validationTemplate = includeValidation\n    ? `\n/**\n * Validate migration preconditions.\n *\n * @param db - Better-sqlite3 database instance\n * @returns Validation result with any errors\n */\nexport function validate(db: Database.Database): { valid: boolean; errors?: string[] } {\n  const errors: string[] = [];\n\n  // TODO: Add validation logic (e.g., check data integrity, foreign keys, etc.)\n  // Example:\n  // const tableExists = db.prepare(\n  //   \"SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name=?\"\n  // ).get(\"existing_table\");\n  // if (!tableExists) {\n  //   errors.push(\"Required table 'existing_table' does not exist\");\n  // }\n\n  return {\n    valid: errors.length === 0,\n    errors: errors.length > 0 ? errors : undefined,\n  };\n}\n`\n    : \"\";\n\n  const template = `/**\n * Migration: ${description}\n */\n\nimport type Database from \"better-sqlite3\";\n\nexport const description = \"${description}\";\n\n/**\n * Apply the migration.\n *\n * @param db -
