Better-sqlite3 database instance\n * @returns Validation result\n */\nexport function validateDatabase(db: Database.Database): {\n  valid: boolean;\n  errors: string[];\n  warnings: string[];\n} {\n  const errors: string[] = [];\n  const warnings: string[] = [];\n\n  try {\n    // Check for foreign key violations\n    const fkResult = db.pragma(\"foreign_key_check\") as Array<Record<string, unknown>> | undefined;\n    if (fkResult && fkResult.length > 0) {\n      errors.push(`Foreign key violations found: ${JSON.stringify(fkResult)}`);\n    }\n\n    // Check for tables without primary keys\n    const tables = db\n      .prepare(\"SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name NOT LIKE '__%'\")\n      .all() as Array<{ name: string }>;\n\n    for (const { name } of tables) {\n      const pkInfo = db.pragma(`table_info(${name})`) as Array<{ pk: number }>;\n      const hasPK = pkInfo.some((col) => col.pk > 0);\n\n      if (!hasPK) {\n        warnings.push(`Table '${name}' has no primary key`);\n      }\n    }\n\n    // Check migration table integrity\n    initMigrationsTable(db);\n    const migrations = db\n      .prepare(\"SELECT version FROM _migrations ORDER BY version\")\n      .all() as Array<{\n      version: number;\n    }>;\n\n    // Check for gaps in migration versions\n    for (let i = 1; i < migrations.length; i++) {\n      const current = migrations[i];\n      const previous = migrations[i - 1];\n      if (current && previous && current.version !== previous.version + 1) {\n        errors.push(`Gap in migration versions between ${previous.version} and ${current.version}`);\n      }\n    }\n  } catch (err) {\n    errors.push(`Database validation error: ${err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)}`);\n  }\n\n  return {\n    valid: errors.length === 0,\n    errors,\n    warnings,\n  };\n}\n","numLines":736,"startLine":1,"totalLines":736}}}

return;\n475\t    }\n476\t\n477\t    if (command === \"backups\") {\n478\t      await cmdBackups();\n479\t      return;\n480\t    }\n481\t\n482\t    if (command === \"restore\") {\n483\t      await cmdRestore(commandArgs[0], values.db);\n484\t      return;\n485\t    }\n486\t\n487\t    if (command === \"cleanup\") {\n488\t      await cmdCleanup(commandArgs[0]);\n489\t      return;\n490\t    }\n491\t\n492\t    // Commands that require database connection\n493\t    try {\n494\t      db = await initDatabase(values.db);\n495\t    } catch (err) {\n496\t      console.error(\n497\t        JSON.stringify({\n498\t          event: \"database_init_error\",\n499\t          error: err.message,\n500\t          dbPath: values.db,\n501\t        })\n502\t      );\n503\t      process.exit(1);\n504\t    }\n505\t\n506\t    try {\n507\t      switch (command) {\n508\t        case \"status\":\n509\t          await cmdStatus(db);\n510\t          break;\n511\t\n512\t        case \"up\":\n513\t          await cmdUp(db, commandArgs[0], values);\n514\t          break;\n515\t\n516\t        case \"down\":\n517\t          if (!commandArgs[0]) {\n518\t            console.error(\"Error: Version number is required for 'down' command\");\n519\t            process.exit(1);\n520\t          }\n521\t          await cmdDown(db, commandArgs[0], values);\n522\t          break;\n523\t\n524\t        case \"rollback\":\n525\t          if (!commandArgs[0]) {\n526\t            console.error(\"Error: Target version is required for 'rollback' command\");\n527\t            process.exit(1);\n528\t          }\n529\t          await cmdRollback(db, commandArgs[0], values);\n530\t          break;\n531\t\n532\t        case \"validate\":\n533\t          await cmdValidate(db);\n534\t          break;\n535\t\n536\t        case \"backup\":\n537\t          await cmdBackup(db, values.db);\n538\t          break;\n539\t\n540\t        default:\n541\t          // No command specified - run pending migrations\n542\t          if (!command || command === \"\") {\n543\t            // Create backup before migrations unless disabled\n544\t            if (!values[\"no-backup\"] && !values.dryRun) {\n545\t              try {\n546\t                await cmdBackup(db, values.db);\n547\t              } catch (err) {\n548\t   }\n557\t      }\n558\t    } finally {\n559\t      if (db) {\n560\t        db.close();\n561\t      }\n562\t    }\n563\t  } catch (err) {\n564\t    console.error(\n565\t      JSON.stringify({\n566\t        event: \"error\",\n567\t        command,\n568\t        error: err.message,\n569\t        stack: err.stack,\n570\t      })\n571\t    );\n572\t    process.exit(1);\n573\t from backup\n *   backups          List available backups\n *   cleanup [n]      Delete old backups (keep n, default: 10)\n *\n * Options:\n *   --dry-run        Preview changes without executing\n *   --force          Run even if validation fails\n *   --db <path>      Custom database path\n *   --no-backup      Skip automatic backup\n *   --help           Show this help message\n */\n\nimport { path\nconst DEFAULT_DB_PATH = join(__dirname, \"../packages/server/data/subscriptions.db\");\n\n/**\n * Parse command-line arguments.\n */\nfunction parseArguments(args) {\n  const { values, Way\n\nUsage:\n  node scripts/migrate.mjs [command] [options]\n\nCommands:\n  (none)           Run all pending migrations\n  status           Show migration status\n  up [version]     Run migrations default: 10)\n\nOptions:\n  --dry-run        Preview changes without executing\n  --force          Run even if validation fails\n  --db <path>      Custom database path (default: packages/server/data/subscriptions.db)\n  --no-backup      Skip automatic backup before migrations\n  --help           Show this help message\n  --validate       Include validation in new migration\n\nExamples:\n  node scripts/migrate.mjs                    # Run pending migrations\n  node scripts/migrate.mjs status              # Show status\n  node scripts/migrate.mjs up 5               # Run up to version 5\n  node scripts/migrate.mjs rollback 10        # Rollback to version 10\n  node scripts/migrate.mjs create add-users   # Create new migration\n  node scripts/migrate.mjs JSON.stringify({\n      event: \"migrate_up\",\n      targetVersion: version,\n      dryRun: options.dryRun,\n    })\n  );\n\n  const results = await runMigrations(\n    db,\n    { dryRun: applied: applied.length,\n      targetVersion: version,\n    })\n  );\n}\n\n/**\n * Command: Rollback a specific migration.\n */\nasync function cmdDown(db, version, options) {\n  const { JSON.stringify({\n        event: \"migrate_down_complete\",\n        version: targetVersion,\n      })\n    );\n  } else {\n    console.log(\n      JSON.stringify({\n        event: rollbackToVersion(db, version, { dryRun: options.dryRun });\n\n  console.log(\n    JSON.stringify({\n      event: \"rollback_complete\",\n      targetVersion: version,\n      rolledBackCount: process.exit(1);\n  }\n\n  // Convert name to kebab-case if it contains spaces\n  const migrationName = name.toLowerCase().replace(/\\s+/g, \"-\");\n  const description = name\n    .split(\"-\")\n    .map((word) => word.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + word.slice(1))\n    .join(\" \");\n\n  console.log(\n    JSON.stringify({\n      event: \"create_migration\",\n      name: migrationName,\n      description,\n    })\n  );\n\n  const filepath = await createMigration(migrationName, description, options.validate);\n\n  console.log(\n    JSON.stringify({\n      event: \"migration_created\",\n      filepath,\n    })\n  );\n}\n\n/**\n * Command: Validate database integrity.\n */\nasync function cmdValidate(db) {\n  const { validateDatabase } = await loadMigrationModule();\n\n  console.log(JSON.stringify({ event: \"validate_start\" }));\n\n  const result = validateDatabase(db);\n\n  console.log(\n    JSON.stringify({\n      event: \"validate_complete\",\n      valid: result.valid,\n      errorCount: result.errors.length,\n      warningCount: result.warnings.length,\n      errors: result.errors,\n      warnings: result.warnings,\n    })\n  );\n\n  if (!result.valid) {\n    process.exit(1);\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Command: Create a database backup.\n */\nasync function cmdBackup(db, dbPath) {\n  const { createBackup } = await loadMigrationModule();\n\n  console.log(\n    JSON.stringify({\n      event: \"backup_start\",\n      database: dbPath,\n    })\n  );\n\n  const backupPath = await createBackup(dbPath);\n\n  console.log(\n    JSON.stringify({\n      event: \"backup_complete\",\n      backupPath,\n    })\n  );\n}\n\n/**\n * Command: Restore from backup.\n */\nasync function cmdRestore(backupFile, dbPath) {\n  const { restoreBackup } = await loadMigrationModule();\n\n  if (!backupFile) {\n    console.error(\"Error: Backup file path is required\");\n    console.error(\"Usage: node scripts/migrate.mjs restore <backup-file>\");\n    process.exit(1);\n  }\n\n  console.log(\n    JSON.stringify({\n      event: \"restore_start\",\n      backup: backupFile,\n      target: dbPath,\n    })\n  );\n\n  await restoreBackup(backupFile, dbPath);\n\n  console.log(\n    JSON.stringify({\n      event: \"restore_complete\",\n      backup: backupFile,\n      target: dbPath,\n    })\n  );\n}\n\n/**\n * Command: List available backups.\n */\nasync function cmdBackups() {\n  const { listBackups } = await loadMigrationModule();\n\n  const backups = await listBackups();\n\n  console.log(\n    JSON.stringify({\n      event: \"backups_list\",\n      count: backups.length,\n      backups: backups.map((b) => ({\n        path: b.path,\n        size: b.size,\n        created: b.created.toISOString(),\n      })),\n    })\n  );\n}\n\n/**\n * Command: Cleanup old backups.\n */\nasync function cmdCleanup(keepCount) {\n  const { cleanupOldBackups } = await loadMigrationModule();\n  const keep = keepCount ? parseInt(keepCount, 10) : 10;\n\n  console.log(\n    JSON.stringify({\n      event: \"cleanup_start\",\n      keepCount: keep,\n    })\n  );\n\n  const deletedCount = await cleanupOldBackups(keep);\n\n  console.log(\n    JSON.stringify({\n      event: \"cleanup_complete\",\n      deletedCount,\n      remainingCount: Math.max(0, keep - deletedCount),\n    })\n  );\n}\n\n/**\n * Main entry point.\n */\nasync function main() {\n  const args = process.argv.slice(2);\n  const { values, positionals } = parseArguments(args);\n\n  if (values.help) {\n    showHelp();\n    return;\n  }\n\n  const [command, ...commandArgs] = positionals;\n\n  try {\n    let db;\n\n    // Commands that don't require database connection\n    if (command === \"create\") {\n      await cmdCreate(commandArgs[0], values);\n      return;\n    }\n\n    if (command === \"backups\") {\n      await cmdBackups();\n      return;\n    }\n\n    if (command === \"restore\") {\n      await cmdRestore(commandArgs[0], values.db);\n      return;\n    }\n\n    if (command === \"cleanup\") {\n      await cmdCleanup(commandArgs[0]);\n      return;\n    }\n\n    // Commands that require database connection\n    try {\n      db = await initDatabase(values.db);\n    } catch (err) {\n      console.error(\n        JSON.stringify({\n          event: \"database_init_error\",\n          error: err.message,\n          dbPath: values.db,\n        })\n      );\n      process.exit(1);\n    }\n\n    try {\n      switch (command) {\n        case \"status\":\n          await cmdStatus(db);\n          break;\n\n        case \"up\":\n          await cmdUp(db, commandArgs[0], values);\n          break;\n\n        case \"down\":\n          if (!commandArgs[0]) {\n            console.error(\"Error: Version number is required for 'down' command\");\n            process.exit(1);\n          }\n          await cmdDown(db, commandArgs[0], values);\n          break;\n\n        case \"rollback\":\n          if (!commandArgs[0]) {\n            console.error(\"Error: Target version is required for 'rollback' command\");\n            process.exit(1);\n          }\n          await cmdRollback(db, commandArgs[0], values);\n          break;\n\n        case \"validate\":\n          await cmdValidate(db);\n          break;\n\n        case \"backup\":\n          await cmdBackup(db, values.db);\n          break;\n\n        default:\n          // No command specified - run pending migrations\n          if (!command || command === \"\") {\n            // Create backup before migrations unless disabled\n            if (!values[\"no-backup\"] && !values.dryRun) {\n              try {\n                await cmdBackup(db, values.db);\n              } catch (err) {\n                console.warn(\"Warning: Backup failed, proceeding with migrations:\", err.message);\n              }\n            }\n            await cmdMigrate(db, values);\n          } else {\n            console.error(`Unknown command: ${command}`);\n            console.error('Run \"node scripts/migrate.mjs --help\" for usage');\n            process.exit(1);\n          }\n      }\n    } finally {\n      if (db) {\n        db.close();\n      }\n    }\n  } catch (err) {\n    console.error(\n      JSON.stringify({\n        event: \"error\",\n        command,\n        error: err.message,\n        stack: err.stack,\n      })\n    );\n    process.exit(1);\n  }\n}\n\nmain();\n","numLines":577,"startLine":1,"totalLines":577}}}

\"better-sqlite3\";\n10\timport {\n11\t  buildColumnList,\n12\t  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars\n13\t  buildPlaceholderList,\n14\t  validateColumnName,\n15\t null;\n21\t\n22\t/** Seed data entry */\n23\texport interface SeedData<T = Record<string, unknown>> {\n24\t  table: string;\n25\t  data: T[];\n26\t  uniqueKeys?: string[]; // Columns that define uniqueness\n27\t  skipDuplicates?: boolean; // Skip rows that would violate constraints\n28\t}\n29\t\n30\t/** Seed operation result */\n31\texport interface SeedResult {\n32\t  table: string;\n33\t  inserted: number;\n34\t  skipped: number;\n35\t  errors: string[];\n36\t}\n37\t\n38\t/**\n39\t * Seed data into a table with conflict handling.\n40\t *\n41\t * @param db - Database instance\n42\t * @param table - Table name\n43\t * @param data - Array of data objects to insert\n44\t * @param options - Seeding options\n45\t * @returns Seed result with counts\n46\t */\n47\texport function seedData<T extends Record<string, unknown>>(\n48\t  db: Database.Database,\n49\t  table: string,\n50\t  data: T[],\n51\t  options: {\n52\t    skipDuplicates?: boolean;\n53\t    uniqueKeys?: string[];\n54\t    onConflict?: \"ignore\" | \"replace\" | \"update\";\n55\t  } = {}\n56\t): SeedResult {\n57\t  const { skipDuplicates = true, onConflict = \"ignore\" } = options;\n58\t  const errors: string[] = [];\n59\t  let inserted = 0;\n60\t  let skipped = 0;\n61\t\n62\t  if (data.length === 0) {\n63\t    return { table, inserted: 0, skipped: 0, errors: [] };\n64\t  }\n65\t\n66\t  // Validate table name to prevent SQL injection\n67\t  const validatedTable = validateTableName(table);\n68\t\n69\t  // Build column names from first data object\n70\t  const columns = return {\n76\t      table,\n77\t      inserted: 0,\n78\t      skipped: 0,\n79\t      errors: [`Invalid column names: ${columnValidation.invalid.join(\", \")}`],\n80\t    };\n81\t  }\n82\t\n83\t  const placeholders = columns.map(() => \"?\").join(\", \");\n84\t  const columnList = buildColumnList(columns);\n85\t\n86\t  // Prepare appropriate statement based on conflict handling\n87\t  let sql: string;\n88\t  switch (onConflict) {\n89\t    case \"replace\":\n90\t      sql = `INSERT OR REPLACE INTO ${validatedTable} (${columnList}) VALUES (${placeholders})`;\n91\t      break;\n92\t    case \"update\":\n93\t      // For UPDATE, we need to use INSERT OR REPLACE with a different approach\n94\t      // SQLite doesn't have ON CONFLICT UPDATE in all versions\n95\t      sql = `INSERT OR REPLACE INTO ${validatedTable} (${columnList}) VALUES (${placeholders})`;\n96\t      break;\n97\t    case \"ignore\":\n98\t    default:\n99\t      sql = `INSERT OR IGNORE INTO ${validatedTable} (${columnList}) VALUES (${placeholders})`;\n100\t      break;\n101\t  }\n102\t\n103\t  const stmt = db.prepare(sql);\n104\t\n105\t  for (const row of data) {\n106\t    try {\n107\t      const values = columns.map((col) => row[col as keyof T] as ParameterType);\n108\t      const result = stmt.run(...values);\n109\t\n110\t      if (result.changes > 0) {\n111\t        inserted++;\n112\t      } else if (skipDuplicates) {\n113\t        skipped++;\n114\t      }\n115\t    } catch (err) {\n116\t      const error = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);\n117\t      errors.push(`Row ${JSON.stringify(row)}: ${error}`);\n118\t    }\n119\t  }\n120\t\n121\t  return { table, inserted, skipped, errors };\n122\t}\n123\t\n124\t/**\n125\t * Seed reference data (static lookup tables).\n126\t *\n127\t * Reference data is typically small, static datasets like\n128\t * status codes, categories, or configuration options.\n129\t *\n130\t * @param db - Database instance\n131\t * @param seeds - Array of seed data entries\n132\t * @returns Array of seed results\n133\t */\n134\texport function seedReferenceData(db: Database.Database, seeds: SeedData[]): SeedResult[] {\n135\t  const results: SeedResult[] = [];\n136\t\n137\t  for (const seed of seeds) {\n138\t    const result = seedData(db, seed.table, seed.data, {\n139\t      skipDuplicates: seed.skipDuplicates ?? true,\n140\t      uniqueKeys: seed.uniqueKeys,\n141\t    });\n142\t    results.push(result);\n143\t  }\n144\t\n145\t  return results;\n146\t}\n147\t\n148\t/**\n149\t * Check if reference data needs seeding.\n150\t *\n151\t * @param db - Database instance\n152\t * @param table - Table name\n153\t * @param minCount - Minimum expected rows for a \"seeded\" table\n154\t * @returns True if table needs seeding\n155\t */\n156\texport function needsSeeding(db: Database.Database, table: string, minCount = 1): boolean {\n157\t  try {\n158\t    const validatedTable = validateTableName(table);\n159\t    const result = db.prepare(`SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM ${validatedTable}`).get() as\n160\t      | { count: number }\n161\t      | undefined;\n162\t    return !result || result.count < minCount;\n163\t  } catch {\n164\t    return true;\n165\t  }\n166\t}\n167\t\n168\t/**\n169\t * Upsert single row (insert or update if exists).\n170\t *\n171\t * @param db - Database instance\n172\t * @param table - Table name\n173\t * @param data - Data object to insert/update\n174\t * @param uniqueColumns - Columns that determine uniqueness\n175\t * @returns True if row was inserted or updated\n176\t */\n177\texport function upsertRow(\n178\t  db: Database.Database,\n179\t  table: string,\n180\t  data: Record<string, unknown>,\n181\t  uniqueColumns: string[]\n182\t): boolean {\n183\t  // Validate table name\n184\t  const validatedTable = validateTableName(table);\n185\t\n186\t  // Validate all column names\n187\t  const columns = Object.keys(data);\n188\t  const columnValidation = validateSqlIdentifiers(columns);\n189\t  if (!columnValidation.valid) {\n190\t    throw new Error(`Invalid column names: ${columnValidation.invalid.join(\", \")}`);\n191\t  }\n192\t\n193\t  // Validate unique column names\n194\t  const uniqueColumnValidation = validateSqlIdentifiers(uniqueColumns);\n195\t  if (!uniqueColumnValidation.valid) {\n196\t    throw new Error(`Invalid unique column names: ${uniqueColumnValidation.invalid.join(\", \")}`);\n197\t  }\n198\t\n199\t  const values = columns.map((col) => data[col]);\n200\t  const placeholders = columns.map(() => \"?\").join(\", \");\n201\t  const columnList = buildColumnList(columns);\n202\t\n203\t  // Build WHERE clause for unique columns\n204\t  const whereClause = uniqueColumns.map((col) => `${validateColumnName(col)} = ?`).join(\" AND \");\n205\t  const whereValues = uniqueColumns.map((col) => data[col]);\n206\t\n207\t  // Check if row exists\n208\t  const existsStmt = db.prepare(`SELECT 1 FROM ${validatedTable} WHERE ${whereClause}`);\n209\t  const exists = existsStmt.get(...whereValues);\n210\t\n211\t  if (exists) {\n212\t    // Update existing row\n213\t    const updateClause = columns\n214\t      .filter((col) => !uniqueColumns.includes(col))\n215\t      .map((col) => `${validateColumnName(col)} = ?`)\n216\t      .join(\", \");\n217\t\n218\t    if * Migration data seeding support.\n *\n * Provides utilities for seeding initial data during migrations,\n * including support for idempotent operations, reference data,\n * and test data generation.\n */\n\nimport type Database from \"better-sqlite3\";\nimport {\n  buildColumnList,\n  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars\n  buildPlaceholderList,\n  validateColumnName,\n  validateSqlIdentifiers,\n  validateTableName,\n} from \"./sql-validator.js\";\n\n/** SQLite parameter type */\ntype ParameterType = string | number | boolean | Buffer | null;\n\n/** Seed data entry */\nexport interface SeedData<T = Record<string, unknown>> {\n  table: string;\n  data: T[];\n  uniqueKeys?: string[]; // Columns that define uniqueness\n  skipDuplicates?: boolean; // Skip rows that would violate constraints\n}\n\n/** Seed operation result */\nexport interface SeedResult {\n  table: string;\n  inserted: number;\n  skipped: number;\n  errors: string[];\n}\n\n/**\n * Seed data into a table with conflict handling.\n *\n * @param db - Database instance\n * @param table - Table name\n * @param data - Array of data objects to insert\n * @param options - Seeding options\n * @returns Seed result with counts\n */\nexport function seedData<T extends Record<string, unknown>>(\n  db: Database.Database,\n  table: string,\n  data: T[],\n  options: {\n    skipDuplicates?: boolean;\n    uniqueKeys?: string[];\n    onConflict?: \"ignore\" | \"replace\" | \"update\";\n  } = {}\n): SeedResult {\n  const { skipDuplicates = true, onConflict = \"ignore\" } = options;\n  const errors: string[] = [];\n  let inserted = 0;\n  let skipped = 0;\n\n  if (data.length === 0) {\n    return { table, inserted: 0, skipped: 0, errors: [] };\n  }\n\n  // Validate table name to prevent SQL injection\n  const validatedTable = validateTableName(table);\n\n  // Build column names from first data object\n  const columns = Object.keys(data[0]);\n\n  // Validate all column names to prevent SQL injection\n  const columnValidation = validateSqlIdentifiers(columns);\n  if (!columnValidation.valid) {\n    return {\n      table,\n      inserted: 0,\n      skipped: 0,\n      errors: [`Invalid column names: ${columnValidation.invalid.join(\", \")}`],\n    };\n  }\n\n  const placeholders = columns.map(() => \"?\").join(\", \");\n  const columnList = buildColumnList(columns);\n\n  // Prepare appropriate statement based on conflict handling\n  let sql: string;\n  switch (onConflict) {\n    case \"replace\":\n      sql = `INSERT OR REPLACE INTO ${validatedTable} (${columnList}) VALUES (${placeholders})`;\n      break;\n    case \"update\":\n      // For UPDATE, we need to use INSERT OR REPLACE with a different approach\n      // SQLite doesn't have ON CONFLICT UPDATE in all versions\n      sql = `INSERT OR REPLACE INTO ${validatedTable} (${columnList}) VALUES (${placeholders})`;\n      break;\n    case \"ignore\":\n    default:\n      sql = `INSERT OR IGNORE INTO ${validatedTable} (${columnList}) VALUES (${placeholders})`;\n      break;\n  }\n\n  const stmt = db.prepare(sql);\n\n  for (const row of data) {\n    try {\n      const values = columns.map((col) => row[col as keyof T] as ParameterType);\n      const result = stmt.run(...values);\n\n      if (result.changes > 0) {\n        inserted++;\n      } else if (skipDuplicates) {\n        skipped++;\n      }\n    } catch (err) {\n      const error = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);\n      errors.push(`Row ${JSON.stringify(row)}: ${error}`);\n    }\n  }\n\n  return { table, inserted, skipped, errors };\n}\n\n/**\n * Seed reference data (static lookup tables).\n *\n * Reference data is typically small, static datasets like\n * status codes, categories, or configuration options.\n *\n * @param db - Database instance\n * @param seeds - Array of seed data entries\n * @returns Array of seed results\n */\nexport function seedReferenceData(db: Database.Database, seeds: SeedData[]): SeedResult[] {\n  const results: SeedResult[] = [];\n\n  for (const seed of seeds) {\n    const result = seedData(db, seed.table, seed.data, {\n      skipDuplicates: seed.skipDuplicates ?? true,\n      uniqueKeys: seed.uniqueKeys,\n    });\n    results.push(result);\n  }\n\n  return results;\n}\n\n/**\n * Check if reference data needs seeding.\n *\n * @param db - Database instance\n * @param table - Table name\n * @param minCount - Minimum expected rows for a \"seeded\" table\n * @returns True if table needs seeding\n */\nexport function needsSeeding(db: Database.Database, table: string, minCount = 1): boolean {\n  try {\n    const validatedTable = validateTableName(table);\n    const result = db.prepare(`SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM ${validatedTable}`).get() as\n      | { count: number }\n      | undefined;\n    return !result || result.count < minCount;\n  } catch {\n    return true;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Upsert single row (insert or update if exists).\n *\n * @param db - Database instance\n * @param table - Table name\n * @param data - Data object to insert/update\n * @param uniqueColumns - Columns that determine uniqueness\n * @returns True if row was inserted or updated\n */\nexport function upsertRow(\n  db: Database.Database,\n  table: string,\n  data: Record<string, unknown>,\n  uniqueColumns: string[]\n): boolean {\n  // Validate table name\n  const validatedTable = validateTableName(table);\n\n  // Validate all column names\n  const columns = Object.keys(data);\n  const columnValidation = validateSqlIdentifiers(columns);\n  if (!columnValidation.valid) {\n    throw new Error(`Invalid column names: ${columnValidation.invalid.join(\", \")}`);\n  }\n\n  // Validate unique column names\n  const uniqueColumnValidation = validateSqlIdentifiers(uniqueColumns);\n  if (!uniqueColumnValidation.valid) {\n    throw new Error(`Invalid unique column names: ${uniqueColumnValidation.invalid.join(\", \")}`);\n  }\n\n  const values = columns.map((col) => data[col]);\n  const placeholders = columns.map(() => \"?\").join(\", \");\n  const columnList = buildColumnList(columns);\n\n  // Build WHERE clause for unique columns\n  const whereClause = uniqueColumns.map((col) => `${validateColumnName(col)} = ?`).join(\" AND \");\n  const whereValues = uniqueColumns.map((col) => data[col]);\n\n  // Check if row exists\n  const existsStmt = db.prepare(`SELECT 1 FROM ${validatedTable} WHERE ${whereClause}`);\n  const exists = existsStmt.get(...whereValues);\n\n  if (exists) {\n    // Update existing row\n    const updateClause = columns\n      .filter((col) => !uniqueColumns.includes(col))\n      .map((col) => `${validateColumnName(col)} = ?`)\n      .join(\", \");\n\n    if (updateClause) {\n      const updateValues = columns\n        .filter((col) => !uniqueColumns.includes(col))\n        .map((col) => data[col]);\n\n      const updateStmt = db.prepare(\n        `UPDATE ${validatedTable} SET ${updateClause} WHERE ${whereClause}`\n      );\n      updateStmt.run(...updateValues, ...whereValues);\n    }\n    return true;\n  } else {\n    // Insert new row\n    const insertStmt = db.prepare(\n      `INSERT INTO ${validatedTable} (${columnList}) VALUES (${placeholders})`\n    );\n    const result = insertStmt.run(...values);\n    return result.changes > 0;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Seed configuration data (key-value pairs).\n *\n * Creates a simple key-value configuration table if it doesn't exist\n * and seeds it with initial configuration.\n *\n * @param db - Database instance\n * @param tableName - Name of the configuration table\n * @param config - Configuration object to seed\n * @param options - Table creation options\n * @returns Seed result\n */\nexport function seedConfig(\n  db: Database.Database,\n  tableName: string,\n  config: Record<string, string | number | boolean>,\n  options: {\n    keyColumn?: string;\n    valueColumn?: string;\n    createIfNotExists?: boolean;\n  } = {}\n): SeedResult {\n  const { keyColumn = \"name\", valueColumn = \"value\", createIfNotExists = true } = options;\n\n  // Validate table and column names\n  const validatedTableName = validateTableName(tableName);\n  const validatedKeyColumn = validateColumnName(keyColumn);\n  const validatedValueColumn = validateColumnName(valueColumn);\n\n  // Create table if needed\n  if (createIfNotExists) {\n    db.exec(`\n      CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ${validatedTableName} (\n        ${validatedKeyColumn} TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n        ${validatedValueColumn} TEXT NOT NULL,\n        updated_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))\n      )\n    `);\n  }\n\n  // Convert config object to array format\n  const data = Object.entries(config).map(([key, value]) => ({\n    [validatedKeyColumn]: key,\n    [validatedValueColumn]: String(value),\n  }));\n\n  return seedData(db, validatedTableName, data, { skipDuplicates: true });\n}\n\n/**\n * Bulk seed from JSON file content.\n *\n * @param db - Database instance\n * @param table - Table name\n * @param jsonData - JSON string or parsed object\n * @returns Seed result\n */\nexport function seedFromJSON(\n  db: Database.Database,\n  table: string,\n  jsonData: string | Record<string, unknown> | Record<string, unknown>[]\n): SeedResult {\n  let data: Record<string, unknown>[];\n\n  if (typeof jsonData === \"string\") {\n    const parsed = JSON.parse(jsonData);\n    data = Array.isArray(parsed) ? parsed : [parsed];\n  } else if (Array.isArray(jsonData)) {\n    data = jsonData;\n  } else {\n    data = [jsonData];\n  }\n\n  return seedData(db, table, data, { skipDuplicates: true });\n}\n\n/**\n * Generate seed data for testing.\n *\n * Creates mock data based on a template factory function.\n *\n * @param db - Database instance\n * @param table - Table name\n * @param count - Number of rows to generate\n * @param factory - Function that generates data for a row\n * @returns Seed result\n */\nexport function generateSeedData<T extends Record<string, unknown>>(\n  db: Database.Database,\n  table: string,\n  count: number,\n  factory: (index: number) => T\n): SeedResult {\n  const data: T[] = [];\n\n  for (let i = 0; i < count; i++) {\n    data.push(factory(i));\n  }\n\n  return seedData(db, table, data);\n}\n\n/**\n * Transactional seeding with rollback on error.\n *\n * All seeding operations are performed in a single transaction.\n * If any operation fails, all changes are rolled back.\n *\n * @param db - Database instance\n * @param seeds - Array of seed data entries\n * @returns Array of seed results or throws on error\n */\nexport function seedTransaction(db: Database.Database, seeds: SeedData[]): SeedResult[] {\n  return db.transaction(() => {\n    return seedReferenceData(db, seeds);\n  })();\n}\n\n/**\n * Reset and reseed a table (for testing).\n *\n * Drops all data from a table and reseeds it with fresh data.\n *\n * @param db - Database instance\n * @param table - Table name\n * @param data - Data to seed\n * @param options - Options including disable foreign keys\n * @returns Seed result\n */\nexport function reseedTable<T extends Record<string, unknown>>(\n  db: Database.Database,\n  table: string,\n  data: T[],\n  options: { disableForeignKeys?: boolean; resetAutoincrement?: boolean } = {}\n): SeedResult {\n  const { disableForeignKeys = true, resetAutoincrement = true } = options;\n\n  // Validate table name\n  const validatedTable = validateTableName(table);\n\n  return db.transaction(() => {\n    // Disable foreign key constraints if requested\n    if (disableForeignKeys) {\n      db.pragma(\"foreign_keys = OFF\");\n    }\n\n    try {\n      // Clear existing data\n      db.prepare(`DELETE FROM ${validatedTable}`).run();\n\n      // Reset autoincrement if requested\n      if (resetAutoincrement) {\n        db.prepare(`DELETE FROM sqlite_sequence WHERE name=?`).run(validatedTable);\n      }\n\n      // Seed fresh data\n      return seedData(db, validatedTable, data);\n    } finally {\n      // Re-enable foreign keys\n      if (disableForeignKeys) {\n        db.pragma(\"foreign_keys = ON\");\n      }\n    }\n  })();\n}\n","numLines":404,"startLine":1,"totalLines":404}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_77f49f0b01c247ac9527878f","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t/**\n2\t * SQL identifier validation helpers.\n3\t *\n4\t * Provides utilities for validating SQL identifiers (table names, column names)\n5\t * to prevent SQL injection attacks when building dynamic SQL queries.\n6\t *\n7\t * SQLite identifier rules:\n8\t * - Must start with a letter or underscore\n9\t * - Can contain letters, digits, and underscores\n10\t * - Can be quoted with square brackets [], backticks ``, or double quotes \"\"\n11\t * - Reserved keywords cannot be used as identifiers unless quoted\n12\t *\n13\t * This module enforces strict validation to prevent injection attacks.\n14\t */\n15\t\n16\t/**\n17\t * Valid SQL identifier pattern.\n18\t * - Must start with a letter (a-z, A-Z) or underscore (_)\n19\t * - Can contain letters, digits (0-9), and underscores\n20\t * - Maximum length of 128 characters (SQLite default limit)\n21\t *\n22\t * This pattern does NOT allow quoted identifiers or special characters\n23\t * to prevent injection attempts.\n24\t */\n25\tconst SQL_IDENTIFIER_PATTERN = /^[a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]{0,127}$/;\n26\t\n27\t/**\n28\t * SQL reserved keywords that cannot be used as identifiers without quoting.\n29\t * This is a subset of SQLite's reserved keywords for common operations.\n30\t */\n31\tconst SQL_RESERVED_KEYWORDS = new Set([\n32\t  // SQLite keywords\n33\t  \"ABORT\",\n34\t  \"ACTION\",\n35\t  \"ADD\",\n36\t  \"AFTER\",\n37\t  \"ALL\",\n38\t  \"ALTER\",\n39\t  \"ANALYZE\",\n40\t  \"AND\",\n41\t  \"AS\",\n42\t  \"ASC\",\n43\t  \"ATTACH\",\n44\t  \"AUTOINCREMENT\",\n45\t  \"BEFORE\",\n46\t  \"BEGIN\",\n47\t  \"BETWEEN\",\n48\t  \"BY\",\n49\t * SQL identifier validation helpers.\n *\n * Provides utilities for validating SQL identifiers (table names, column names)\n * to prevent SQL injection attacks when building dynamic SQL queries.\n *\n * SQLite identifier rules:\n * - Must start with a letter or underscore\n * - Can contain letters, digits, and underscores\n * - Can be quoted with square brackets [], backticks ``, or double quotes \"\"\n * - Reserved keywords cannot be used as identifiers unless quoted\n *\n * This module enforces strict validation to prevent injection attacks.\n */\n\n/**\n * Valid SQL identifier pattern.\n * - Must start with a letter (a-z, A-Z) or underscore (_)\n * - Can contain letters, digits (0-9), and underscores\n * - Maximum length of 128 characters (SQLite default limit)\n *\n * This pattern does NOT allow quoted identifiers or special characters\n * to prevent injection attempts.\n */\nconst SQL_IDENTIFIER_PATTERN = /^[a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]{0,127}$/;\n\n/**\n * SQL reserved keywords that cannot be used as identifiers without quoting.\n * This is a subset of SQLite's reserved keywords for common operations.\n */\nconst SQL_RESERVED_KEYWORDS = new Set([\n  // SQLite keywords\n  \"ABORT\",\n  \"ACTION\",\n  \"ADD\",\n  \"AFTER\",\n  \"ALL\",\n  \"ALTER\",\n  \"ANALYZE\",\n  \"AND\",\n  \"AS\",\n  \"ASC\",\n  \"ATTACH\",\n  \"AUTOINCREMENT\",\n  \"BEFORE\",\n  \"BEGIN\",\n  \"BETWEEN\",\n  \"BY\",\n  \"CASCADE\",\n  \"CASE\",\n  \"CAST\",\n  \"CHECK\",\n  \"COLLATE\",\n  \"COLUMN\",\n  \"COMMIT\",\n  \"CONFLICT\",\n  \"CONSTRAINT\",\n  \"CREATE\",\n  \"CROSS\",\n  \"CURRENT_DATE\",\n  \"CURRENT_TIME\",\n  \"CURRENT_TIMESTAMP\",\n  \"DATABASE\",\n  \"DEFAULT\",\n  \"DEFERRABLE\",\n  \"DEFERRED\",\n  \"DELETE\",\n  \"DESC\",\n  \"DETACH\",\n  \"DISTINCT\",\n  \"DROP\",\n  \"EACH\",\n  \"ELSE\",\n  \"END\",\n  \"ESCAPE\",\n  \"EXCEPT\",\n  \"EXCLUSIVE\",\n  \"EXISTS\",\n  \"EXPLAIN\",\n  \"FAIL\",\n  \"FOR\",\n  \"FOREIGN\",\n  \"FROM\",\n  \"FULL\",\n  \"GLOB\",\n  \"GROUP\",\n  \"HAVING\",\n  \"IF\",\n  \"IGNORE\",\n  \"IMMEDIATE\",\n  \"IN\",\n  \"INDEX\",\n  \"INDEXED\",\n  \"INITIALLY\",\n  \"INNER\",\n  \"INSERT\",\n  \"INSTEAD\",\n \"REGEXP\",\n  \"REINDEX\",\n  \"RELEASE\",\n  \"RENAME\",\n  \"REPLACE\",\n  \"RESTRICT\",\n  \"RIGHT\",\n  \"ROLLBACK\",\n  \"ROW\",\n  \"ROWS\",\n  \"SAVEPOINT\",\n  \"SELECT\",\n  \"SET\",\n \"VIEW\",\n  \"VIRTUAL\",\n  \"WHEN\",\n  \"WHERE\",\n  \"WITH\",\n  \"WITHOUT\",\n]);\n\n/**\n * Validate a SQL identifier (table name, column name, etc.).\n *\n * @param identifier - The identifier to validate\n * @returns True if the identifier is safe to use in SQL queries\n *\n * @example\n * ```ts\n * if (!validateSqlIdentifier(\"users\")) {\n *   throw new Error(\"Invalid table name\");\n * }\n * ```\n */\nexport function validateSqlIdentifier(identifier: string): boolean {\n  // Check against pattern\n  if (!SQL_IDENTIFIER_PATTERN.test(identifier)) {\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  // Check if it's a reserved keyword (case-insensitive)\n  const upperIdentifier = identifier.toUpperCase();\n  if (SQL_RESERVED_KEYWORDS.has(upperIdentifier)) {\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  // Additional safety checks\n  // - No SQL comments\n  if (identifier.includes(\"--\") || identifier.includes(\"/*\") || identifier.includes(\"*/\")) {\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  // - No semicolons (statement separator)\n  if (identifier.includes(\";\")) {\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  // - No quotes (could be used for injection)\n  if (identifier.includes(\"'\") || identifier.includes('\"') || identifier.includes(\"`\")) {\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  // - No square brackets (SQLite identifier quoting)\n  if (identifier.includes(\"[\") || identifier.includes(\"]\")) {\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  // - No whitespace (could be used to separate tokens)\n  if (/\\s/.test(identifier)) {\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  return true;\n}\n\n/**\n * Validate multiple SQL identifiers.\n *\n * @param identifiers - Array of identifiers to validate\n * @returns Object with validation result and any invalid identifiers\n *\n * @example\n * ```ts\n * const result = validateSqlIdentifiers([\"users\", \"id\", \"name\"]);\n * if (!result.valid) {\n *   throw new Error(`Invalid identifiers: ${result.invalid.join(\", \")}`);\n * }\n * ```\n */\nexport function validateSqlIdentifiers(identifiers: string[]): {\n  valid: boolean;\n  invalid: string[];\n} {\n  const invalid: string[] = [];\n\n  for (const identifier of identifiers) {\n    if (!validateSqlIdentifier(identifier)) {\n      invalid.push(identifier);\n    }\n  }\n\n  return {\n    valid: invalid.length === 0,\n    invalid,\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * Sanitize a SQL identifier by throwing an error if invalid.\n * This is a convenience function for use in code that needs to fail fast.\n *\n * @param identifier - The identifier to validate\n * @param identifierType - Type of identifier (for error messages)\n * @throws Error if the identifier is invalid\n *\n * @example\n * ```ts\n * sanitizeSqlIdentifier(tableName, \"table name\");\n * // Throws Error if invalid with descriptive message\n * ```\n */\nexport function sanitizeSqlIdentifier(\n  identifier: string,\n  identifierType: string = \"identifier\"\n): string {\n  if (!validateSqlIdentifier(identifier)) {\n    throw new Error(\n      `Invalid SQL ${identifierType}: \"${identifier}\". ` +\n        `SQL identifiers must start with a letter or underscore, ` +\n        `contain only letters, digits, and underscores, ` +\n        `and not be a reserved SQL keyword.`\n    );\n  }\n\n  return identifier;\n}\n\n/**\n * Validate a table name.\n * Alias for sanitizeSqlIdentifier with a specific type.\n *\n * @param tableName - The table name to validate\n * @throws Error if the table name is invalid\n */\nexport function validateTableName(tableName: string): string {\n  return sanitizeSqlIdentifier(tableName, \"table name\");\n}\n\n/**\n * Validate a column name.\n * Alias for sanitizeSqlIdentifier with a specific type.\n *\n * @param columnName - The column name to validate\n * @throws Error if the column name is invalid\n */\nexport function validateColumnName(columnName: string): string {\n  return sanitizeSqlIdentifier(columnName, \"column name\");\n}\n\n/**\n * Build a comma-separated list of validated column names.\n * Useful for SELECT queries and INSERT statements.\n *\n * @param columnNames - Array of column names to validate and join\n * @returns Comma-separated list of validated column names\n * @throws Error if any column name is invalid\n *\n * @example\n * ```ts\n * const columns = buildColumnList([\"id\", \"name\", \"email\"]);\n * // Returns: \"id, name, email\"\n * ```\n */\nexport function buildColumnList(columnNames: string[]): string {\n  const validated = columnNames.map((name) => validateColumnName(name));\n  return validated.join(\", \");\n}\n\n/**\n * Build a placeholder list for parameterized queries.\n * Returns a string like \"(?, ?, ?)\" for the given count.\n *\n * @param count - Number of placeholders needed\n * @returns String of comma-separated question marks\n *\n * @example\n * ```ts\n * const placeholders = buildPlaceholderList(3);\n * // Returns: \"(?, ?, ?)\"\n * ```\n */\nexport function buildPlaceholderList(count: number): string {\n  if (count < 0) {\n [];\n350\t\n351\t    for (const validator of validators) {\n352\t      const result = validator(db);\n353\t      errors.push(...result.errors);\n354\t      warnings.push(...result.warnings);\n355\t    }\n356\t\n357\t    return { valid: errors.length === 0, errors, warnings };\n358\t  };\n359\t}\n360\t\n361\t/**\n362\t * Safe data transformation helper.\n363\t *\n364\t * Performs a transformation in each row\n369\t * @param batchSize - Number of rows to process per batch\n370\t * @returns Number of rows transformed\n371\t */\n372\texport function safeTransform(\n373\t  db: Database.Database,\n374\t  tableName: string,\n375\t  transform: (row: Record<string, unknown>) => void,\n376\t  batchSize = 1000\n377\t): number {\n378\t  // Validate table name\n379\t  const }\n384\t\n385\t  // Get primary key column\n386\t  const columns = getTableColumns(db, validatedTableName);\n387\t  const pkColumn = columns.find((c) => c.pk > 0);\n388\t  if (!pkColumn) {\n389\t    throw new Error(`Table '${validatedTableName}' has no primary key`);\n390\t  }\n391\t\n392\t  const count = db.prepare(`SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM ${validatedTableName}`).get() as {\n393\t    count: number;\n394\t  };\n395\t  let transformed = 0;\n396\t\n397\t  // Process in batches\n398\t  for (let offset = 0; offset < count.count; offset += batchSize) {\n399\t    const rows = db\n400\t      .prepare(`SELECT * FROM ${validatedTableName} LIMIT ${batchSize} OFFSET ${offset}`)\n401\t      .all();\n402\t\n403\t    db.transaction(() => {\n404\t      for (const row of rows) {\n405\t        try {\n406\t          transform(row);\n407\t          transformed++;\n408\t        } catch (err) {\n409\t          throw new Error(\n410\t            `Failed to transform row with ${pkColumn.name}=${row[pkColumn.name]}: ${err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)}`\n411\t          );\n412\t        }\n413\t      }\n414\t    })();\n415\t  }\n416\t\n417\t  return * Migration validation helpers.\n *\n * Provides utilities for validating data before and after migrations,\n * including table structure checks, data integrity checks, and safe\n * transformation patterns.\n */\n\nimport type Database from \"better-sqlite3\";\nimport { validateColumnName, validateTableName } from \"./sql-validator.js\";\n\n/** Table column information */\nexport interface ColumnInfo {\n  cid: number;\n  name: string;\n  type: string;\n  notnull: number;\n  dflt_value: string | null;\n  pk: number;\n}\n\n/** Table index information */\nexport interface IndexInfo {\n  name: string;\n  unique: number;\n  origin: string;\n  partial: number;\n}\n\n/** Validation result */\nexport interface ValidationResult {\n  valid: boolean;\n  errors: string[];\n  warnings: string[];\n}\n\n/**\n * Check if a table exists in the database.\n *\n * @param db - Database instance\n * @param tableName - Name of the table to check\n * @returns True if table exists\n */\nexport function tableExists(db: Database.Database, tableName: string): boolean {\n  // Validate table name to prevent SQL injection\n  const validatedTableName = validateTableName(tableName);\n  const result = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name=?\")\n    .get(validatedTableName);\n  return !!result;\n}\n\n/**\n * Get column information for a table.\n *\n * @param db - Database instance\n * @param tableName - Name of the table\n * @returns Array of column information\n */\nexport function getTableColumns(db: Database.Database, tableName: string): ColumnInfo[] {\n  // Validate table name to prevent SQL injection\n  const validatedTableName = validateTableName(tableName);\n  return db.pragma(`table_info(${validatedTableName})`) as ColumnInfo[];\n}\n\n/**\n * Get index information for a table.\n *\n * @param db - Database instance\n * @param tableName - Name of the table\n * @returns Array of index information\n */\nexport function getTableIndexes(db: Database.Database, tableName: string): IndexInfo[] {\n  // Validate table name to prevent SQL injection\n  const validatedTableName = validateTableName(tableName);\n  // PRAGMA index_list returns: seq, name, unique, origin, partial\n  return db.pragma(`index_list(${validatedTableName})`) as IndexInfo[];\n}\n\n/**\n * Check if a column exists in a table.\n *\n * @param db - Database instance\n * @param tableName - Name of the table\n * @param columnName - Name of the column\n * @returns True if column exists\n */\nexport function columnExists(\n  db: Database.Database,\n  tableName: string,\n  columnName: string\n): boolean {\n  // Validate table and column names to prevent SQL injection\n  const validatedTableName = validateTableName(tableName);\n  const validatedColumnName = validateColumnName(columnName);\n  const columns = getTableColumns(db, validatedTableName);\n  return columns.some((col) => col.name === validatedColumnName);\n}\n\n/**\n * Check if an index exists.\n *\n * @param db - Database instance\n * @param indexName - Name of the index\n * @returns True if index exists\n */\nexport function indexExists(db: Database.Database, indexName: string): boolean {\n  const result = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='index' AND name=?\")\n    .get(indexName);\n  return !!result;\n}\n\n/**\n * Validate table has expected columns.\n *\n * @param db - Database instance\n * @param tableName - Name of the table\n * @param expectedColumns - Array of column names that should exist\n * @returns Validation result\n */\nexport function validateTableColumns(\n  db: Database.Database,\n  tableName: string,\n  expectedColumns: string[]\n): ValidationResult {\n  const errors: string[] = [];\n  const warnings: string[] = [];\n\n  // Validate table name\n  const validatedTableName = validateTableName(tableName);\n\n  if (!tableExists(db, validatedTableName)) {\n    errors.push(`Table '${validatedTableName}' does not exist`);\n    return { valid: false, errors, warnings };\n  }\n\n  const columns = getTableColumns(db, validatedTableName);\n  const columnNames = new Set(columns.map((c) => c.name));\n\n  for (const col of expectedColumns) {\n    // Validate each column name\n    const validatedCol = validateColumnName(col);\n    if (!columnNames.has(validatedCol)) {\n      errors.push(`Column '${validatedCol}' does not exist in table '${validatedTableName}'`);\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Check for unexpected columns\n  for (const col of columnNames) {\n    if (!expectedColumns.includes(col) && !col.startsWith(\"_\")) {\n      warnings.push(`Unexpected column '${col}' in table '${validatedTableName}'`);\n    }\n  }\n\n  return { valid: errors.length === 0, errors, warnings };\n}\n\n/**\n * Validate data integrity with a custom check function.\n *\n * @param db - Database instance\n * @param check - Function that performs the validation check\n * @returns Validation result\n */\nexport function validateData(\n  db: Database.Database,\n  check: (db: Database.Database) => { valid: boolean; errors: string[]; warnings?: string[] }\n): ValidationResult {\n  try {\n    const result = check(db);\n    return {\n      valid: result.valid,\n      errors: result.errors,\n      warnings: result.warnings ?? [],\n    };\n  } catch (err) {\n    return {\n      valid: false,\n      errors: [`Validation check failed: ${err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)}`],\n      warnings: [],\n    };\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Check for foreign key violations.\n *\n * @param db - Database instance\n * @returns Array of violation descriptions\n */\nexport function checkForeignKeyViolations(db: Database.Database): string[] {\n  const violations: string[] = [];\n\n  try {\n    const fkResult = db.pragma(\"foreign_key_check\");\n    if (fkResult && fkResult.length > 0) {\n      for (const row of fkResult as Array<Record<string, unknown>>) {\n        violations.push(\n          `FK violation: table=${row.table}, rowid=${row.rowid}, parent=${row.parent}, fkid=${row.fkid}`\n        );\n      }\n    }\n  } catch {\n    // Foreign key checks may not be enabled\n  }\n\n  return violations;\n}\n\n/**\n * Validate row count is within expected range.\n *\n * @param db - Database instance\n * @param tableName - Name of the table\n * @param minRows - Minimum expected rows (inclusive)\n * @param maxRows - Maximum expected rows (inclusive, optional)\n * @returns Validation result\n */\nexport function validateRowCount(\n  db: Database.Database,\n  tableName: string,\n  minRows = 0,\n  maxRows?: number\n): ValidationResult {\n  const errors: string[] = [];\n  const warnings: string[] = [];\n\n  // Validate table name\n  const validatedTableName = validateTableName(tableName);\n\n  if (!tableExists(db, validatedTableName)) {\n    errors.push(`Table '${validatedTableName}' does not exist`);\n    return { valid: false, errors, warnings };\n  }\n\n  const result = db.prepare(`SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM ${validatedTableName}`).get() as {\n    count: number;\n  };\n  const count = result.count;\n\n  if (count < minRows) {\n    errors.push(`Table '${validatedTableName}' has ${count} rows, expected at least ${minRows}`);\n  }\n\n  if (maxRows !== undefined && count > maxRows) {\n    warnings.push(`Table '${validatedTableName}' has ${count} rows, expected at most ${maxRows}`);\n  }\n\n  return { valid: errors.length === 0, errors, warnings };\n}\n\n/**\n * Validate no duplicate values in a column.\n *\n * @param db - Database instance\n * @param tableName - Name of the table\n * @param columnName - Name of the column to check\n * @returns Validation result\n */\nexport function validateNoDuplicates(\n  db: Database.Database,\n  tableName: string,\n  columnName: string\n): ValidationResult {\n  const errors: string[] = [];\n  const warnings: string[] = [];\n\n  // Validate table and column names\n  const validatedTableName = validateTableName(tableName);\n  const validatedColumnName = validateColumnName(columnName);\n\n  if (!tableExists(db, validatedTableName)) {\n    errors.push(`Table '${validatedTableName}' does not exist`);\n    return { valid: false, errors, warnings };\n  }\n\n  if (!columnExists(db, validatedTableName, validatedColumnName)) {\n    errors.push(`Column '${validatedColumnName}' does not exist in table '${validatedTableName}'`);\n    return { valid: false, errors, warnings };\n  }\n\n  const result = db\n    .prepare(\n      `SELECT COUNT(*) - COUNT(DISTINCT ${validatedColumnName}) as duplicates FROM ${validatedTableName} WHERE ${validatedColumnName} IS NOT NULL`\n    )\n    .get() as { duplicates: number };\n\n  if (result.duplicates > 0) {\n    errors.push(\n      `Table '${validatedTableName}' has ${result.duplicates} duplicate values in column '${validatedColumnName}'`\n    );\n  }\n\n  return { valid: errors.length === 0, errors, warnings };\n}\n\n/**\n * Validate all NOT NULL columns have values.\n *\n * @param db - Database instance\n * @param tableName - Name of the table\n * @returns Validation result\n */\nexport function validateNotNullColumns(db: Database.Database, tableName: string): ValidationResult {\n  const errors: string[] = [];\n  const warnings: string[] = [];\n\n  // Validate table name\n  const validatedTableName = validateTableName(tableName);\n\n  if (!tableExists(db, validatedTableName)) {\n    errors.push(`Table '${validatedTableName}' does not exist`);\n    return { valid: false, errors, warnings };\n  }\n\n  const columns = getTableColumns(db, validatedTableName);\n  const notNullColumns = columns.filter((c) => c.notnull === 1 && c.pk === 0);\n\n  for (const col of notNullColumns) {\n    const validatedColName = validateColumnName(col.name);\n    const result = db\n      .prepare(\n        `SELECT COUNT(*) as null_count FROM ${validatedTableName} WHERE ${validatedColName} IS NULL`\n      )\n      .get() as { null_count: number };\n\n    if (result.null_count > 0) {\n      errors.push(\n        `Column '${validatedColName}' in table '${validatedTableName}' has ${result.null_count} NULL values but is NOT NULL`\n      );\n    }\n  }\n\n  return { valid: errors.length === 0, errors, warnings };\n}\n\n/**\n * Create a combined validation from multiple validators.\n *\n * @param validators - Array of validation functions\n * @returns Combined validation function\n */\nexport function combineValidators(\n  ...validators: Array<(db: Database.Database) => ValidationResult>\n): (db: Database.Database) => ValidationResult {\n  return (db: Database.Database) => {\n    const errors: string[] = [];\n    const warnings: string[] = [];\n\n    for (const validator of validators) {\n      const result = validator(db);\n      errors.push(...result.errors);\n      warnings.push(...result.warnings);\n    }\n\n    return { valid: errors.length === 0, errors, warnings };\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * Safe data transformation helper.\n *\n * Performs a transformation in batches with error handling and rollback capability.\n *\n * @param db - Database instance\n * @param tableName - Name of the table\n * @param transform - Function to transform each row\n * @param batchSize - Number of rows to process per batch\n * @returns Number of rows transformed\n */\nexport function safeTransform(\n  db: Database.Database,\n  tableName: string,\n  transform: (row: Record<string, unknown>) => void,\n  batchSize = 1000\n): number {\n  // Validate table name\n  const validatedTableName = validateTableName(tableName);\n\n  if (!tableExists(db, validatedTableName)) {\n    throw new Error(`Table '${validatedTableName}' does not exist`);\n  }\n\n  // Get primary key column\n  const columns = getTableColumns(db, validatedTableName);\n  const pkColumn = columns.find((c) => c.pk > 0);\n  if (!pkColumn) {\n    throw new Error(`Table '${validatedTableName}' has no primary key`);\n  }\n\n  const count = db.prepare(`SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM ${validatedTableName}`).get() as {\n    count: number;\n  };\n  let transformed = 0;\n\n  // Process in batches\n  for (let offset = 0; offset < count.count; offset += batchSize) {\n    const rows = db\n      .prepare(`SELECT * FROM ${validatedTableName} LIMIT ${batchSize} OFFSET ${offset}`)\n      .all();\n\n    db.transaction(() => {\n      for (const row of rows) {\n        try {\n          transform(row);\n          transformed++;\n        } catch (err) {\n          throw new Error(\n            `Failed to transform row with ${pkColumn.name}=${row[pkColumn.name]}: ${err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)}`\n          );\n        }\n      }\n    })();\n  }\n\n  return transformed;\n}\n","numLines":419,"startLine":1,"totalLines":419}}}

Constants;\n}","originalFile":"/**\n * Data migration system with version tracking, rollback, and safety features.\n *\n * Migrations are numbered files in packages/server/src/migration/migrations/\n * Each migration exports:\n * - up(): Function to apply the migration\n * - down(): Function to rollback the migration\n * - description: Human-readable description\n *\n * Features:\n * - Dry-run mode to preview migrations without executing\n * - Pre-migration database backup\n * - Migration locking to prevent concurrent runs\n * - Rollback to specific version\n * - Data validation helpers\n *\n * Usage:\n * ```ts\n * import { runMigrations, rollbackToVersion } from './migration/index.js';\n * await runMigrations(db);\n * await rollbackToVersion(db, 5); // Rollback all migrations after version 5\n * ```\n */\n\nimport { copyFile, mkdir, readdir, unlink } from \"node:fs/promises\";\nimport { constants } from \"node:os\";\nimport type { constants as Constants } from \"node:os\";\nimport { dirname, join } from \"node:path\";\nimport { fileURLToPath } from \"node:url\";\nimport type Database from \"better-sqlite3\";\nimport { logger } from \"../observability/logger.js\";\n\nconst __dirname = dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url));\nconst MIGRATIONS_DIR = join(__dirname, \"migrations\");\nconst BACKUP_DIR = join(__dirname, \"../../data/backups\");\n\n/** Migration interface */\nexport interface Migration {\n  version: number;\n  name: string;\n  description: string;\n  up: (db: Database.Database) => void;\n  down: (db: Database.Database) => void;\n  validate?: (db: Database.Database) => { valid: boolean; errors?: string[] };\n}\n\n/** Migration record in database */\nexport interface MigrationRecord {\n  version: number;\n  name: string;\n  description: string | null;\n  appliedAt: string;\n  executionTimeMs: number;\n}\n\n/** Migration options */\nexport interface MigrationOptions {\n  dryRun?: boolean;\n  backup?: boolean;\n  force?: boolean;\n}\n\n/** Migration result */\nexport interface MigrationResult {\n  version: number;\n  name: string;\n  description: string;\n  applied: boolean;\n  skipped: boolean;\n  executionTimeMs: number;\n  error?: string;\n}\n\n/** Migration lock */\nlet migrationLock = false;\n\n/**\n * Acquire migration lock to prevent concurrent migrations.\n */\nfunction acquireLock(): boolean {\n  if (migrationLock) {\n    return false;\n  }\n  migrationLock = true;\n  return true;\n}\n\n/**\n * Release migration lock.\n */\nfunction releaseLock(): void {\n  migrationLock = false;\n}\n\n/**\n * Initialize migrations table in the database.\n */\nfunction initMigrationsTable(db: Database.Database): void {\n  db.exec(`\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS _migrations (\n      version INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,\n      name TEXT NOT NULL,\n      description TEXT,\n      appliedAt TEXT NOT NULL,\n      executionTimeMs INTEGER NOT NULL\n    )\n  `);\n\n  // Add checksum column for data integrity if not exists\n  try {\n    db.exec(`ALTER TABLE _migrations ADD COLUMN checksum TEXT`);\n  } catch {\n    // Column already exists\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Get all applied migrations from the database.\n */\nfunction getAppliedMigrations(db: Database.Database): Set<number> {\n  const rows = db.prepare(\"SELECT version FROM _migrations ORDER BY version\").all() as Array<{\n    version: number;\n  }>;\n  return new Set(rows.map((r) => r.version));\n}\n\n/**\n * Create a backup of the database file.\n *\n * @param dbPath - Path to the database file\n * @returns Path to the backup file\n */\nexport async function createBackup(dbPath: string): Promise<string> {\n  await mkdir(BACKUP_DIR, { recursive: true });\n\n  const timestamp = new Date().toISOString().replace(/[:.]/g, \"-\");\n  const basename = dbPath.split(\"/\").pop() ?? \"database.db\";\n  const backupPath = join(BACKUP_DIR, `${basename}-${timestamp}.bak`);\n\n  await copyFile(dbPath, backupPath, (constants as typeof Constants).COPYFILE_EXCL);\n\n  logger.info(\"Backup created\", { backupPath });\n\n  return backupPath;\n}\n\n/**\n * List all available backups.\n *\n * @returns Array of backup file information\n */\nexport async function listBackups(): Promise<Array<{ path: string; size: number; created: Date }>> {\n  try {\n    const { readdir } = await import(\"node:fs/promises\");\n    const { stat } = await import(\"node:fs/promises\");\n    const files = await readdir(BACKUP_DIR);\n    const backups: Array<{ path: string; size: number; created: Date }> = [];\n\n    for (const file of files) {\n      if (file.endsWith(\".bak\")) {\n        const fullPath = join(BACKUP_DIR, file);\n        const stats = await stat(fullPath);\n        backups.push({\n          path: fullPath,\n          size: stats.size,\n          created: stats.mtime,\n        });\n      }\n    }\n\n    return backups.sort((a, b) => b.created.getTime() - a.created.getTime());\n  } catch {\n    return [];\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Restore a database from a backup.\n *\n * @param backupPath - Path to the backup file\n * @param targetPath - Path where to restore the database\n */\nexport async function restoreBackup(backupPath: string, targetPath: string): Promise<void> {\n  const { copyFile } = await import(\"node:fs/promises\");\n  await copyFile(backupPath, targetPath);\n\n  logger.info(\"Backup restored\", { backupPath, targetPath });\n}\n\n/**\n * Delete old backups, keeping only the most recent ones.\n *\n * @param keepCount - Number of backups to keep (default: 10)\n */\nexport async function cleanupOldBackups(keepCount: number = 10): Promise<number> {\n  const backups = await listBackups();\n\n  if (backups.length <= keepCount) {\n    return 0;\n  }\n\n  const toDelete = backups.slice(keepCount);\n  let deletedCount = 0;\n\n  for (const backup of toDelete) {\n    try {\n      await unlink(backup.path);\n      deletedCount++;\n    } catch (err) {\n      logger.warn(\"Failed to delete backup\", err instanceof Error ? err : undefined, {\n        backupPath: backup.path,\n      });\n    }\n  }\n\n  if (deletedCount > 0) {\n    logger.info(\"Backups cleaned\", {\n      deletedCount,\n      remainingCount: backups.length - deletedCount,\n    });\n  }\n\n  return deletedCount;\n}\n\n/**\n * Load all migration files from the migrations directory.\n *\n * @param customDir - Optional custom directory to load migrations from\n */\nasync function loadMigrations(customDir?: string): Promise<Migration[]> {\n  const migrations: Migration[] = [];\n  const dir = customDir ?? MIGRATIONS_DIR;\n\n  try {\n    const files = await readdir(dir);\n    const migrationFiles = files.filter((f) => f.endsWith(\".ts\") && !f.endsWith(\".test.ts\"));\n\n    for (const file of migrationFiles) {\n      // Extract version number from filename (e.g., 001-add-users.ts)\n      const match = file.match(/^(\\d+)-(.+)\\.ts$/);\n      if (!match) continue;\n\n      const [, versionStr, name] = match as [string, string, string];\n      const version = parseInt(versionStr, 10);\n\n      try {\n        const module = await import(join(dir, file));\n        if (module.up && module.down && module.description) {\n          migrations.push({\n            version,\n            name,\n            description: module.description,\n            up: module.up,\n            down: module.down,\n            validate: module.validate,\n          });\n        }\n      } catch (err) {\n        logger.error(\"Failed to load migration\", err instanceof Error ? err : undefined, {\n          file,\n        });\n      }\n    }\n  } catch {\n    // Migrations directory doesn't exist yet\n    logger.debug(\"No migrations directory found\");\n  }\n\n  return migrations.sort((a, b) => a.version - b.version);\n}\n\n/**\n * Run all pending migrations.\n *\n * @param db - Better-sqlite3 database instance\n * @param options - Migration options (dryRun, backup, force)\n * @param targetVersion - Optional target version to migrate to\n * @returns Array of migration results\n */\nexport async function runMigrations(\n  db: Database.Database,\n  options: MigrationOptions = {},\n  targetVersion?: number\n): Promise<MigrationResult[]> {\n  // Acquire lock\n  if (!acquireLock()) {\n    throw new Error(\"Migration already in progress\");\n  }\n\n  try {\n    const { dryRun = false, force = false } = options;\n\n    initMigrationsTable(db);\n    const applied = getAppliedMigrations(db);\n    const migrations = await loadMigrations();\n    const results: MigrationResult[] = [];\n\n    for (const migration of migrations) {\n      // Skip if already applied\n      if (applied.has(migration.version)) {\n        results.push({\n          version: migration.version,\n          name: migration.name,\n          description: migration.description,\n          applied: false,\n          skipped: true,\n          executionTimeMs: 0,\n        });\n        continue;\n      }\n\n      // Stop if target version is specified and we've reached it\n      if (targetVersion !== undefined && migration.version > targetVersion) {\n        break;\n      }\n\n      const startTime = Date.now();\n      let validationErrors: string[] | undefined;\n\n      // Run pre-migration validation if available\n      if (migration.validate && !dryRun) {\n        const validation = migration.validate(db);\n        if (!validation.valid) {\n          validationErrors = validation.errors;\n          if (!force) {\n            throw new Error(\n              `Migration ${migration.version} validation failed: ${validationErrors?.join(\", \")}`\n            );\n          }\n        }\n      }\n\n      try {\n        if (dryRun) {\n          // In dry-run mode, just log what would be done\n          logger.info(\"Migration dry run\", {\n            version: migration.version,\n            name: migration.name,\n            description: migration.description,\n            validationErrors,\n          });\n\n          results.push({\n            version: migration.version,\n            name: migration.name,\n            description: migration.description,\n            applied: false,\n            skipped: false,\n            executionTimeMs: 0,\n          });\n        } else {\n          // Run migration within a transaction\n          db.transaction(() => {\n            migration.up(db);\n\n            // Record migration\n            db.prepare(\n              \"INSERT INTO _migrations (version, name, description, appliedAt, executionTimeMs) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\"\n            ).run(\n              migration.version,\n              migration.name,\n              migration.description,\n              new Date().toISOString(),\n              Date.now() - startTime\n            );\n          })();\n\n          results.push({\n            version: migration.version,\n            name: migration.name,\n            description: migration.description,\n            applied: true,\n            skipped: false,\n            executionTimeMs: Date.now() - startTime,\n          });\n\n          logger.info(\"Migration applied\", {\n            version: migration.version,\n            name: migration.name,\n            description: migration.description,\n            executionTimeMs: Date.now() - startTime,\n          });\n        }\n      } catch (err) {\n        const error = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);\n        logger.error(\"Migration failed\", err instanceof Error ? err : undefined, {\n          version: migration.version,\n          name: migration.name,\n          error,\n        });\n\n        results.push({\n          version: migration.version,\n          name: migration.name,\n          description: migration.description,\n          applied: false,\n          skipped: false,\n          executionTimeMs: Date.now() - startTime,\n          error,\n        });\n\n        if (!dryRun) {\n          throw err;\n        }\n      }\n    }\n\n    return results;\n  } finally {\n    releaseLock();\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Rollback a specific migration.\n *\n * @param db - Better-sqlite3 database instance\n * @param version - Migration version to rollback\n * @param options - Migration options (dryRun, backup)\n * @returns True if rolled back, false if not found\n */\nexport async function rollbackMigration(\n  db: Database.Database,\n  version: number,\n  options: MigrationOptions = {}\n): Promise<boolean> {\n  // Acquire lock\n  if (!acquireLock()) {\n    throw new Error(\"Migration already in progress\");\n  }\n\n  try {\n    const { dryRun = false } = options;\n\n    initMigrationsTable(db);\n    const applied = getAppliedMigrations(db);\n\n    if (!applied.has(version)) {\n      logger.warn(\"Rollback: migration not applied\", { version });\n      return false;\n    }\n\n    const migrations = await loadMigrations();\n    const migration = migrations.find((m) => m.version === version);\n\n    if (!migration) {\n      logger.error(\"Rollback: migration file not found\", undefined, { version });\n      return false;\n    }\n\n    const startTime = Date.now();\n\n    if (dryRun) {\n      logger.info(\"Rollback dry run\", {\n        version: migration.version,\n        name: migration.name,\n      });\n      return true;\n    }\n\n    try {\n      db.transaction(() => {\n        // Run rollback\n        migration.down(db);\n\n        // Remove from migrations table\n        db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM _migrations WHERE version = ?\").run(version);\n      })();\n\n      logger.info(\"Migration rolled back\", {\n        version: migration.version,\n        name: migration.name,\n        executionTimeMs: Date.now() - startTime,\n      });\n\n      return true;\n    } catch (err) {\n      logger.error(\"Rollback failed\", err instanceof Error ? err : undefined, {\n        version: migration.version,\n        name: migration.name,\n      });\n      throw err;\n    }\n  } finally {\n    releaseLock();\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Rollback all migrations after a specific version.\n *\n * @param db - Better-sqlite3 database instance\n * @param targetVersion - Target version to rollback to (all migrations after this will be rolled back)\n * @param options - Migration options (dryRun, backup)\n * @returns Array of rolled back migration versions\n */\nexport async function rollbackToVersion(\n  db: Database.Database,\n  targetVersion: number,\n  options: MigrationOptions = {}\n): Promise<number[]> {\n  // Acquire lock\n  if (!acquireLock()) {\n    throw new Error(\"Migration already in progress\");\n  }\n\n  try {\n    const { dryRun = false } = options;\n\n    initMigrationsTable(db);\n    const applied = db\n      .prepare(\"SELECT version FROM _migrations ORDER BY version DESC\")\n      .all() as Array<{ version: number }>;\n\n    // Find all migrations to rollback (those greater than targetVersion)\n    const toRollback = applied.filter((m) => m.version > targetVersion);\n\n    if (toRollback.length === 0) {\n      logger.info(\"Rollback to version: no migrations to rollback\", {\n        targetVersion,\n      });\n      return [];\n    }\n\n    if (dryRun) {\n      logger.info(\"Rollback to version dry run\", {\n        targetVersion,\n        migrationsToRollback: toRollback.map((m) => m.version),\n      });\n      return toRollback.map((m) => m.version);\n    }\n\n    const rolledBack: number[] = [];\n\n    // Rollback migrations in reverse order (highest version first)\n    for (const { version } of toRollback.sort((a, b) => b.version - a.version)) {\n      const success = await rollbackMigration(db, version, { dryRun: false });\n      if (success) {\n        rolledBack.push(version);\n      } else {\n        throw new Error(`Failed to rollback migration ${version}`);\n      }\n    }\n\n    logger.info(\"Rollback to version complete\", {\n      targetVersion,\n      rolledBackVersions: rolledBack,\n    });\n\n    return rolledBack;\n  } finally {\n    releaseLock();\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Get migration status.\n *\n * @param db - Better-sqlite3 database instance\n * @returns Applied and pending migrations\n */\nexport async function getMigrationStatus(db: Database.Database): Promise<{\n  applied: MigrationRecord[];\n  pending: Migration[];\n  currentVersion: number;\n  latestVersion: number;\n}> {\n  initMigrationsTable(db);\n  const applied = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM _migrations ORDER BY version\")\n    .all() as MigrationRecord[];\n\n  const migrations = await loadMigrations();\n  const appliedVersions = new Set(applied.map((a) => a.version));\n  const pending = migrations.filter((m) => !appliedVersions.has(m.version));\n\n  const currentVersion = applied.length > 0 ? Math.max(...applied.map((a) => a.version)) : 0;\n  const latestVersion = migrations.length > 0 ? Math.max(...migrations.map((m) => m.version)) : 0;\n\n  return { applied, pending, currentVersion, latestVersion };\n}\n\n/**\n * Create a new migration file.\n *\n * @param name - Migration name (kebab-case)\n * @param description - Human-readable description\n * @param includeValidation - Whether to include validation template\n * @param migrationsDir - Optional custom migrations directory (for testing)\n * @returns Path to the created migration file\n */\nexport async function createMigration(\n  name: string,\n  description: string,\n  includeValidation = false,\n  migrationsDir = MIGRATIONS_DIR\n): Promise<string> {\n  const migrations = await loadMigrations(migrationsDir);\n  const nextVersion = migrations.length > 0 ? Math.max(...migrations.map((m) => m.version)) + 1 : 1;\n  const filename = `${String(nextVersion).padStart(3, \"0\")}-${name}.ts`;\n  const filepath = join(migrationsDir, filename);\n\n  const validationTemplate = includeValidation\n    ? `\n/**\n * Validate migration preconditions.\n *\n * @param db - Better-sqlite3 database instance\n * @returns Validation result with any errors\n */\nexport function validate(db: Database.Database): { valid: boolean; errors?: string[] } {\n  const errors: string[] = [];\n\n  // TODO: Add validation logic (e.g., check data integrity, foreign keys, etc.)\n  // Example:\n  // const tableExists = db.prepare(\n  //   \"SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name=?\"\n  // ).get(\"existing_table\");\n  // if (!tableExists) {\n  //   errors.push(\"Required table 'existing_table' does not exist\");\n  // }\n\n  return {\n    valid: errors.length === 0,\n    errors: errors.length > 0 ? errors : undefined,\n  };\n}\n`\n    : \"\";\n\n  const template = `/**\n * Migration: ${description}\n */\n\nimport type Database from \"better-sqlite3\";\n\nexport const description = \"${description}\";\n\n/**\n * Apply the migration.\n *\n * @param db - Better-sqlite3 database instance\n */\nexport function up(db: Database.Database): void {\n  // TODO: Implement migration up\n  // Use db.transaction(() => { ... }) for atomic operations\n}\n${validationTemplate}\n/**\n * Rollback the migration.\n *\n * @param db - Better-sqlite3 database instance\n */\nexport function down(db: Database.Database): void {\n  // TODO: Implement migration down\n  // This should reverse all changes made in up()\n}\n`;\n\n  // Create migrations directory if it doesn't exist\n  await mkdir(migrationsDir, { recursive: true });\n  const { writeFile } = await import(\"node:fs/promises\");\n  await writeFile(filepath, template, \"utf-8\");\n\n  logger.info(\"Migration created\", {\n    filepath,\n    version: nextVersion,\n    name,\n  });\n\n  return filepath;\n}\n\n/**\n * Validate migration data integrity.\n *\n * @param db - Better-sqlite3 database instance\n * @returns Validation result\n */\nexport function validateDatabase(db: Database.Database): {\n  valid: boolean;\n  errors: string[];\n  warnings: string[];\n} {\n  const errors: string[] = [];\n  const warnings: string[] = [];\n\n  try {\n    // Check for foreign key violations\n    const fkResult = db.pragma(\"foreign_key_check\") as Array<Record<string, unknown>> | undefined;\n    if (fkResult && fkResult.length > 0) {\n      errors.push(`Foreign key violations found: ${JSON.stringify(fkResult)}`);\n    }\n\n    // Check for tables without primary keys\n    const tables = db\n      .prepare(\"SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name NOT LIKE '__%'\")\n      .all() as Array<{ name: string }>;\n\n    for (const { name } of tables) {\n      const pkInfo = db.pragma(`table_info(${name})`) as Array<{ pk: number }>;\n      const hasPK = pkInfo.some((col) => col.pk > 0);\n\n      if (!hasPK) {\n        warnings.push(`Table '${name}' has no primary key`);\n      }\n    }\n\n    // Check migration table integrity\n    initMigrationsTable(db);\n    const migrations = db\n      .prepare(\"SELECT version FROM _migrations ORDER BY version\")\n      .all() as Array<{\n      version: number;\n    }>;\n\n    // Check for gaps in migration versions\n    for (let i = 1; i < migrations.length; i++) {\n      const current = migrations[i];\n      const previous = migrations[i -
