constants: typeof Constants;\ntry {\n  constants = require(\"node:os\").constants;\n} catch {\n  // Fallback for environments where require is not available\n  constants = {} as typeof Constants;\n}\nimport { dirname, join } from \"node:path\";\nimport { fileURLToPath } from \"node:url\";\nimport type Database from \"better-sqlite3\";\nimport { logger } from \"../observability/logger.js\";\n\nconst __dirname = dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url));\nconst MIGRATIONS_DIR = join(__dirname, \"migrations\");\nconst BACKUP_DIR = join(__dirname, \"../../data/backups\");\n\n/** Migration interface */\nexport interface Migration {\n  version: number;\n  name: string;\n  description: string;\n  up: (db: Database.Database) => void;\n  down: (db: Database.Database) => void;\n  validate?: (db: Database.Database) => { valid: boolean; errors?: string[] };\n}\n\n/** Migration record in database */\nexport interface MigrationRecord {\n  version: number;\n  name: string;\n  description: string | null;\n  appliedAt: string;\n  executionTimeMs: number;\n}\n\n/** Migration options */\nexport interface MigrationOptions {\n  dryRun?: boolean;\n  backup?: boolean;\n  force?: boolean;\n}\n\n/** Migration result */\nexport interface MigrationResult {\n  version: number;\n  name: string;\n  description: string;\n  applied: boolean;\n  skipped: boolean;\n  executionTimeMs: number;\n  error?: string;\n}\n\n/** Migration lock */\nlet migrationLock = false;\n\n/**\n * Acquire migration lock to prevent concurrent migrations.\n */\nfunction acquireLock(): boolean {\n  if (migrationLock) {\n    return false;\n  }\n  migrationLock = true;\n  return true;\n}\n\n/**\n * Release migration lock.\n */\nfunction releaseLock(): void {\n  migrationLock = false;\n}\n\n/**\n * Initialize migrations table in the database.\n */\nfunction initMigrationsTable(db: Database.Database): void {\n  db.exec(`\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS _migrations (\n      version INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,\n      name TEXT NOT NULL,\n      description TEXT,\n      appliedAt TEXT NOT NULL,\n      executionTimeMs INTEGER NOT NULL\n    )\n  `);\n\n  // Add checksum column for data integrity if not exists\n  try {\n    db.exec(`ALTER TABLE _migrations ADD COLUMN checksum TEXT`);\n  } catch {\n    // Column already exists\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Get all applied migrations from the database.\n */\nfunction getAppliedMigrations(db: Database.Database): Set<number> {\n  const rows = db.prepare(\"SELECT version FROM _migrations ORDER BY version\").all() as Array<{\n    version: number;\n  }>;\n  return new Set(rows.map((r) => r.version));\n}\n\n/**\n * Create a backup of the database file.\n *\n * @param dbPath - Path to the database file\n * @returns Path to the backup file\n */\nexport async function createBackup(dbPath: string): Promise<string> {\n  await mkdir(BACKUP_DIR, { recursive: true });\n\n  const timestamp = new Date().toISOString().replace(/[:.]/g, \"-\");\n  const basename = dbPath.split(\"/\").pop() ?? \"database.db\";\n  const backupPath = join(BACKUP_DIR, `${basename}-${timestamp}.bak`);\n\n  await copyFile(dbPath, backupPath, (constants as typeof Constants).COPYFILE_EXCL);\n\n  logger.info(\"Backup created\", { backupPath });\n\n  return backupPath;\n}\n\n/**\n * List all available backups.\n *\n * @returns Array of backup file information\n */\nexport async function listBackups(): Promise<Array<{ path: string; size: number; created: Date }>> {\n  try {\n    const { readdir } = await import(\"node:fs/promises\");\n    const { stat } = await import(\"node:fs/promises\");\n    const files = await readdir(BACKUP_DIR);\n    const backups: Array<{ path: string; size: number; created: Date }> = [];\n\n    for (const file of files) {\n      if (file.endsWith(\".bak\")) {\n        const fullPath = join(BACKUP_DIR, file);\n        const stats = await stat(fullPath);\n        backups.push({\n          path: fullPath,\n          size: stats.size,\n          created: stats.mtime,\n        });\n      }\n    }\n\n    return backups.sort((a, b) => b.created.getTime() - a.created.getTime());\n  } catch {\n    return [];\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Restore a database from a backup.\n *\n * @param backupPath - Path to the backup file\n * @param targetPath - Path where to restore the database\n */\nexport async function restoreBackup(backupPath: string, targetPath: string): Promise<void> {\n  const { copyFile } = await import(\"node:fs/promises\");\n  await copyFile(backupPath, targetPath);\n\n  logger.info(\"Backup restored\", { backupPath, targetPath });\n}\n\n/**\n * Delete old backups, keeping only the most recent ones.\n *\n * @param keepCount - Number of backups to keep (default: 10)\n */\nexport async function cleanupOldBackups(keepCount: number = 10): Promise<number> {\n  const backups = await listBackups();\n\n  if (backups.length <= keepCount) {\n    return 0;\n  }\n\n  const toDelete = backups.slice(keepCount);\n  let deletedCount = 0;\n\n  for (const backup of toDelete) {\n    try {\n      await unlink(backup.path);\n      deletedCount++;\n    } catch (err) {\n      logger.warn(\"Failed to delete backup\", err instanceof Error ? err : undefined, {\n        backupPath: backup.path,\n      });\n    }\n  }\n\n  if (deletedCount > 0) {\n    logger.info(\"Backups cleaned\", {\n      deletedCount,\n      remainingCount: backups.length - deletedCount,\n    });\n  }\n\n  return deletedCount;\n}\n\n/**\n * Load all migration files from the migrations directory.\n *\n * @param customDir - Optional custom directory to load migrations from\n */\nasync function loadMigrations(customDir?: string): Promise<Migration[]> {\n  const migrations: Migration[] = [];\n  const dir = customDir ?? MIGRATIONS_DIR;\n\n  try {\n    const files = await readdir(dir);\n    const migrationFiles = files.filter((f) => f.endsWith(\".ts\") && !f.endsWith(\".test.ts\"));\n\n    for (const file of migrationFiles) {\n      // Extract version number from filename (e.g., 001-add-users.ts)\n      const match = file.match(/^(\\d+)-(.+)\\.ts$/);\n      if (!match) continue;\n\n      const [, versionStr, name] = match as [string, string, string];\n      const version = parseInt(versionStr, 10);\n\n      try {\n        const module = await import(join(dir, file));\n        if (module.up && module.down && module.description) {\n          migrations.push({\n            version,\n            name,\n            description: module.description,\n            up: module.up,\n            down: module.down,\n            validate: module.validate,\n          });\n        }\n      } catch (err) {\n        logger.error(\"Failed to load migration\", err instanceof Error ? err : undefined, {\n          file,\n        });\n      }\n    }\n  } catch {\n    // Migrations directory doesn't exist yet\n    logger.debug(\"No migrations directory found\");\n  }\n\n  return migrations.sort((a, b) => a.version - b.version);\n}\n\n/**\n * Run all pending migrations.\n *\n * @param db - Better-sqlite3 database instance\n * @param options - Migration options (dryRun, backup, force)\n * @param targetVersion - Optional target version to migrate to\n * @returns Array of migration results\n */\nexport async function runMigrations(\n  db: Database.Database,\n  options: MigrationOptions = {},\n  targetVersion?: number\n): Promise<MigrationResult[]> {\n  // Acquire lock\n  if (!acquireLock()) {\n    throw new Error(\"Migration already in progress\");\n  }\n\n  try {\n    const { dryRun = false, force = false } = options;\n\n    initMigrationsTable(db);\n    const applied = getAppliedMigrations(db);\n    const migrations = await loadMigrations();\n    const results: MigrationResult[] = [];\n\n    for (const migration of migrations) {\n      // Skip if already applied\n      if (applied.has(migration.version)) {\n        results.push({\n          version: migration.version,\n          name: migration.name,\n          description: migration.description,\n          applied: false,\n          skipped: true,\n          executionTimeMs: 0,\n        });\n        continue;\n      }\n\n      // Stop if target version is specified and we've reached it\n      if (targetVersion !== undefined && migration.version > targetVersion) {\n        break;\n      }\n\n      const startTime = Date.now();\n      let validationErrors: string[] | undefined;\n\n      // Run pre-migration validation if available\n      if (migration.validate && !dryRun) {\n        const validation = migration.validate(db);\n        if (!validation.valid) {\n          validationErrors = validation.errors;\n          if (!force) {\n            throw new Error(\n              `Migration ${migration.version} validation failed: ${validationErrors?.join(\", \")}`\n            );\n          }\n        }\n      }\n\n      try {\n        if (dryRun) {\n          // In dry-run mode, just log what would be done\n          logger.info(\"Migration dry run\", {\n            version: migration.version,\n            name: migration.name,\n            description: migration.description,\n            validationErrors,\n          });\n\n          results.push({\n            version: migration.version,\n            name: migration.name,\n            description: migration.description,\n            applied: false,\n            skipped: false,\n            executionTimeMs: 0,\n          });\n        } else {\n          // Run migration within a transaction\n          db.transaction(() => {\n            migration.up(db);\n\n            // Record migration\n            db.prepare(\n              \"INSERT INTO _migrations (version, name, description, appliedAt, executionTimeMs) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\"\n            ).run(\n              migration.version,\n              migration.name,\n              migration.description,\n              new Date().toISOString(),\n              Date.now() - startTime\n            );\n          })();\n\n          results.push({\n            version: migration.version,\n            name: migration.name,\n            description: migration.description,\n            applied: true,\n            skipped: false,\n            executionTimeMs: Date.now() - startTime,\n          });\n\n          logger.info(\"Migration applied\", {\n            version: migration.version,\n            name: migration.name,\n            description: migration.description,\n            executionTimeMs: Date.now() - startTime,\n          });\n        }\n      } catch (err) {\n        const error = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);\n        logger.error(\"Migration failed\", err instanceof Error ? err : undefined, {\n          version: migration.version,\n          name: migration.name,\n          error,\n        });\n\n        results.push({\n          version: migration.version,\n          name: migration.name,\n          description: migration.description,\n          applied: false,\n          skipped: false,\n          executionTimeMs: Date.now() - startTime,\n          error,\n        });\n\n        if (!dryRun) {\n          throw err;\n        }\n      }\n    }\n\n    return results;\n  } finally {\n    releaseLock();\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Rollback a specific migration.\n *\n * @param db - Better-sqlite3 database instance\n * @param version - Migration version to rollback\n * @param options - Migration options (dryRun, backup)\n * @returns True if rolled back, false if not found\n */\nexport async function rollbackMigration(\n  db: Database.Database,\n  version: number,\n  options: MigrationOptions = {}\n): Promise<boolean> {\n  // Acquire lock\n  if (!acquireLock()) {\n    throw new Error(\"Migration already in progress\");\n  }\n\n  try {\n    const { dryRun = false } = options;\n\n    initMigrationsTable(db);\n    const applied = getAppliedMigrations(db);\n\n    if (!applied.has(version)) {\n      logger.warn(\"Rollback: migration not applied\", { version });\n      return false;\n    }\n\n    const migrations = await loadMigrations();\n    const migration = migrations.find((m) => m.version === version);\n\n    if (!migration) {\n      logger.error(\"Rollback: migration file not found\", undefined, { version });\n      return false;\n    }\n\n    const startTime = Date.now();\n\n    if (dryRun) {\n      logger.info(\"Rollback dry run\", {\n        version: migration.version,\n        name: migration.name,\n      });\n      return true;\n    }\n\n    try {\n      db.transaction(() => {\n        // Run rollback\n        migration.down(db);\n\n        // Remove from migrations table\n        db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM _migrations WHERE version = ?\").run(version);\n      })();\n\n      logger.info(\"Migration rolled back\", {\n        version: migration.version,\n        name: migration.name,\n        executionTimeMs: Date.now() - startTime,\n      });\n\n      return true;\n    } catch (err) {\n      logger.error(\"Rollback failed\", err instanceof Error ? err : undefined, {\n        version: migration.version,\n        name: migration.name,\n      });\n      throw err;\n    }\n  } finally {\n    releaseLock();\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Rollback all migrations after a specific version.\n *\n * @param db - Better-sqlite3 database instance\n * @param targetVersion - Target version to rollback to (all migrations after this will be rolled back)\n * @param options - Migration options (dryRun, backup)\n * @returns Array of rolled back migration versions\n */\nexport async function rollbackToVersion(\n  db: Database.Database,\n  targetVersion: number,\n  options: MigrationOptions = {}\n): Promise<number[]> {\n  // Acquire lock\n  if (!acquireLock()) {\n    throw new Error(\"Migration already in progress\");\n  }\n\n  try {\n    const { dryRun = false } = options;\n\n    initMigrationsTable(db);\n    const applied = db\n      .prepare(\"SELECT version FROM _migrations ORDER BY version DESC\")\n      .all() as Array<{ version: number }>;\n\n    // Find all migrations to rollback (those greater than targetVersion)\n    const toRollback = applied.filter((m) => m.version > targetVersion);\n\n    if (toRollback.length === 0) {\n      logger.info(\"Rollback to version: no migrations to rollback\", {\n        targetVersion,\n      });\n      return [];\n    }\n\n    if (dryRun) {\n      logger.info(\"Rollback to version dry run\", {\n        targetVersion,\n        migrationsToRollback: toRollback.map((m) => m.version),\n      });\n toRollback.sort((a, b) => b.version - a.version)) {\n      const success = await rollbackMigration(db, version, { dryRun: false });\n      if (success) {\n        rolledBack.push(version);\n      } else {\n        throw new Error(`Failed to rollback migration ${version}`);\n      }\n    }\n\n    logger.info(\"Rollback to version complete\", {\n      targetVersion,\n      rolledBackVersions: number;\n}> {\n  initMigrationsTable(db);\n  const applied = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM _migrations ORDER BY version\")\n    .all() as MigrationRecord[];\n\n  const migrations = await loadMigrations();\n  const appliedVersions = new Set(applied.map((a) => a.version));\n  const pending = migrations.filter((m) => !appliedVersions.has(m.version));\n\n  const currentVersion = applied.length > 0 ? Math.max(...applied.map((a) => a.version)) : 0;\n  const latestVersion = migrations.length > 0 ? Math.max(...migrations.map((m) => m.version)) : 0;\n\n  return { applied, pending, currentVersion, latestVersion };\n}\n\n/**\n * Create a new migration file.\n *\n * @param name - Migration name (kebab-case)\n * @param description - Human-readable description\n * @param includeValidation - Whether to include validation template\n * @param migrationsDir - Optional custom migrations directory (for testing)\n * @returns Path to the created migration file\n */\nexport async function createMigration(\n  name: string,\n  description: string,\n  includeValidation = false,\n  migrationsDir = MIGRATIONS_DIR\n): Promise<string> {\n  const migrations = await loadMigrations(migrationsDir);\n  const nextVersion = migrations.length > 0 ? executing\n * - Pre-migration database backup\n * - Migration locking to prevent concurrent runs\n * - Rollback to specific version\n * - Data validation helpers\n *\n * Usage:\n * ```ts\n * import { runMigrations, rollbackToVersion } from './migration/index.js';\n * await runMigrations(db);\n * await rollbackToVersion(db, 5); // Rollback all migrations after version 5\n * ```\n */\n\nimport { copyFile, mkdir, readdir, unlink } from \"node:fs/promises\";\nimport type { constants as Constants } from \"node:os\";\n\n// Import constants dynamically for better-sqlite3 compatibility\nlet constants: typeof Constants;\ntry {\n  constants = require(\"node:os\").constants;\n} catch {\n  // Fallback for environments where require is not available\n  constants = {} as typeof Constants;\n}\nimport { dirname, join } from \"node:path\";\nimport { fileURLToPath } from \"node:url\";\nimport type Database from \"better-sqlite3\";\nimport { logger } from \"../observability/logger.js\";\n\nconst __dirname = dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url));\nconst MIGRATIONS_DIR = join(__dirname, \"migrations\");\nconst BACKUP_DIR = join(__dirname, \"../../data/backups\");\n\n/** Migration interface */\nexport interface Migration {\n  version: number;\n  name: string;\n  description: string;\n  up: (db: Database.Database) => void;\n  down: (db: Database.Database) => void;\n  validate?: (db: Database.Database) => { valid: boolean; errors?: string[] };\n}\n\n/** Migration record in database */\nexport interface MigrationRecord {\n  version: number;\n  name: string;\n  description: string | null;\n  appliedAt: string;\n  executionTimeMs: number;\n}\n\n/** Migration options */\nexport interface MigrationOptions {\n  dryRun?: boolean;\n  backup?: boolean;\n  force?: boolean;\n}\n\n/** Migration result */\nexport interface MigrationResult {\n  version: number;\n  name: string;\n  description: string;\n  applied: boolean;\n  skipped: boolean;\n  executionTimeMs: number;\n  error?: string;\n}\n\n/** Migration lock */\nlet migrationLock = false;\n\n/**\n * Acquire migration lock to prevent concurrent migrations.\n */\nfunction acquireLock(): boolean {\n  if (migrationLock) {\n    return false;\n  }\n  migrationLock = true;\n  return true;\n}\n\n/**\n * Release migration lock.\n */\nfunction releaseLock(): void {\n  migrationLock = false;\n}\n\n/**\n * Initialize migrations table in the database.\n */\nfunction initMigrationsTable(db: Database.Database): void {\n  db.exec(`\n    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS _migrations (\n      version INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,\n      name TEXT NOT NULL,\n      description TEXT,\n      appliedAt TEXT NOT NULL,\n      executionTimeMs INTEGER NOT NULL\n    )\n  `);\n\n  // Add checksum column for data integrity if not exists\n  try {\n    db.exec(`ALTER TABLE _migrations ADD COLUMN checksum TEXT`);\n  } catch {\n    // Column already exists\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Get all applied migrations from the database.\n */\nfunction getAppliedMigrations(db: Database.Database): Set<number> {\n  const rows = db.prepare(\"SELECT version FROM _migrations ORDER BY version\").all() as Array<{\n    version: number;\n  }>;\n  return new Set(rows.map((r) => r.version));\n}\n\n/**\n * Create a backup of the database file.\n *\n * @param dbPath - Path to the database file\n * @returns Path to the backup file\n */\nexport async function createBackup(dbPath: string): Promise<string> {\n  await mkdir(BACKUP_DIR, { recursive: true });\n\n  const timestamp = new Date().toISOString().replace(/[:.]/g, \"-\");\n  const basename = dbPath.split(\"/\").pop() ?? \"database.db\";\n  const backupPath = join(BACKUP_DIR, `${basename}-${timestamp}.bak`);\n\n  await copyFile(dbPath, backupPath, constants.COPYFILE_EXCL || 0);\n\n  logger.info(\"Backup created\", { backupPath });\n\n  return backupPath;\n}\n\n/**\n * List all available backups.\n *\n * @returns Array of backup file information\n */\nexport async function listBackups(): Promise<Array<{ path: string; size: number; created: Date }>> {\n  try {\n    const { readdir } = await import(\"node:fs/promises\");\n    const { stat } = await import(\"node:fs/promises\");\n    const files = await readdir(BACKUP_DIR);\n    const backups: Array<{ path: string; size: number; created: Date }> = [];\n\n    for (const file of files) {\n      if (file.endsWith(\".bak\")) {\n        const fullPath = join(BACKUP_DIR, file);\n        const stats = await stat(fullPath);\n        backups.push({\n          path: fullPath,\n          size: stats.size,\n          created: stats.mtime,\n        });\n      }\n    }\n\n    return backups.sort((a, b) => b.created.getTime() - a.created.getTime());\n  } catch {\n    return [];\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Restore a database from a backup.\n *\n * @param backupPath - Path to the backup file\n * @param targetPath - Path where to restore the database\n */\nexport async function restoreBackup(backupPath: string, targetPath: string): Promise<void> {\n  const { copyFile } = await import(\"node:fs/promises\");\n  await copyFile(backupPath, targetPath);\n\n  logger.info(\"Backup restored\", { backupPath, targetPath });\n}\n\n/**\n * Delete old backups, keeping only the most recent ones.\n *\n * @param keepCount - Number of backups to keep (default: 10)\n */\nexport async function cleanupOldBackups(keepCount: number = 10): Promise<number> {\n  const backups = await listBackups();\n\n  if (backups.length <= keepCount) {\n    return 0;\n  }\n\n  const toDelete = backups.slice(keepCount);\n  let deletedCount = 0;\n\n  for (const backup of toDelete) {\n    try {\n      await unlink(backup.path);\n      deletedCount++;\n    } catch (err) {\n      logger.warn(\"Failed to delete backup\", err instanceof Error ? err : undefined, {\n        backupPath: backup.path,\n      });\n    }\n  }\n\n  if (deletedCount > 0) {\n    logger.info(\"Backups cleaned\", {\n      deletedCount,\n      remainingCount: backups.length - deletedCount,\n    });\n  }\n\n  return deletedCount;\n}\n\n/**\n * Load all migration files from the migrations directory.\n *\n * @param customDir - Optional custom directory to load migrations from\n */\nasync function loadMigrations(customDir?: string): Promise<Migration[]> {\n  const migrations: Migration[] = [];\n  const dir = customDir ?? MIGRATIONS_DIR;\n\n  try {\n    const files = await readdir(dir);\n    const migrationFiles = files.filter((f) => f.endsWith(\".ts\") && !f.endsWith(\".test.ts\"));\n\n    for (const file of migrationFiles) {\n      // Extract version number from filename (e.g., 001-add-users.ts)\n      const match = file.match(/^(\\d+)-(.+)\\.ts$/);\n      if (!match) continue;\n\n      const [, versionStr, name] = match as [string, string, string];\n      const version = parseInt(versionStr, 10);\n\n      try {\n        const module = await import(join(dir, file));\n        if (module.up && module.down && module.description) {\n          migrations.push({\n            version,\n            name,\n            description: module.description,\n            up: module.up,\n            down: module.down,\n            validate: module.validate,\n          });\n        }\n      } catch (err) {\n        logger.error(\"Failed to load migration\", err instanceof Error ? err : undefined, {\n          file,\n        });\n      }\n    }\n  } catch {\n    // Migrations directory doesn't exist yet\n    logger.debug(\"No migrations directory found\");\n  }\n\n  return migrations.sort((a, b) => a.version - b.version);\n}\n\n/**\n * Run all pending migrations.\n *\n * @param db - Better-sqlite3 database instance\n * @param options - Migration options (dryRun, backup, force)\n * @param targetVersion - Optional target version to migrate to\n * @returns Array of migration results\n */\nexport async function runMigrations(\n  db: Database.Database,\n  options: MigrationOptions = {},\n  targetVersion?: number\n): Promise<MigrationResult[]> {\n  // Acquire lock\n  if (!acquireLock()) {\n    throw new Error(\"Migration already in progress\");\n  }\n\n  try {\n    const { dryRun = false, force = false } = options;\n\n    initMigrationsTable(db);\n    const applied = getAppliedMigrations(db);\n    const migrations = await loadMigrations();\n    const results: MigrationResult[] = [];\n\n    for (const migration of migrations) {\n      // Skip if already applied\n      if (applied.has(migration.version)) {\n        results.push({\n          version: migration.version,\n          name: migration.name,\n          description: migration.description,\n          applied: false,\n          skipped: true,\n          executionTimeMs: 0,\n        });\n        continue;\n      }\n\n      // Stop if target version is specified and we've reached it\n      if (targetVersion !== undefined && migration.version > targetVersion) {\n        break;\n      }\n\n      const startTime = Date.now();\n      let validationErrors: string[] | undefined;\n\n      // Run pre-migration validation if available\n      if (migration.validate && !dryRun) {\n        const validation = migration.validate(db);\n        if (!validation.valid) {\n          validationErrors = validation.errors;\n          if (!force) {\n            throw new Error(\n              `Migration ${migration.version} validation failed: ${validationErrors?.join(\", \")}`\n            );\n          }\n        }\n      }\n\n      try {\n        if (dryRun) {\n          // In dry-run mode, just log what would be done\n          logger.info(\"Migration dry run\", {\n            version: migration.version,\n            name: migration.name,\n            description: migration.description,\n            validationErrors,\n          });\n\n          results.push({\n            version: migration.version,\n            name: migration.name,\n            description: migration.description,\n            applied: false,\n            skipped: false,\n            executionTimeMs: 0,\n          });\n        } else {\n          // Run migration within a transaction\n          db.transaction(() => {\n            migration.up(db);\n\n            // Record migration\n            db.prepare(\n              \"INSERT INTO _migrations (version, name, description, appliedAt, executionTimeMs) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\"\n            ).run(\n              migration.version,\n              migration.name,\n              migration.description,\n              new Date().toISOString(),\n              Date.now() - startTime\n            );\n          })();\n\n          results.push({\n            version: migration.version,\n            name: migration.name,\n            description: migration.description,\n            applied: true,\n            skipped: false,\n            executionTimeMs: Date.now() - startTime,\n          });\n\n          logger.info(\"Migration applied\", {\n            version: migration.version,\n            name: migration.name,\n            description: migration.description,\n            executionTimeMs: Date.now() - startTime,\n          });\n        }\n      } catch (err) {\n        const error = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);\n        logger.error(\"Migration failed\", err instanceof Error ? err : undefined, {\n          version: migration.version,\n          name: migration.name,\n          error,\n        });\n\n        results.push({\n          version: migration.version,\n          name: migration.name,\n          description: migration.description,\n          applied: false,\n          skipped: false,\n          executionTimeMs: Date.now() - startTime,\n          error,\n        });\n\n        if (!dryRun) {\n          throw err;\n        }\n      }\n    }\n\n    return results;\n  } finally {\n    releaseLock();\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Rollback a specific migration.\n *\n * @param db - Better-sqlite3 database instance\n * @param version - Migration version to rollback\n * @param options - Migration options (dryRun, backup)\n * @returns True if rolled back, false if not found\n */\nexport async function rollbackMigration(\n  db: Database.Database,\n  version: number,\n  options: MigrationOptions = {}\n): Promise<boolean> {\n  // Acquire lock\n  if (!acquireLock()) {\n    throw new Error(\"Migration already in progress\");\n  }\n\n  try {\n    const { dryRun = false } = options;\n\n    initMigrationsTable(db);\n    const applied = getAppliedMigrations(db);\n\n    if (!applied.has(version)) {\n      logger.warn(\"Rollback: migration not applied\", { version });\n      return false;\n    }\n\n    const migrations = await loadMigrations();\n    const migration = migrations.find((m) => m.version === version);\n\n    if (!migration) {\n      logger.error(\"Rollback: migration file not found\", undefined, { version });\n      return false;\n    }\n\n    const startTime = Date.now();\n\n    if (dryRun) {\n      logger.info(\"Rollback dry run\", {\n        version: migration.version,\n        name: migration.name,\n      });\n      return true;\n    }\n\n    try {\n      db.transaction(() => {\n        // Run rollback\n        migration.down(db);\n\n        // Remove from migrations table\n        db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM _migrations WHERE version = ?\").run(version);\n      })();\n\n      logger.info(\"Migration rolled back\", {\n        version: migration.version,\n        name: migration.name,\n        executionTimeMs: Date.now() - startTime,\n      });\n\n      return true;\n    } catch (err) {\n      logger.error(\"Rollback failed\", err instanceof Error ? err : undefined, {\n        version: migration.version,\n        name: migration.name,\n      });\n      throw err;\n    }\n  } finally {\n    releaseLock();\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Rollback all migrations after a specific version.\n *\n * @param db - Better-sqlite3 database instance\n * @param targetVersion - Target version to rollback to (all migrations after this will be rolled back)\n * @param options - Migration options (dryRun, backup)\n * @returns Array of rolled back migration versions\n */\nexport async function rollbackToVersion(\n  db: Database.Database,\n  targetVersion: number,\n  options: MigrationOptions = {}\n): Promise<number[]> {\n  // Acquire lock\n  if (!acquireLock()) {\n    throw new Error(\"Migration already in progress\");\n  }\n\n  try {\n    const { dryRun = false } = options;\n\n    initMigrationsTable(db);\n    const applied = db\n      .prepare(\"SELECT version FROM _migrations ORDER BY version DESC\")\n      .all() as Array<{ version: number }>;\n\n    // Find all migrations to rollback (those greater than targetVersion)\n    const toRollback = applied.filter((m) => m.version > targetVersion);\n\n    if (toRollback.length === 0) {\n      logger.info(\"Rollback to version: no migrations to rollback\", {\n        targetVersion,\n      });\n      return [];\n    }\n\n    if (dryRun) {\n      logger.info(\"Rollback to version dry run\", {\n        targetVersion,\n        migrationsToRollback: toRollback.map((m) => m.version),\n      });\n      return toRollback.map((m) => m.version);\n    }\n\n    const rolledBack: number[] = [];\n\n    // Rollback migrations in reverse order (highest version first)\n    for (const { version } of toRollback.sort((a, b) => b.version - a.version)) {\n      const success = await rollbackMigration(db, version, { dryRun: false });\n      if (success) {\n        rolledBack.push(version);\n      } else {\n        throw new Error(`Failed to rollback migration ${version}`);\n      }\n    }\n\n    logger.info(\"Rollback to version complete\", {\n      targetVersion,\n      rolledBackVersions: pending migrations\n */\nexport async function getMigrationStatus(db: Database.Database): Promise<{\n  applied: MigrationRecord[];\n  pending: Migration[];\n  currentVersion: number;\n  latestVersion: number;\n}> {\n  initMigrationsTable(db);\n  const applied = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM _migrations ORDER BY version\")\n    .all() as MigrationRecord[];\n\n  const migrations = await loadMigrations();\n  const appliedVersions = new Set(applied.map((a) => a.version));\n  const pending = migrations.filter((m) => !appliedVersions.has(m.version));\n\n  const currentVersion = applied.length > 0 ? Math.max(...applied.map((a) => a.version)) : 0;\n  const latestVersion = migrations.length > 0 ? Math.max(...migrations.map((m) => m.version)) : 0;\n\n  return { applied, pending, currentVersion, latestVersion };\n}\n\n/**\n * Create a new migration file.\n *\n * @param name - Migration name (kebab-case)\n * @param description - Human-readable description\n * @param includeValidation - Whether to include validation template\n * @param migrationsDir - Optional custom migrations directory (for testing)\n * @returns Path to the created migration file\n */\nexport async function createMigration(\n  name: string,\n  description: string,\n  includeValidation = false,\n  migrationsDir = MIGRATIONS_DIR\n): Promise<string> {\n  const migrations = await loadMigrations(migrationsDir);\n  const nextVersion = migrations.length > 0 ? Better-sqlite3 database instance\n */\nexport function down(db: Database.Database): void {\n  // TODO: Implement migration down\n  // This should reverse all changes made in up()\n}\n`;\n\n  // Create migrations directory if it doesn't exist\n  await mkdir(migrationsDir, { recursive: true });\n  const { writeFile } = await import(\"node:fs/promises\");\n  await writeFile(filepath, template, \"utf-8\");\n\n  logger.info(\"Migration created\", {\n    filepath,\n    version: nextVersion,\n    name,\n  });\n\n  return filepath;\n}\n\n/**\n * Validate migration data integrity.\n *\n * @param db - Better-sqlite3 database instance\n * @returns Validation result\n */\nexport function validateDatabase(db: Database.Database): {\n  valid: boolean;\n  errors: string[];\n  warnings: string[];\n} {\n  const errors: string[] = [];\n  const warnings: string[] = [];\n\n  try {\n    // Check for foreign key violations\n    const fkResult = db.pragma(\"foreign_key_check\") as Array<Record<string, unknown>> | undefined;\n    if (fkResult && fkResult.length > 0) {\n      errors.push(`Foreign key violations found: ${JSON.stringify(fkResult)}`);\n    }\n\n    // Check for tables without primary keys\n    const tables = db\n      .prepare(\"SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name NOT LIKE '__%'\")\n      .all() as Array<{ name: string }>;\n\n    for (const { name } of tables) {\n      const pkInfo = db.pragma(`table_info(${name})`) as Array<{ pk: number }>;\n      const hasPK = pkInfo.some((col) => col.pk > 0);\n\n      if (!hasPK) {\n        warnings.push(`Table '${name}' has no primary key`);\n      }\n    }\n\n    // Check migration table integrity\n    initMigrationsTable(db);\n    const migrations = db\n      .prepare(\"SELECT version FROM _migrations ORDER BY version\")\n      .all() as Array<{\n      version: number;\n    }>;\n\n    // Check for gaps in migration versions\n    for (let i = 1; i < migrations.length; i++) {\n      const current = migrations[i];\n      const previous = migrations[i - 1];\n      if (current && previous && current.version !== previous.version + 1) {\n        errors.push(`Gap in migration versions between ${previous.version} and ${current.version}`);\n      }\n    }\n  } catch (err) {\n    errors.push(`Database validation error: ${err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)}`);\n  }\n\n  return {\n    valid: errors.length === 0,\n    errors,\n    warnings,\n  };\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":215,"oldLines":8,"newStart":215,"newLines":9,"lines":["       await unlink(backup.path);","       deletedCount++;","     } catch (err) {","-      logger.warn(\"Failed to delete backup\", err instanceof Error ? err : undefined, {","+      logger.warn(\"Failed to delete backup\", {","         backupPath: backup.path,","+        error: err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err),","       });","     }","   }"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

initial data during migrations,\n * including support for idempotent operations, reference data,\n * and test data generation.\n */\n\nimport type Database from \"better-sqlite3\";\nimport {\n  buildColumnList,\n  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars\n  buildPlaceholderList,\n  validateColumnName,\n  validateSqlIdentifiers,\n  validateTableName,\n} from \"./sql-validator.js\";\n\n/** SQLite parameter type */\ntype ParameterType = string | number | boolean | Buffer | null;\n\n/** Seed data entry */\nexport interface SeedData<T = Record<string, unknown>> {\n  table: string;\n  data: T[];\n  uniqueKeys?: string[]; // Columns that define uniqueness\n  skipDuplicates?: boolean; // Skip rows that would violate constraints\n}\n\n/** Seed operation result */\nexport interface SeedResult {\n  table: string;\n  inserted: number;\n  skipped: number;\n  errors: string[];\n}\n\n/**\n * Seed data into a table with conflict handling.\n *\n * @param db - Database instance\n * @param table - Table name\n * @param data - Array of data objects to insert\n * @param options - Seeding options\n * @returns Seed result with counts\n */\nexport function seedData<T extends Record<string, unknown>>(\n  db: Database.Database,\n  table: string,\n  data: T[],\n  options: {\n    skipDuplicates?: boolean;\n    uniqueKeys?: string[];\n    onConflict?: \"ignore\" | \"replace\" | \"update\";\n  } = {}\n): SeedResult {\n  const { skipDuplicates = true, onConflict = \"ignore\" } = options;\n  const errors: string[] = [];\n  let inserted = 0;\n  let skipped = 0;\n\n  if (data.length === 0) {\n    return { table, inserted: 0, skipped: 0, errors: [] };\n  }\n\n  // Validate table name to prevent SQL injection\n  const validatedTable = validateTableName(table);\n\n  // Build column names from first data object\n  const columns = Object.keys(data[0]);\n\n  // Validate all column names to prevent SQL injection\n  const columnValidation = validateSqlIdentifiers(columns);\n  if (!columnValidation.valid) {\n    return {\n      table,\n      inserted: 0,\n      skipped: 0,\n      errors: [`Invalid column names: ${columnValidation.invalid.join(\", \")}`],\n    };\n  }\n\n  const placeholders = columns.map(() => \"?\").join(\", \");\n  const columnList = buildColumnList(columns);\n\n  // Prepare appropriate statement based on conflict handling\n  let sql: string;\n  switch (onConflict) {\n    case \"replace\":\n      sql = `INSERT OR REPLACE INTO ${validatedTable} (${columnList}) VALUES (${placeholders})`;\n      break;\n    case \"update\":\n      // For UPDATE, we need to use INSERT OR REPLACE with a different approach\n      // SQLite doesn't have ON CONFLICT UPDATE in all versions\n      sql = `INSERT OR REPLACE INTO ${validatedTable} (${columnList}) VALUES (${placeholders})`;\n      break;\n    case \"ignore\":\n    default:\n      sql = `INSERT OR IGNORE INTO ${validatedTable} (${columnList}) VALUES (${placeholders})`;\n      break;\n  }\n\n  const stmt = db.prepare(sql);\n\n  for (const row of data) {\n    try {\n      const values = columns.map((col) => row[col as keyof T] as ParameterType);\n      const result = stmt.run(...values);\n\n      if (result.changes > 0) {\n        inserted++;\n      } else if (skipDuplicates) {\n        skipped++;\n      }\n    } catch (err) {\n      const error = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);\n      errors.push(`Row ${JSON.stringify(row)}: ${error}`);\n    }\n  }\n\n  return { table, inserted, skipped, errors };\n}\n\n/**\n * Seed reference data (static lookup tables).\n *\n * Reference data is typically small, static datasets like\n * status codes, categories, or configuration options.\n *\n * @param db - Database instance\n * @param seeds - Array of seed data entries\n * @returns Array of seed results\n */\nexport function seedReferenceData(db: Database.Database, seeds: SeedData[]): SeedResult[] {\n  const results: SeedResult[] = [];\n\n  for (const seed of seeds) {\n    const result = seedData(db, seed.table, seed.data, {\n      skipDuplicates: seed.skipDuplicates ?? true,\n      uniqueKeys: seed.uniqueKeys,\n    });\n    results.push(result);\n  }\n\n  return results;\n}\n\n/**\n * Check if reference data needs seeding.\n *\n * @param db - Database instance\n * @param table - Table name\n * @param minCount - Minimum expected rows for a \"seeded\" table\n * @returns True if table needs seeding\n */\nexport function needsSeeding(db: Database.Database, table: string, minCount = 1): boolean {\n  try {\n    const validatedTable = validateTableName(table);\n    const result = db.prepare(`SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM ${validatedTable}`).get() as\n      | { count: number }\n      | undefined;\n    return !result || result.count < minCount;\n  } catch {\n    return true;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Upsert single row (insert or update if exists).\n *\n * @param db - Database instance\n * @param table - Table name\n * @param data - Data object to insert/update\n * @param uniqueColumns - Columns that determine uniqueness\n * @returns True if row was inserted or updated\n */\nexport function upsertRow(\n  db: Database.Database,\n  table: string,\n  data: Record<string, unknown>,\n  uniqueColumns: string[]\n): boolean {\n  // Validate table name\n  const validatedTable = validateTableName(table);\n\n  // Validate all column names\n  const columns = Object.keys(data);\n  const columnValidation = validateSqlIdentifiers(columns);\n  if (!columnValidation.valid) {\n    throw new Error(`Invalid column names: ${columnValidation.invalid.join(\", \")}`);\n  }\n\n  // Validate unique column names\n  const uniqueColumnValidation = validateSqlIdentifiers(uniqueColumns);\n  if (!uniqueColumnValidation.valid) {\n    throw new Error(`Invalid unique column names: ${uniqueColumnValidation.invalid.join(\", \")}`);\n  }\n\n  const values = columns.map((col) => data[col]);\n  const placeholders = columns.map(() => \"?\").join(\", \");\n  const columnList = buildColumnList(columns);\n\n  // Build WHERE clause for unique columns\n  const whereClause = uniqueColumns.map((col) => `${validateColumnName(col)} = ?`).join(\" AND \");\n  const whereValues = uniqueColumns.map((col) => data[col]);\n\n  // Check if row exists\n  const existsStmt = db.prepare(`SELECT 1 FROM ${validatedTable} WHERE ${whereClause}`);\n  const exists = existsStmt.get(...whereValues);\n\n  if (exists) {\n    // Update existing row\n    const updateClause = columns\n      .filter((col) => !uniqueColumns.includes(col))\n      .map((col) => `${validateColumnName(col)} = ?`)\n      .join(\", \");\n\n    if (updateClause) {\n      const updateValues = columns\n        .filter((col) => !uniqueColumns.includes(col))\n        .map((col) => data[col]);\n\n      const updateStmt = db.prepare(\n        `UPDATE ${validatedTable} SET ${updateClause} WHERE ${whereClause}`\n      );\n      updateStmt.run(...updateValues, ...whereValues);\n    }\n    return true;\n  } else {\n    // Insert new row\n    const insertStmt = db.prepare(\n      `INSERT INTO ${validatedTable} (${columnList}) VALUES (${placeholders})`\n    );\n    const result = insertStmt.run(...values);\n    return result.changes > 0;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Seed configuration data (key-value pairs).\n *\n * Creates a simple key-value configuration table if it doesn't exist\n * and seeds it with initial configuration.\n *\n * @param db - Database instance\n * @param tableName - Name of the configuration table\n * @param config - Configuration object to seed\n * @param options - Table creation options\n * @returns Seed result\n */\nexport function seedConfig(\n  db: Database.Database,\n  tableName: string,\n  config: Record<string, string | number | boolean>,\n  options: {\n    keyColumn?: string;\n    valueColumn?: string;\n    createIfNotExists?: boolean;\n  } = {}\n): SeedResult {\n  const { keyColumn = \"name\", valueColumn = \"value\", createIfNotExists = true } = options;\n\n  // Validate table and column names\n  const validatedTableName = validateTableName(tableName);\n  const validatedKeyColumn = validateColumnName(keyColumn);\n  const validatedValueColumn = validateColumnName(valueColumn);\n\n  // Create table if needed\n  if (createIfNotExists) {\n    db.exec(`\n      CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ${validatedTableName} (\n        ${validatedKeyColumn} TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n        ${validatedValueColumn} TEXT NOT NULL,\n        updated_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))\n      )\n    `);\n  }\n\n  // Convert config object to array format\n  const data = Object.entries(config).map(([key, value]) => ({\n    [validatedKeyColumn]: key,\n    [validatedValueColumn]: String(value),\n  }));\n\n  return seedData(db, validatedTableName, data, { skipDuplicates: true });\n}\n\n/**\n * Bulk seed from JSON file content.\n *\n * @param db - Database instance\n * @param table - Table name\n * @param jsonData - JSON string or parsed object\n * @returns Seed result\n */\nexport function seedFromJSON(\n  db: Database.Database,\n  table: string,\n  jsonData: string | Record<string, unknown> | Record<string, unknown>[]\n): SeedResult {\n  let data: Record<string, unknown>[];\n\n  if (typeof jsonData === \"string\") {\n    const parsed = JSON.parse(jsonData);\n    data = Array.isArray(parsed) ? parsed : [parsed];\n  } else if (Array.isArray(jsonData)) {\n    data = jsonData;\n  } else {\n    data = [jsonData];\n  }\n\n  return seedData(db, table, data, { skipDuplicates: true });\n}\n\n/**\n * Generate seed data for testing.\n *\n * Creates mock data based on a template factory function.\n *\n * @param db - Database instance\n * @param table - Table name\n * @param count - Number of rows to generate\n * @param factory - Function that generates data for a row\n * @returns Seed result\n */\nexport function generateSeedData<T extends Record<string, unknown>>(\n  db: Database.Database,\n  table: string,\n  count: number,\n  factory: (index: number) => T\n): SeedResult {\n  const data: T[] = [];\n\n  for (let i = 0; i < count; i++) {\n    data.push(factory(i));\n  }\n\n  return seedData(db, table, data);\n}\n\n/**\n * Transactional seeding with rollback on error.\n *\n * All seeding operations are performed in a single transaction.\n * If any operation fails, all changes are rolled back.\n *\n * @param db - Database instance\n * @param seeds - Array of seed data entries\n * @returns Array of seed results or throws on error\n */\nexport function seedTransaction(db: Database.Database, seeds: SeedData[]): SeedResult[] {\n  return db.transaction(() => {\n    return seedReferenceData(db, seeds);\n  })();\n}\n\n/**\n * Reset and reseed a table (for testing).\n *\n * Drops all data from a table and reseeds it with fresh data.\n *\n * @param db - Database instance\n * @param table - Table name\n * @param data - Data to seed\n * @param options - Options including disable foreign keys\n * @returns Seed result\n */\nexport function reseedTable<T extends Record<string, unknown>>(\n  db: Database.Database,\n  table: string,\n  data: T[],\n  options: { disableForeignKeys?: boolean; resetAutoincrement?: boolean } = {}\n): SeedResult {\n  const { disableForeignKeys = true, resetAutoincrement = true } = options;\n\n  // Validate table name\n  const validatedTable = validateTableName(table);\n\n  return db.transaction(() => {\n    // Disable foreign key constraints if requested\n    if (disableForeignKeys) {\n      db.pragma(\"foreign_keys = OFF\");\n    }\n\n    try {\n      // Clear existing data\n      db.prepare(`DELETE FROM ${validatedTable}`).run();\n\n      // Reset autoincrement if requested\n      if (resetAutoincrement) {\n        db.prepare(`DELETE FROM sqlite_sequence WHERE name=?`).run(validatedTable);\n      }\n\n      // Seed fresh data\n      return seedData(db, validatedTable, data);\n    } finally {\n      // Re-enable foreign keys\n      if (disableForeignKeys) {\n        db.pragma(\"foreign_keys = ON\");\n      }\n    }\n  })();\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":67,"oldLines":7,"newStart":67,"newLines":11,"lines":["   const validatedTable = validateTableName(table);"," ","   // Build column validateSqlIdentifiers,\n  validateTableName,\n} from \"./sql-validator.js\";","originalFile":"/**\n * Migration data seeding support.\n *\n * Provides utilities for seeding initial data during migrations,\n * including support for idempotent operations, reference data,\n * and test data generation.\n */\n\nimport type Database from \"better-sqlite3\";\nimport {\n  buildColumnList,\n  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars\n  buildPlaceholderList,\n  validateColumnName,\n  validateSqlIdentifiers,\n  validateTableName,\n} from \"./sql-validator.js\";\n\n/** SQLite parameter type */\ntype ParameterType = string | number | boolean | Buffer | null;\n\n/** Seed data entry */\nexport interface SeedData<T = Record<string, unknown>> {\n  table: string;\n  data: T[];\n  uniqueKeys?: string[]; // Columns that define uniqueness\n  skipDuplicates?: boolean; // Skip rows that would violate constraints\n}\n\n/** Seed operation result */\nexport interface SeedResult {\n  table: string;\n  inserted: number;\n  skipped: number;\n  errors: string[];\n}\n\n/**\n * Seed data into a table with conflict handling.\n *\n * @param db - Database instance\n * @param table - Table name\n * @param data - Array of data objects to insert\n * @param options - Seeding options\n * @returns Seed result with counts\n */\nexport function seedData<T extends Record<string, unknown>>(\n  db: Database.Database,\n  table: string,\n  data: T[],\n  options: {\n    skipDuplicates?: boolean;\n    uniqueKeys?: string[];\n    onConflict?: \"ignore\" | \"replace\" | \"update\";\n  } = {}\n): SeedResult {\n  const { skipDuplicates = true, onConflict = \"ignore\" } = options;\n  const errors: string[] = [];\n  let inserted = 0;\n  let skipped = 0;\n\n  if (data.length === 0) {\n    return { table, inserted: 0, skipped: 0, errors: [] };\n  }\n\n  // Validate table name to prevent SQL injection\n  const validatedTable = validateTableName(table);\n\n  // Build column names from first data object\n  const firstRow = data[0];\n  if (!firstRow) {\n    return { table, inserted: 0, skipped: 0, errors: [] };\n  }\n  const columns = Object.keys(firstRow);\n\n  // Validate all column names to prevent SQL injection\n  const columnValidation = validateSqlIdentifiers(columns);\n  if (!columnValidation.valid) {\n    return {\n      table,\n      inserted: 0,\n      skipped: 0,\n      errors: [`Invalid column names: ${columnValidation.invalid.join(\", \")}`],\n    };\n  }\n\n  const placeholders = columns.map(() => \"?\").join(\", \");\n  const columnList = buildColumnList(columns);\n\n  // Prepare appropriate statement based on conflict handling\n  let sql: string;\n  switch (onConflict) {\n    case \"replace\":\n      sql = `INSERT OR REPLACE INTO ${validatedTable} (${columnList}) VALUES (${placeholders})`;\n      break;\n    case \"update\":\n      // For UPDATE, we need to use INSERT OR REPLACE with a different approach\n      // SQLite doesn't have ON CONFLICT UPDATE in all versions\n      sql = `INSERT OR REPLACE INTO ${validatedTable} (${columnList}) VALUES (${placeholders})`;\n      break;\n    case \"ignore\":\n    default:\n      sql = `INSERT OR IGNORE INTO ${validatedTable} (${columnList}) VALUES (${placeholders})`;\n      break;\n  }\n\n  const stmt = db.prepare(sql);\n\n  for (const row of data) {\n    try {\n      const values = columns.map((col) => row[col as keyof T] as ParameterType);\n      const result = stmt.run(...values);\n\n      if (result.changes > 0) {\n        inserted++;\n      } else if (skipDuplicates) {\n        skipped++;\n      }\n    } catch (err) {\n      const error = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);\n      errors.push(`Row ${JSON.stringify(row)}: ${error}`);\n    }\n  }\n\n  return { table, inserted, skipped, errors };\n}\n\n/**\n * Seed reference data (static lookup tables).\n *\n * Reference data is typically small, static datasets like\n * status codes, categories, or configuration options.\n *\n * @param db - Database instance\n * @param seeds - Array of seed data entries\n * @returns Array of seed results\n */\nexport function seedReferenceData(db: Database.Database, seeds: SeedData[]): SeedResult[] {\n  const results: SeedResult[] = [];\n\n  for (const seed of seeds) {\n    const result = seedData(db, seed.table, seed.data, {\n      skipDuplicates: seed.skipDuplicates ?? true,\n      uniqueKeys: seed.uniqueKeys,\n    });\n    results.push(result);\n  }\n\n  return results;\n}\n\n/**\n * Check if reference data needs seeding.\n *\n * @param db - Database instance\n * @param table - Table name\n * @param minCount - Minimum expected rows for a \"seeded\" table\n * @returns True if table needs seeding\n */\nexport function needsSeeding(db: Database.Database, table: string, minCount = 1): boolean {\n  try {\n    const validatedTable = validateTableName(table);\n    const result = db.prepare(`SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM ${validatedTable}`).get() as\n      | { count: number }\n      | undefined;\n    return !result || result.count < minCount;\n  } catch {\n    return true;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Upsert single row (insert or update if exists).\n *\n * @param db - Database instance\n * @param table - Table name\n * @param data - Data object to insert/update\n * @param uniqueColumns - Columns that determine uniqueness\n * @returns True if row was inserted or updated\n */\nexport function upsertRow(\n  db: Database.Database,\n  table: string,\n  data: Record<string, unknown>,\n  uniqueColumns: string[]\n): boolean {\n  // Validate table name\n  const validatedTable = validateTableName(table);\n\n  // Validate all column names\n  const columns = Object.keys(data);\n  const columnValidation = validateSqlIdentifiers(columns);\n  if (!columnValidation.valid) {\n    throw new Error(`Invalid column names: ${columnValidation.invalid.join(\", \")}`);\n  }\n\n  // Validate unique column names\n  const uniqueColumnValidation = validateSqlIdentifiers(uniqueColumns);\n  if (!uniqueColumnValidation.valid) {\n    throw new Error(`Invalid unique column names: ${uniqueColumnValidation.invalid.join(\", \")}`);\n  }\n\n  const values = columns.map((col) => data[col]);\n  const placeholders = columns.map(() => \"?\").join(\", \");\n  const columnList = buildColumnList(columns);\n\n  // Build WHERE clause for unique columns\n  const whereClause = uniqueColumns.map((col) => `${validateColumnName(col)} = ?`).join(\" AND \");\n  const whereValues = uniqueColumns.map((col) => data[col]);\n\n  // Check if row exists\n  const existsStmt = db.prepare(`SELECT 1 FROM ${validatedTable} WHERE ${whereClause}`);\n  const exists = existsStmt.get(...whereValues);\n\n  if (exists) {\n    // Update existing row\n    const updateClause = columns\n      .filter((col) => !uniqueColumns.includes(col))\n      .map((col) => `${validateColumnName(col)} = ?`)\n      .join(\", \");\n\n    if (updateClause) {\n      const updateValues = columns\n        .filter((col) => !uniqueColumns.includes(col))\n        .map((col) => data[col]);\n\n      const updateStmt = db.prepare(\n        `UPDATE ${validatedTable} SET ${updateClause} WHERE ${whereClause}`\n      );\n      updateStmt.run(...updateValues, ...whereValues);\n    }\n    return true;\n  } else {\n    // Insert new row\n    const insertStmt = db.prepare(\n      `INSERT INTO ${validatedTable} (${columnList}) VALUES (${placeholders})`\n    );\n    const result = insertStmt.run(...values);\n    return result.changes > 0;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Seed configuration data (key-value pairs).\n *\n * Creates a simple key-value configuration table if it doesn't exist\n * and seeds it with initial configuration.\n *\n * @param db - Database instance\n * @param tableName - Name of the configuration table\n * @param config - Configuration object to seed\n * @param options - Table creation options\n * @returns Seed result\n */\nexport function seedConfig(\n  db: Database.Database,\n  tableName: string,\n  config: Record<string, string | number | boolean>,\n  options: {\n    keyColumn?: string;\n    valueColumn?: string;\n    createIfNotExists?: boolean;\n  } = {}\n): SeedResult {\n  const { keyColumn = \"name\", valueColumn = \"value\", createIfNotExists = true } = options;\n\n  // Validate table and column names\n  const validatedTableName = validateTableName(tableName);\n  const validatedKeyColumn = validateColumnName(keyColumn);\n  const validatedValueColumn = validateColumnName(valueColumn);\n\n  // Create table if needed\n  if (createIfNotExists) {\n    db.exec(`\n      CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ${validatedTableName} (\n        ${validatedKeyColumn} TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n        ${validatedValueColumn} TEXT NOT NULL,\n        updated_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))\n      )\n    `);\n  }\n\n  // Convert config object to array format\n  const data = Object.entries(config).map(([key, value]) => ({\n    [validatedKeyColumn]: key,\n    [validatedValueColumn]: String(value),\n  }));\n\n  return seedData(db, validatedTableName, data, { skipDuplicates: true });\n}\n\n/**\n * Bulk seed from JSON file content.\n *\n * @param db - Database instance\n * @param table - Table name\n * @param jsonData - JSON string or parsed object\n * @returns Seed result\n */\nexport function seedFromJSON(\n  db: Database.Database,\n  table: string,\n  jsonData: string | Record<string, unknown> | Record<string, unknown>[]\n): SeedResult {\n  let data: Record<string, unknown>[];\n\n  if (typeof jsonData === \"string\") {\n    const parsed = JSON.parse(jsonData);\n    data = Array.isArray(parsed) ? parsed : [parsed];\n  } else if (Array.isArray(jsonData)) {\n    data = jsonData;\n  } else {\n    data = [jsonData];\n  }\n\n  return seedData(db, table, data, { skipDuplicates: true });\n}\n\n/**\n * Generate seed data for testing.\n *\n * Creates mock data based on a template factory function.\n *\n * @param db - Database instance\n * @param table - Table name\n * @param count - Number of rows to generate\n * @param factory - Function that generates data for a row\n * @returns Seed result\n */\nexport function generateSeedData<T extends Record<string, unknown>>(\n  db: Database.Database,\n  table: string,\n  count: number,\n  factory: (index: number) => T\n): SeedResult {\n  const data: T[] = [];\n\n  for (let i = 0; i < count; i++) {\n    data.push(factory(i));\n  }\n\n  return seedData(db, table, data);\n}\n\n/**\n * Transactional seeding with rollback on error.\n *\n * All seeding operations are performed in a single transaction.\n * If any operation fails, all changes are rolled back.\n *\n * @param db - Database instance\n * @param seeds - Array of seed data entries\n * @returns Array of seed results or throws on error\n */\nexport function seedTransaction(db: Database.Database, seeds: SeedData[]): SeedResult[] {\n  return db.transaction(() => {\n    return seedReferenceData(db, seeds);\n  })();\n}\n\n/**\n * Reset and reseed a table (for testing).\n *\n * Drops all data from a table and reseeds it with fresh data.\n *\n * @param db - Database instance\n * @param table - Table name\n * @param data - Data to seed\n * @param options - Options including disable foreign keys\n * @returns Seed result\n */\nexport function reseedTable<T extends Record<string, unknown>>(\n  db: Database.Database,\n  table: string,\n  data: T[],\n  options: { disableForeignKeys?: boolean; resetAutoincrement?: boolean } = {}\n): SeedResult {\n  const { disableForeignKeys = true, resetAutoincrement = true } = options;\n\n  // Validate table name\n  const validatedTable = validateTableName(table);\n\n  return db.transaction(() => {\n    // Disable foreign key constraints if requested\n    if (disableForeignKeys) {\n      db.pragma(\"foreign_keys = OFF\");\n    }\n\n    try {\n      // Clear existing data\n      db.prepare(`DELETE FROM ${validatedTable}`).run();\n\n      // Reset autoincrement if requested\n      if (resetAutoincrement) {\n        db.prepare(`DELETE FROM sqlite_sequence WHERE name=?`).run(validatedTable);\n      }\n\n      // Seed fresh data\n      return seedData(db, validatedTable, data);\n    } finally {\n      // Re-enable foreign keys\n      if (disableForeignKeys) {\n        db.pragma(\"foreign_keys = ON\");\n      }\n    }\n  })();\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":9,"oldLines":8,"newStart":9,"newLines":6,"lines":[" import type Database from \"better-sqlite3\";"," import {","   buildColumnList,","-  //
